::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 3. Browser Extension Crash Course {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB39 .pagebreak aria-label="39" role="doc-pagebreak" title="39"}In this chapter, you'll learn how to build a basic browser extension from scratch and run it in your browser. The extension will *not* use any third-party libraries or dependencies, just vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Chapters later in this book will cover various aspects of browser extensions in more detail.

This chapter is geared toward developers that have never built an extension before; however, even if you are already somewhat comfortable with extension development, this chapter may yet prove useful as it touches upon some corners of the domain that you may not have experimented with before.

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Creating the Manifest {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par4 .Para}
As described in the previous chapter, the extension [manifest]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1} primarily defines the following:

::: OrderedList
1.  ::: ItemNumber
    1\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    What the extension is allowed to do
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

2.  ::: ItemNumber
    2\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Where its files are located
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB40 .pagebreak aria-label="40" role="doc-pagebreak" title="40"}
::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par7 .Para}
Begin by creating a new empty directory named [[extension-crash-course]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2}. All the files we create in this crash course will be created inside it:

:::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
extension-crash-course/
:::

::: FixedLine
└── manifest.json
:::
:::::
::::::
:::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par8 .Para}
Inside this [directory]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3}, let's create the simplest possible [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file. The file structure is shown here:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Extension Crash Course\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"1.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-1]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4}
:::
::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

These fields define the formal browser extension name string [Extension Crash Course]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This name will be reflected within the browser itself when the extension is installed, as well as when the extension is published on a public extension marketplace such as the Chrome Web Store. Also defined in this file is the semantic version [1.0]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, indicating the [version]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5} of the extension package. It also defines the manifest version string [3]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, indicating to the browser how the [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file should be parsed.
::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
## Minimum Viable Extension {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

Besides the manifest file, all elements of browser extensions are optional for use. Therefore, this lone [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file with its small amount of boilerplate is the minimum viable browser [extension]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6}. The extension has no features or user interfaces, and it incurs effectively zero browser overhead. It is essentially the browser extension equivalent of a [no-operation (NOOP)]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7} instruction. Nevertheless, browsers will happily install this single file as a new browser extension just as it would any other.
:::

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
## Installing Your Extension {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par11 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB41 .pagebreak aria-label="41" role="doc-pagebreak" title="41"}The fastest way to [install]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8} and test your extension is to load it into Google Chrome by enabling extension developer mode. This mode can be enabled on your browser's Chrome Extensions page. There are two ways to reach this page:

::: UnorderedList
-   ⋮ ➤ **Extensions** ➤ **Manage Extensions** (Figure [[3-1](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef})**.**

-   Navigate your browser to [chrome://extensions.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a blank page with the URL &quot;about:blank&quot; in the address bar. The browser&#39;s menu is open, showing options like &quot;New Tab,&quot; &quot;New Window,&quot; &quot;New Incognito Window,&quot; &quot;History,&quot; &quot;Downloads,&quot; &quot;Extensions,&quot; and &quot;Settings.&quot; The &quot;Extensions&quot; option is highlighted, with a submenu offering &quot;Manage Extensions&quot; and &quot;Visit Chrome Web Store.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-1</span>
<p>Opening the <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9">Chrome Extensions page</span> via the browser menu</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par14 .Para}
The default behavior of Google Chrome is to disallow loading [extensions]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10} from the local filesystem. To enable this behavior, on the [Chrome Extensions page]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11}, you will need to enable the *Developer mode* toggle, shown in Figures [[3-2](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig2)]{.InternalRef} and [[3-3](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig3)]{.InternalRef}.

<figure id="Fig2" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO2" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig2_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle4" alt="A screenshot of the Chrome browser&#39;s Extensions page. The page includes a search bar at the top, options for &quot;My extensions&quot; and &quot;Keyboard shortcuts&quot; on the left, and a link to discover more extensions in the Chrome Web Store. The page is mostly empty, with a prompt to find extensions and themes. The &quot;Developer mode&quot; toggle is visible on the right." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-2</span>
<p>The Chrome <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12">Extensions</span> page with Developer mode disabled</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig3" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO3" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig3_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle4" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome browser&#39;s Extensions page. The page includes options to &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update.&quot; A search bar is present at the top. The left sidebar shows &quot;My extensions&quot; and &quot;Keyboard shortcuts.&quot; A message suggests discovering more extensions and themes in the Chrome Web Store, with a link provided. The page is in a minimalistic design with a white background." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-3</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13">Chrome Extensions page</span> with Developer mode enabled</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par15 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB43 .pagebreak aria-label="43" role="doc-pagebreak" title="43"}With Developer mode [enabled]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14}, you can then load the extension by clicking "Load unpacked" and selecting the [extension-crash-course]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} directory that contains [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} (Figure [[3-4](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig4)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig4" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO4" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig4_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a file selection dialog box within a web browser extension interface. The dialog box displays a folder named &quot;extension-crash-course&quot; highlighted in blue, with details such as size, kind, and date added. The left sidebar shows various locations like Recents, Applications, and Downloads. The top of the dialog box includes navigation buttons and a search bar. The background shows a blurred view of the browser&#39;s extensions page with options like &quot;Load unpacked&quot; and &quot;Developer mode.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-4</span>
<p>Selecting the directory to load the unpacked extension</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par16 .Para}
After selecting the [directory]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}, your extension will be installed in the browser and the Chrome Extensions page will reflect this by adding a card for the new extension (Figure [[3-5](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig5)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB44 .pagebreak aria-label="44" role="doc-pagebreak" title="44"}

<figure id="Fig5" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO5" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig5_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome Extensions page showing an installed extension named &quot;Extension Crash Course 1.0.&quot; The extension card displays options for &quot;Details&quot; and &quot;Remove,&quot; with a toggle switch set to on. The page includes buttons for &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update,&quot; with a search bar at the top. The left sidebar has options for &quot;My extensions&quot; and &quot;Keyboard shortcuts.&quot; A notification at the bottom left indicates &quot;Extension loaded.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-5</span>
<p>Google <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16">Chrome</span> with the crash course extension newly installed</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For ease of access, you should pin the extension toolbar icon so that it always displays. Click on the puzzle piece extension icon, and then click the pin button.
::::
::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
## Reloading Your Extension {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

When making [changes]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17} to extension code, understanding when those changes should appear can be difficult. Different parts of extensions will be reloaded at different times. Furthermore, multiple tabs and windows mean that you may have multiple versions of an extension content script running at the same time!

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par19 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB45 .pagebreak aria-label="45" role="doc-pagebreak" title="45"}There are several different points at which portions of the extension are reloaded:

::: UnorderedList
-   An **extension reload** obtains a new copy of [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, updates the background service worker, and closes any stale extension UI pages that are open. This is required when the [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} changes. When sideloaded, an extension reload will always re-fire all install events.

-   An **extension page reload** is a page refresh of any page that uses the extension protocol [chrome-extension://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This is required to reflect changes in HTML, JS, CSS, and images in extension UI pages.

-   A **web page reload**[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18} is a page refresh of any web page that has content scripts injected. This is required to reflect changes in the injected content scripts.

-   A **devtools reload** is closing and opening the browser's developer tools interface. This is required to reflect changes in devtools pages.

-   An **extension reinstall** is like an extension reload, but it will also flush extension storage.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par25 .Para}
There are three [ways]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19} to force an extension reload:

::: UnorderedList
-   Uninstall and reinstall the extension.

-   Click the reload icon ↺ in the corresponding card on the Chrome Extensions page.

-   Programmatically reload the extension using [chrome.runtime.reload()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [chrome.management.setEnabled().]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB46 .pagebreak aria-label="46" role="doc-pagebreak" title="46"}Programmatically reloading the extension is covered in more detail in Chapter [[[13]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par30 .Para}
When sending [messages]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20} using the WebExtensions API, you will occasionally see an error similar to the following:

::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
Uncaught (in promise) Error: Could not establish connection. Receiving end does not exist.
:::
::::
:::::
::::::

Browsers understand that different versions of extension content scripts can be running at the same time, and so they explicitly disallow sending messages between the stale content script and the reloaded [extension]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21}. Seeing this error typically indicates that a content script is stale and requires a page reload.

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For the purposes of this [crash course]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22}, reloading the extension *and* the extension page will be sufficient to reflect any changes you have made.
::::
:::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
## Building the Extension Skeleton {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
### Adding a Background Script {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par33 .Para}
Next, let's add a simple background [script]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23} and verify that it is running. In the same directory as [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, create a [background.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [file]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24}:

::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
extension-crash-course/
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ manifest.json
:::

::: FixedLine
└─ **background.js**
:::
::::::
:::::::
::::::::

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par34 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB47 .pagebreak aria-label="47" role="doc-pagebreak" title="47"}The file content is shown below:

::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from the background script!\");
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-2a]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::
::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par35 .Para}
The [manifest]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25} doesn't know this file exists, so let's update it so it loads this script file as a background service worker:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"background\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"service_worker\": \"background.js\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-2b]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par36 .Para}
After reloading the extension, you will notice that the card on the Chrome Extensions page shows a link to the service [worker]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26} (Figure [[3-6](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig6)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig6" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO6" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig6_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Chrome browser&#39;s Extensions page. The page displays a section titled &quot;All Extensions&quot; with an extension named &quot;Extension Crash Course 1.0&quot; described as a &quot;Browser extension created from scratch.&quot; The extension card shows an ID and options to &quot;Details,&quot; &quot;Remove,&quot; and a toggle switch for enabling or disabling the extension. The page also includes options to &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update&quot; at the top. A search bar is present for finding extensions." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-6</span>
<p>Extension displaying a link to the service worker</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par37 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB48 .pagebreak aria-label="48" role="doc-pagebreak" title="48"}This link will open a developer [tools]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27} console for the [background service worker]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28}, where you will see the output of the [console.log]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} (Figure [[3-7](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig7)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig7" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO7" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig7_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser&#39;s developer tools console displaying a message: &quot;Hello from the background script!&quot; The console shows the file name &quot;background.js&quot; with a clickable link and indicates no issues. The interface includes tabs for Console, Sources, Network, and more, with a filter option and settings icon." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-7</span>
<p>Console for the <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29">background service worker</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

You'll note that the [card]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30} in the [Chrome Extensions page]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31} may show the service worker as *inactive* after a period of time. In the interest of freeing unused system resources, Google Chrome decided that the service worker was idle and automatically unloaded it. The worker is reloaded when needed again, such as when an extension message is sent.

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Unloading and reloading service workers is an important concept. It will be covered further in Chapter [[[6]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
### Adding a Popup Page {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par40 .Para}
Next, let's add the first user [interface]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32} to the extension. Create a new [popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} directory in the same directory as [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Inside the new directory, create three new files: [popup.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [popup.css]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [popup.js:]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB49 .pagebreak aria-label="49" role="doc-pagebreak" title="49"}

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
extension-crash-course/
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ manifest.json
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ background.js
:::

::: FixedLine
**└─ popup/**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **├─ popup.html**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **├─ popup.css**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **└─ popup.js**
:::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par41 .Para}
The file [contents]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33} are shown below:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<link href=\"popup.css\" rel=\"stylesheet\" /\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is the popup page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"popup.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-3a]{.CaptionNumber}

[popup/popup.html]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
body {
:::

::: FixedLine
  width: 400px;
:::

::: FixedLine
  margin: 2rem;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-3b]{.CaptionNumber}

[popup/popup.css]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35}
:::
::::
::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from the popup!\");
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-3c]{.CaptionNumber}

popup/popup.js
:::
::::
:::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB50 .pagebreak aria-label="50" role="doc-pagebreak" title="50"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par42 .Para}
The manifest [needs]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36} to be told when to render this HTML, so let's update it so it opens the popup page when the toolbar icon is clicked:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"background\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"service_worker\": \"background.js\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"action\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_popup\": \"popup/popup.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-3d]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par43 .Para}
After reloading the [extension]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38}, click the toolbar extension icon to open the popup page (Figure [[3-8](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig8)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig8" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO8" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig8_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a blank page with the URL &quot;about:blank&quot; in the address bar. A popup message in the top right corner reads, &quot;This is the popup page!&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-8</span>
<p><span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39">Opened popup page</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB51 .pagebreak aria-label="51" role="doc-pagebreak" title="51"}Because you have not defined an extension icon, Google Chrome will automatically generate one from the name of the extension. As shown here, the default is the first letter of the extension name, E, on a gray background.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
### Adding an Options Page {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par45 .Para}
Next, let's add an options [page]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40} with a similar structure to the popup page:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
extension-crash-course/
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ manifest.json
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ background.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ popup/
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ popup.html
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ popup.css
:::

::: FixedLine
│  └─ popup.js
:::

::: FixedLine
**└─ options/**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **├─ options.html**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **├─ options.css**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **└─ options.js**
:::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par46 .Para}
The file contents are shown below:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<link href=\"options.css\" rel=\"stylesheet\" /\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is the options page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"options.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-4a]{.CaptionNumber}

[options/options.html]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB52 .pagebreak aria-label="52" role="doc-pagebreak" title="52"}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC14 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
body {
:::

::: FixedLine
  margin: 2rem;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-4b]{.CaptionNumber}

options/options.css
:::
::::
:::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC15 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from options!\");
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-4c]{.CaptionNumber}

options/options.js
:::
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par47 .Para}
The manifest [needs]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42} to be configured to use this HTML as the options page:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC16 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"options_page\": \"options/options.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-4d]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par48 .Para}
Reload the [extension]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43}, then open the extension options page by right-clicking on the toolbar icon and selecting "Options" (Figure [[3-9](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig9)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB53 .pagebreak aria-label="53" role="doc-pagebreak" title="53"}

<figure id="Fig9" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO9" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig9_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a webpage with the title &quot;Extension Crash Course&quot; and URL &quot;chrome-extension:/bjmha.../options/options.html&quot;. The page contains the text &quot;This is the options page!&quot; in bold. The interface includes standard browser elements like the address bar and tabs." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-9</span>
<p>Extension options <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44">page</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
### Adding a Side Panel {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par49 .Para}
Next, let's add a side panel [page]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45}, again with a similar structure.

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC17 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
extension-crash-course/
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ manifest.json
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ background.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ popup/
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ popup.html
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ popup.css
:::

::: FixedLine
│  └─ popup.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ options/
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ options.html
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ options.css
:::

::: FixedLine
│  └─ options.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ content-script/
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ content-script.css
:::

::: FixedLine
│  └─ content-script.js
:::

::: FixedLine
**└─ side-panel/**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **├─ side-panel.html**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **├─ side-panel.css**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **└─ side-panel.js**
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par50 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB54 .pagebreak aria-label="54" role="doc-pagebreak" title="54"}The file [contents]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46} are shown below:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC18 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<link href=\"side-panel.css\" rel=\"stylesheet\" /\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is the side panel page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"side-panel.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-5a]{.CaptionNumber}

side-panel/side-panel.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC19 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
body {
:::

::: FixedLine
  margin: 2rem;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-5b]{.CaptionNumber}

side-panel/side-panel.css
:::
::::
:::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC20 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from side-panel!\");
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-5c]{.CaptionNumber}

side-panel/side-panel.js
:::
::::
:::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB55 .pagebreak aria-label="55" role="doc-pagebreak" title="55"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par51 .Para}
The manifest [needs]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47} to be configured to use this HTML as the side panel page. Additionally, the extension must also request the [sidePanel]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission in order for the browser to recognize the page as an eligible side panel.

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC21 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"permissions\": \[\"sidePanel\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\...**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"side_panel\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_path\": \"side-panel/side-panel.html\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-5d]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par52 .Para}
Reload the [extension]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49}, open a web page like [blank.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, then open the side panel page by right-clicking on the toolbar icon and selecting "Open Side Panel" (Figure [[3-10](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig10)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig10" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO10" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig10_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A web browser screenshot displaying a blank page with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. On the right side, a side panel titled &quot;Extension Crash Course&quot; contains the text &quot;This is the side panel page!&quot; in bold. The browser interface includes standard navigation buttons and tabs." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-10</span>
<p>Extension side panel <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50">page</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
### Adding a Content Script {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par53 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB56 .pagebreak aria-label="56" role="doc-pagebreak" title="56"}Next, let's create a content [script]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51} that renders a widget inside the web page:

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC22 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
extension-crash-course/
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ manifest.json
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ background.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ popup/
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ popup.html
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ popup.css
:::

::: FixedLine
│  └─ popup.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ options/
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ options.html
:::

::: FixedLine
│  ├─ options.css
:::

::: FixedLine
│  └─ options.js
:::

::: FixedLine
**└─ content-script/**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **├─ content-script.css**
:::

::: FixedLine
   **└─ content-script.js**
:::
:::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par54 .Para}
The content [scripts]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52} will add a styled custom container into the page:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC23 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
#container {
:::

::: FixedLine
  position: absolute;
:::

::: FixedLine
  background-color: gray;
:::

::: FixedLine
  color: white;
:::

::: FixedLine
  padding: 2rem;
:::

::: FixedLine
  top: 0;
:::

::: FixedLine
  left: 0;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-6a]{.CaptionNumber}

content-script/content-script.css
:::
::::
::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB57 .pagebreak aria-label="57" role="doc-pagebreak" title="57"}

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC24 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from content script!\");
:::
::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const tpl = \`
:::

::: FixedLine
\<div id=\"container\"\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<h1\>This is the content script!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</div\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\`;
:::
::::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
document.body.insertAdjacentHTML(\"beforeend\", tpl);
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-6b]{.CaptionNumber}

content-script/content-script.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par55 .Para}
Configure the [manifest]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53} to inject these content scripts into all valid web pages:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC25 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
\...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"content_scripts\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"css\": \[\"content-script/content-script.css\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"js\": \[\"content-script/content-script.js\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-6c]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For the purposes of extensions, \"content scripts\" cover anything that is injected into the page -- both JS and CSS.
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par57 .Para}
Open a real web [page]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55} such as [[[[https://blank.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://blank.org)]{.ExternalRef} to see the content script render (Figure [[3-11](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig11)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB58 .pagebreak aria-label="58" role="doc-pagebreak" title="58"}

<figure id="Fig11" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO11" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig11_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a webpage with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The page is mostly white with a gray box in the upper left corner containing the text &quot;This is the content script!&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-11</span>
<p>Content script <span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56">running</span> on web page</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
## Tying Everything Together {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par58 .Para}
This extension doesn't do anything yet, so let's enhance the popup page with buttons that can open other extension pieces on command. First, add buttons and handlers to the popup page:

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC26 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<link href=\"popup.css\" rel=\"stylesheet\" /\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is the popup page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\<button id=\"options\"\>Options\</button\>**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\<button id=\"content-script\"\>Content Script\</button\>**
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"popup.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7a]{.CaptionNumber}

[popup/popup.html]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB59 .pagebreak aria-label="59" role="doc-pagebreak" title="59"}

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC27 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from the popup!\");
:::
::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**function on(selector, event, handler) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **return document**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **.querySelector(selector)**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **?.addEventListener(event, handler);**
:::

::: FixedLine
**}**
:::
::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**on(\"#options\", \"click\", () =\> {});**
:::

::: FixedLine
**on(\"#content-script\", \"click\", () =\> {});**
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7b]{.CaptionNumber}

[popup/popup.js]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
### Programmatically Opening Options and Side Panel {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par59 .Para}
The [WebExtensions API]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59} allows you to perform special extension tasks that are not available to regular web page [JavaScript]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60}. You'll become more familiar with aspects of the API later in this book, but for the purposes of this [crash course]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61}, we can begin simply:

::: UnorderedList
-   The [WebExtensions API]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62} is exposed through a global object, accessible in all major browsers via both [chrome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [browser]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} for backward compatibility.

-   This global object is accessible only inside extension JavaScript.

-   []{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB60 .pagebreak aria-label="60" role="doc-pagebreak" title="60"}This global [chrome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} object features an assortment of namespaces to organize the different parts of the API. [chrome.storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} contains the API parts related to storage, [chrome.tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} contains the API parts related to the browser tabs, etc.
:::
::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par63 .Para}
Let's begin with a simple API method to programmatically open the options [page]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63}: [chrome.runtime.openOptionsPage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. As we have already registered [options.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} as the options page inside the manifest, this method conveniently opens a new tab pointing to that page. Add it inside the options button click handler:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC28 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**\...**
:::

::: FixedLine
**on(\"#options\", \"click\", () =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.runtime.openOptionsPage();**
:::

::: FixedLine
**});**
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7c]{.CaptionNumber}

popup/popup.js
:::
::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
### Adding a Welcome Message {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par64 .Para}
When a user first installs the extension, it's useful to be able to show them a [welcome message]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm64} or onboarding instructions. For the sake of simplicity, we'll just adjust the options page to show a welcome message if the [welcome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} query parameter is present.

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC29 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from options!\");
:::
::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const tpl = **\`\<h2\>Thanks for installing!\</h2\>\`;**
:::

::: FixedLine
**if (window.location.search.includes(\"welcome\")) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **document.body.insertAdjacentHTML(\"beforeend\", tpl);**
:::

::: FixedLine
**}**
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7d]{.CaptionNumber}

options/options.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB61 .pagebreak aria-label="61" role="doc-pagebreak" title="61"}
:::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par65 .Para}
We need to add a handler in the background that will trigger when the extension is initially installed. When this [happens]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm65}, open the options page with [?welcome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} appended to the URL. This uses a few new APIs:

::: UnorderedList
-   [chrome.runtime.onInstalled]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} fires at several lifecycle points of the extension.

-   [chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is an enum describing the possible install lifecycle points.

-   [chrome.runtime.getURL]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} converts a path string to the full extension URL.

-   [chrome.tabs.create]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [opens]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm66} the provided URL in a new tab[.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_FPar7 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

These are just quick and dirty explanations of these methods; there is much more nuance to all these methods covered later in the book.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par71 .Para}
These can all be [combined]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm67} in the background script:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC30 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from the background script!\");
:::
::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener((details) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **if (details.reason !==**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.INSTALL) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **return;**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::
::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **const optionsUrl = chrome.runtime.getURL(**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\'options/options.html?welcome\');**
:::
:::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **chrome.tabs.create({ url: optionsUrl });**
:::

::: FixedLine
**});**
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7e]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB62 .pagebreak aria-label="62" role="doc-pagebreak" title="62"}
::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par72 .Para}
You can test this by uninstalling/reinstalling your extension to [trigger]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm68} the welcome page (Figure [[3-12](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Fig12)]{.InternalRef}**).**

<figure id="Fig12" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_MO12" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_3_Chapter/534317_2_En_3_Fig12_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser window displaying an options page for a Chrome extension. The page contains the text &quot;This is the options page! Thanks for installing!&quot; The URL in the address bar is &quot;chrome-extension:/jekblijifjmmcbcdogfdgpgbnpm/options/options.html?welcome&quot;. The browser tabs show &quot;Extensions&quot; and &quot;Extension Crash Course&quot;." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 3-12</span>
<p><span id="534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69">Welcome message</span> displayed on options page after install</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
### Triggering a Content Script Render {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

As it currently exists, the content script widget is automatically rendering on every web page. Instead, let's change it so it only renders when we click the [content script]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70} button in the popup.

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par74 .Para}
To begin, we'll need to add the [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission added in the manifest.[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB63 .pagebreak aria-label="63" role="doc-pagebreak" title="63"}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC31 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"permissions\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"sidePanel\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"activeTab\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\...**
:::

::: FixedLine
**}**
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7f]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par75 .Para}
The [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm71}[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72} is covered more later in the book, but it's a convenient shortcut to get full permissions for the active browser tab only after a user gesture, such as clicking a button. Alter the popup to send a message to the content script on the active tab:

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC32 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\...
:::

::: FixedLine
**on(\"#content-script\", \"click\", async () =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **const tabs = await chrome.tabs.query({**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **active: true, currentWindow: true**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::
::::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **if (tabs.length === 0) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **return;**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::
::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **const activeTab = tabs\[0\];**
:::
::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **chrome.tabs.sendMessage(activeTab.id, {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **action: \"renderWidget\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
**});**
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7g]{.CaptionNumber}

popup/popup.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB64 .pagebreak aria-label="64" role="doc-pagebreak" title="64"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Par76 .Para}
Finally, modify the [content script]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73} so it waits for this message before rendering:

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PC33 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Hello from content script!\");
:::
::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const tpl = \`
:::

::: FixedLine
\<div id=\"container\"\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<h1\>This is the content script!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</div\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\`;
:::
::::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener(**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **(message, sender, sendResponse) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **if (message.action !== \"renderWidget\") {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **return;**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::
::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    **document.body.insertAdjacentHTML(\"beforeend\", tpl);**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 3-7h]{.CaptionNumber}

content-script/content-script.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

Now, visit [blank.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74}, open the popup, and click the content script button to render the content script widget.
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you were guided through the process of creating a simple Chrome extension. The purpose of this [crash course]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75} was to provide step-by-step instructions on how to create an extension that touched all the different user interface elements that browser extensions can use. After completing the crash course, you should have a good understanding of how various user interfaces are produced from their source code, as well as how a manifest should be laid out to achieve these ends.

[]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_PB65 .pagebreak aria-label="65" role="doc-pagebreak" title="65"}You should also have a very basic understanding of how the [WebExtensions API]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76} can be used. This [crash course]{#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77} only touched upon a handful of basic methods, but it gives you an initial concept for all the various ways that browser extensions can manipulate the browser to achieve their ends.

The next chapter will explore the architecture of browser extensions, including how files are organized and how the various elements behave inside the browser.
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml}

::::: ChapterContextInformation
:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_b979-8-8688-1594-2_4 .ContextInformation}
