:::

[M. Frisbie]{.ContextInformationAuthorEditorNames}[[Building Browser Extensions]{.BookTitle}]{.ContextInformationBookTitles}[<https://doi.org/10.1007/979-8-8688-1594-2_4>]{.ChapterDOI}
::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 4. Browser Extension Architecture {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par2 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB67 .pagebreak aria-label="67" role="doc-pagebreak" title="67"}Understanding how the various elements of browser extensions work is important, but even more important is understanding how they work together. The architecture of browser extensions is unusual and multifaceted:

::: UnorderedList
-   Extensions are formed from a [network of distributed elements]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1}: background scripts, content scripts, popup and options pages, and devtools pages.

-   Extensions can manage the complex browser/page infrastructure that spans across [multiple windows and tabs]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2}. Because content scripts can run on multiple pages and are independently loaded, they require special considerations such as a multiplexed communication channel and update version control.

-   Extensions offer a [web page--based user interface]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3} in the popup page, options page, side panel page, devtools pages, and content scripts. They can also directly integrate with the native browser chrome with features such as keyboard shortcuts, URL bars, and desktop notifications.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB68 .pagebreak aria-label="68" role="doc-pagebreak" title="68"}This chapter is a high-level overview covering how the pieces connect and interact. The APIs and other specific details are primarily covered later in this book.
::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Architecture Overview {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par7 .Para}
The browser extension architecture can be more easily understood visually. Consider the [diagram]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4} shown in Figure [[4-1](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef} displaying how different pieces of browser extensions fit together.

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle5" alt="Diagram illustrating the architecture of a web extension using a flow chart. The flow begins with a webpage interacting with the DOM, leading to content scripts. These scripts connect to a popup page and the Web Extensions API. The API links to an options page, a devtools page with multiple panels, and a background service worker with event listeners. Additional components include omnibox, keyboard shortcuts, desktop notifications, and a toolbar button, all interacting with the background service worker. Arrows indicate the flow of data and interactions between components." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-1</span>
<p>Browser extension architecture <span id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5">diagram</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par8 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB69 .pagebreak aria-label="69" role="doc-pagebreak" title="69"}Let's unpack the various elements and connections displayed here, identified in the above [diagram]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6} by number:

::: OrderedList
1.  ::: ItemNumber
    1\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Popup pages]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7} can use the WebExtensions API to perform tasks like reading/writing data to the storage API, sending messages to the background page, or triggering behavior in the active tab's content script.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

2.  ::: ItemNumber
    2\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Content scripts]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9} can view and manipulate the host web page DOM as well as listen for DOM events.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

3.  ::: ItemNumber
    3\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Content scripts are injected directly into web pages. Each tab with a permitted scheme ([http://, https://, file:///]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}) is eligible to have content scripts injected.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

4.  ::: ItemNumber
    4\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Content scripts can use the WebExtensions API to perform tasks like sending and receiving instructions from the background script, as well as exporting information from the page DOM. Content scripts on a single tab or subset of tabs can be targeted via [chrome.tabs.query().]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

5.  ::: ItemNumber
    5\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    In a similar fashion to popup pages, options pages interact with the rest of the extension via the WebExtensions API.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

6.  ::: ItemNumber
    6\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The [WebExtensions API]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11} is the connective tissue for any web extension. It allows for bidirectional messaging between any two extension components, as well as access to a shared storage API. The extensions messaging protocol is a broadcast format, meaning that extension messages will be delivered to any part of the extension that is listening for them.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

7.  ::: ItemNumber
    7\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    []{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB70 .pagebreak aria-label="70" role="doc-pagebreak" title="70"}[Devtools pages]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12} are only able to use a limited subset of the WebExtensions API.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

8.  ::: ItemNumber
    8\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Background service workers]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13} manage the rest of the extension via the WebExtensions API. This includes dispatching messages to another part of the extension when an API event handler is triggered.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

9.  ::: ItemNumber
    9\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The devtools [page]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14} is initialized each time the developer tools interface is opened, and disposed of when it is closed. It is primarily used to initialize child elements such as panels. Devtools pages are headless web pages.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

10. ::: ItemNumber
    10\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The [background service worker]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15} is the nerve center of the extension. It is frequently used to handle events, dispatch messages, perform authentication duties, and store secrets that the extension wishes to keep private. The background service worker has the useful property of being a singleton; for any number of tabs or windows, only one service worker will ever be running.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

11. ::: ItemNumber
    11\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Using the Devtools API, a devtools [page]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16} can generate multiple child pages that render natively inside the browser's devtools interface. These child pages take the form of panels and sidebars rendered inside the devtools interface itself.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

12. ::: ItemNumber
    12\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The background service worker is the only element of a browser extension that is guaranteed to reliably handle browser events. Components of extensions like options pages, popup pages, side panels, content scripts, and devtools pages are transient and therefore may miss events fired when they are not running.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

13. ::: ItemNumber
    13\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    []{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB71 .pagebreak aria-label="71" role="doc-pagebreak" title="71"}[Devtools pages]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17} are granted access to a supplementary Devtools API that enables methods for creating child views and inspecting and debugging web pages.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

14. ::: ItemNumber
    14\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The [Omnibox]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19} can be enabled and configured in the manifest. When a special keyword is typed into the URL bar, a special search-like interface is displayed. This interface will dispatch omnibox events with the search bar's contents to the extension, thereby allowing the extension to offer search engine[--]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}style behavior.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

15. ::: ItemNumber
    15\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Keyboard shortcuts]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20} can be enabled either to trigger native behavior like opening the popup page or to trigger a custom command event.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

16. ::: ItemNumber
    16\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Native desktop notifications]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21} can be displayed programmatically. The notifications will also trigger click and close events.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

17. ::: ItemNumber
    17\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The native toolbar [icon]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22} can behave in one of two ways: it can trigger the popup page, or it can fire an extension click event.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::
:::
::::
::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
## Plurality, Lifecycles, and Updates {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

Some critical considerations when developing browser extensions are how many of each element can exist at any given time, the fashion in which these elements are created and destroyed, and how they handle updates to the extension.

::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
### Background Service Worker {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB72 .pagebreak aria-label="72" role="doc-pagebreak" title="72"}The [background service worker]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24} is the only extension element that guarantees singleton behavior irrespective of the number of extension pages, windows, or tabs open. The service worker is torn down when the browser detects the service worker is idle and restarted on demand (e.g., when the browser detects an incoming event with a handler). The browser will restart the service worker when the extension is updated.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For more details on how background service workers behave, refer to Chapter [[[6]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
### Extension UIs {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

[Extension UIs]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26} are rendered as web pages but are displayed inside special extension-only containers. Unlike browser tabs, which can be created in any quantity and persist indefinitely, extension UI containers have rules about how, when, and in what manner they are displayed. The most common examples are popup pages, options pages, and side panel pages.

::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
#### Form Factor {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par30 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   **Popup pages**, as their name suggests, "pop down" [from]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27} the browser's chrome and therefore only once can ever be opened per window. [Popup]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29} containers should be smaller than a regular web page and, as a result, more closely resemble a mobile app than a full web page.

-   **Options pages** can display as a stand-alone tab or a modal window inside the browser's extension management interface. Any number of options tabs []{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB73 .pagebreak aria-label="73" role="doc-pagebreak" title="73"}can be opened, but only one options modal can ever be opened per window. Options [pages]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30} are [typically]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32} styled as conventional web pages.

-   **Side panel pages** are displayed as a vertical strip alongside the currently visible browser [tab]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33}, and only one can ever be displayed per window. The browser will shrink the visible web page's horizontal real estate to make room for the vertical side panel bar. Like popups, side panels do not have a conventional web page aspect ratio, and therefore, their user [interfaces]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34} are [similarly]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36} condensed.
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
#### Lifecycle {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par34 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   [**Popup pages**]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37} can be configured to open when clicking the extension toolbar icon, or opened [programmatically]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39} via [chrome.action.openPopup()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. The browser will render a fresh popup page each time the popup is opened, and opening a different extension or focusing away from the popup will dispose of the popup.

-   [**Options pages**]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40} can be opened by selecting the "Extension Options" menu item in the browser's extension management interface, or opened programmatically via [chrome.runtime.openOptionsPage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Options tabs will be freshly rendered each time it is opened, but the browser will reuse an existing options modal if already opened. Options pages are only disposed of when explicitly discarded by the user (via closing the tab or modal).

-   []{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB74 .pagebreak aria-label="74" role="doc-pagebreak" title="74"}**Side panel pages** can be opened by clicking the extension toolbar icon, selecting the "Open side panel" menu item in the [browser's]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42} extension management interface, or opened programmatically via [[chrome.sidePanel.open()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43}. A side panel will be freshly rendered each time it is opened. Side panels are only disposed of when explicitly discarded by the user (via closing the side panel or window), when the user navigates away from a matching page hostname (when applicable), or when closed programmatically.
:::
::::

To prevent stale pages from being displayed, the browser will forcibly close all open extension UI [pages]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45} (popups, options, side panels) when the extension is updated.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For more details on how extension UI pages behave, refer to Chapter [[[7]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
### Devtools Pages {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

The devtools [page]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47} is rendered exactly once each time the browser's devtools interface is opened. Therefore, because there can be only one developer tools interface per window, there can be exactly one devtools page per window. This page and the child pages it creates, consisting of panels and sidebars, will persist as long as the developer tools interface is opened. Importantly, these developer tools pages will not be affected when the extension is updated, and so they are susceptible to becoming stale.
:::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
### Content Scripts {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB75 .pagebreak aria-label="75" role="doc-pagebreak" title="75"}[Content scripts]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49} are blocks of JavaScript and CSS that an extension injects into existing web pages. Their purpose is to enhance or modify the behavior of the web page or to introduce supplemental user interfaces.

Injecting content scripts into a web page is analogous to cars merging into traffic. When done with care, the new [cars]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51} (content scripts) seamlessly integrate into the flow of traffic (the page's scripts and styling). However, a lack of care can cause collisions, potentially disrupting the entire traffic flow (the web page) and resulting in partial or complete functionality failure.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par43 .Para}
The process of injecting content scripts is governed by the extension's manifest file, which defines several key aspects of how and when the scripts are introduced:

::: UnorderedList
-   **Injection method:** [Specifies]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53} how the script is added to the page, such as through the scripting API or declarative methods

-   **JavaScript \"world\":** Determines whether the script runs in an isolated world (separate from the page's JavaScript context) or the main world (where it can directly interact with the page's scripts)

-   **Timing:** Defines the point in the page load lifecycle when the script is executed, such as before the DOM is ready or when the page is idle
:::
::::

Unless [otherwise]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55} specified in the manifest, content scripts are injected into an isolated world by default. In this mode, they run once the DOM and other resources have finished loading. Content scripts are not [terminated]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57} or restarted when an extension updates, so they have the potential to become stale.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB76 .pagebreak aria-label="76" role="doc-pagebreak" title="76"}For more details on how content scripts behave, refer to Chapter [[[8]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
### Browser Extension File Server {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

When an extension is installed, the browser makes all the extension's files accessible through a built-in file server. The extension's files are organized in a single directory, with the [manifest.json file]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58} located at the root. This directory may also contain HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and other assets, either in the root directory itself or within subdirectories.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par50 .Para}
To better understand how the file server works and explore key concepts related to extension file structures, let's create a simple browser extension. The [file structure]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59} is as follows:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
mvx/
:::

::: FixedLine
├── manifest.json
:::

::: FixedLine
├── background.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├── content-script.js
:::

::: FixedLine
├── fetch-page.js
:::

::: FixedLine
└── extra.html
:::
:::::::::
::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"web_accessible_resources\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"resources\": \[\"fetch-page.js\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 4-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB77 .pagebreak aria-label="77" role="doc-pagebreak" title="77"}

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
import \"./fetch-page.js\";
:::

::: FixedLine
console.log(\"background.js\");
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 4-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61}
:::
::::
::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"content-script.js\");
:::
::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
import(chrome.runtime.getURL(\"fetch-page.js\"));
:::
::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const el = document.createElement(\"script\");
:::

::: FixedLine
el.src = chrome.runtime.getURL(\"fetch-page.js\");
:::

::: FixedLine
document.body.appendChild(el);
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 4-1c]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"fetch-page.js\");
:::

::: FixedLine
fetch(chrome.runtime.getURL(\"extra.html\"));
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 4-1d]{.CaptionNumber}

[fetch-page.js]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63}
:::
::::
::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB78 .pagebreak aria-label="78" role="doc-pagebreak" title="78"}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>Extra Page\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"fetch-page.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 4-1e]{.CaptionNumber}

[extra.html]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm64}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par51 .Para}
Take a minute to examine what this extension is doing. Some [things]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm65} to note:

::: UnorderedList
-   The extension will inject [content-script.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} on all web pages.

-   The manifest designates [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} as a web accessible resource, and it is accessible by all web pages.

-   [content-script.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} attempts to load [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in two different ways: via dynamic [import]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and via dynamic [\<script\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} loading.

-   [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} sends a network request for the [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file. This file will be loaded and executed in multiple locations in the extension, with different results.
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par56 .Para}
To understand how the extension file server works, we'll dissect this extension one file at a time. Begin by loading the extension in Google Chrome and inspecting the background service worker console output by clicking the *service worker* [link]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm66} in the extension card (Figure [[4-2](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig2)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB79 .pagebreak aria-label="79" role="doc-pagebreak" title="79"}

<figure id="Fig2" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO2" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig2_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome Extensions page showing an installed extension named &quot;MVX 0.1.&quot; The extension card displays its ID as &quot;hbkfhklpkpmfiodockaaneohhphphne&quot; and includes options to &quot;Details&quot; and &quot;Remove.&quot; A toggle switch is present, indicating the extension is enabled. The page also features options to &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update,&quot; with a search bar for finding extensions. Developer mode is off." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-2</span>
<p>Clicking the service worker link will open the background console output</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par57 .Para}
The [background service worker]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm67} console output will appear as in Figure [[4-3](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig3)]{.InternalRef}.[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB80 .pagebreak aria-label="80" role="doc-pagebreak" title="80"}

<figure id="Fig3" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO3" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig3_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A screenshot of a browser&#39;s developer tools console window. It displays two JavaScript files: &quot;fetch-page.js&quot; and &quot;background.js&quot; with their respective line numbers, 1 and 2. The interface includes tabs for &quot;Elements,&quot; &quot;Console,&quot; &quot;Sources,&quot; &quot;Network,&quot; &quot;Performance,&quot; &quot;Memory,&quot; and &quot;Application.&quot; The console is set to &quot;Default levels&quot; with &quot;No Issues&quot; indicated." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-3</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm68">background console output</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par58 .Para}
This indicates that the [background script]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69} was able to successfully import and execute the [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} script. Let's look to the *Network* tab to see how this occurred (Figure [[4-4](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig4)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB81 .pagebreak aria-label="81" role="doc-pagebreak" title="81"}

<figure id="Fig4" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO4" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig4_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A screenshot of a browser&#39;s developer tools in the Network tab. It shows two requests: &quot;fetch-page.js&quot; and &quot;extra.html,&quot; both with a status of 200. The &quot;fetch-page.js&quot; is a script initiated by &quot;background.js:1,&quot; and &quot;extra.html&quot; is a fetch initiated by &quot;fetch-page.js:2.&quot; Both have a size of 0 B and a time of 1 ms. The interface includes options for filtering and viewing different resource types like Fetch/XHR, Doc, CSS, JS, and more." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-4</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70">background network activity log</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par59 .Para}
Reload the extension to trigger these network requests. You will see two successful [network requests]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm71}: one for the imported JavaScript file and one for the fetched HTML file. Inspecting the JavaScript request reveals what is shown in Figure [[4-5](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig5)]{.InternalRef}.[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB82 .pagebreak aria-label="82" role="doc-pagebreak" title="82"}

<figure id="Fig5" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO5" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig5_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser&#39;s developer tools in the Network tab, displaying a request for &quot;fetch-page.js&quot;. The request method is &quot;GET&quot; with a status code of &quot;200 OK&quot;. The request URL is a Chrome extension link. The &quot;Headers&quot; section shows details like &quot;Referrer Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin&quot; and response headers including &quot;Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *&quot; and &quot;Cache-Control: no-cache&quot;. The interface includes tabs for &quot;Headers&quot;, &quot;Preview&quot;, &quot;Response&quot;, &quot;Initiator&quot;, and &quot;Timing&quot;." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-5</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72">fetch-page.js request</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par60 .Para}
This network request successfully sends a GET request to the extension file server, and the file server returns a 200. Note the URL beginning with the [chrome-extension://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [protocol]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73}. Let's unpack the parts of this URL:

::: UnorderedList
-   **chrome-extension://** is the URL [protocol]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75}, which indicates to Chromium browsers that the request should be routed to the browser's installed extensions. The protocol differs between browsers: Mozilla Firefox uses [moz-extension://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, Microsoft Edge uses [extension://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and Opera uses [opera://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

-   []{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB83 .pagebreak aria-label="83" role="doc-pagebreak" title="83"}**hbkhfkilpnbmfioodckaaneohmhphhne**[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76} is the extension ID. Your extension's ID will be different. This ID is used to uniquely identify this instance of the extension both inside the browser as well as when published in the extension marketplace. The ID format will vary slightly between browsers: for example, Chromium browsers use an unbroken string of 32 lowercase letters, whereas Mozilla Firefox uses a v4 UUID.

-   [**fetch-page.js**]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm78} is the URL path. This will exactly match the file path inside the extension directory.
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par64 .Para}
Next, examine the [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} request (Figure [[4-6](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig6)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig6" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO6" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig6_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser&#39;s developer tools in the Network tab, displaying a request for &quot;extra.html&quot;. The request method is &quot;GET&quot; with a status code of 200, indicating success. The request URL is a Chrome extension path. The &quot;Headers&quot; section shows details like &quot;Cache-Control: no-cache&quot; and &quot;Content-Type: text/html&quot;. The interface includes options for filtering requests and viewing different types of data such as &quot;Doc&quot;, &quot;CSS&quot;, &quot;JS&quot;, and more." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-6</span>
<p>The <span class="EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional">extra.html</span> <span id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm79">request</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB84 .pagebreak aria-label="84" role="doc-pagebreak" title="84"}Compare the two requests and note the presence or absence of the [[Access-Control-Allow-Origin]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm80} and [Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [headers]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm81}. These are automatically added because [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is listed under [web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Compare this to the request for [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}: you will notice these headers are absent, as [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm82} is not listed as a web accessible resource.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

You also will notice that [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is not referenced directly or indirectly anywhere in the manifest, and yet the extension file server is perfectly happy to return the file. When sending requests from an extension context (background, popup, extension protocol), requests for *any* file in the extension will return that file successfully.
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par67 .Para}
Next, right-click on the request to copy the URL of the [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [request]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm83} (remember, your extension ID will differ from the screenshot above) and open it in a new browser tab. Once [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} renders, open the developer tools to open the developer tools to reveal the network requests as displayed in Figure [[4-7](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig7)]{.InternalRef}.[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB85 .pagebreak aria-label="85" role="doc-pagebreak" title="85"}

<figure id="Fig7" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO7" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig7_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser window displaying a network activity panel. The URL bar shows a Chrome extension path. The left side of the window has a blank page with the text &quot;Extra Page.&quot; The right side shows the Network tab with three requests listed: &quot;extra.html&quot; (document), &quot;fetch-page.js&quot; (script), and &quot;extra.html&quot; (fetch). Each request has a status of 200, indicating successful loading. The panel includes columns for Name, Status, Type, Initiator, Size, and Time, with a timeline graph above." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-7</span>
<p>The <span class="EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional">extra.html</span><span id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm84"></span> page with network requests displayed</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par68 .Para}
The browser loads [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm85} from the extension file server, renders the HTML, loads the [\<script\>,]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and executes the JavaScript code (which performs a duplicate fetch of [extra.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}). There are some critical takeaways to note here:

::: UnorderedList
-   Browser [extensions]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm86} can load and render any file included in the extension package, allowing them to host an unlimited number of web pages, even those not explicitly referenced in the manifest.

-   The special [chrome-extension://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [protocol]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm87} routes requests to the extension file server. Pages served with this protocol are treated as extension UI pages and therefore can use the WebExtensions API in their scripts or load any file in the extension directory.
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par71 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB86 .pagebreak aria-label="86" role="doc-pagebreak" title="86"}This behavior is drastically different when compared to [content script execution]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm88}. Note that the manifest is configured to inject the content script on any web page, so next direct your browser to an inert test site such as [blank.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and view the console output (Figure [[4-8](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Fig8)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig8" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_MO8" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_4_Chapter/534317_2_En_4_Fig8_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser screenshot showing a console window with several error messages. The page is titled &quot;blank&quot; with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot;. The console displays errors related to a Chrome extension, including &quot;Denying load of chrome-extension:...&quot;, &quot;Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined&quot;, and &quot;GET chrome-extension:/invalid/ net::ERR_FAILED&quot;. The errors are associated with JavaScript files like &quot;fetch-page.js&quot;." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 4-8</span>
<p>The console output from the extension’s content <span id="534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm89">scripts</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

First off, note that the content [script]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm90} is *not* being served via network request. Rather than being loaded from the extension file server, the browser is injecting it into the page directly.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par73 .Para}
Observe that the content script is attempting to fetch the JS file in two different ways, and each throws an error for a different reason:

::: UnorderedList
-   The first attempt dynamically imports the [[fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm91}[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm92} file and executes it. The dynamic import succeeds because [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is a web accessible resource. However, because this is executing inside a content script, it cannot load the HTML file since it is *not* made accessible under [web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This throws a [Denying load]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} error.

-   []{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB87 .pagebreak aria-label="87" role="doc-pagebreak" title="87"}The second attempt creates a [\<script\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [element]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm93} to load the [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file and execute it. The script load succeeds because [fetch-page.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is a web accessible resource. However, the execution context of the loaded script is not granted access to the WebExtensions API, and therefore, the attempt to use [chrome.runtime.getURL]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} throws a [TypeError]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::

Although [content scripts]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm94} still can access the extension file server, its access is considerably more restricted.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

The extension file server is a flexible way to make your extension behave more like a web page with multiple views and routing, but the request routing does not support basic features like redirects or custom 404 pages.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
## Sandboxed Pages {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par78 .Para}
In Manifest V3, the values allowed in the [Content Security Policy (CSP)]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm95} are considerably more limited when compared to v2. It newly disallows the following in extension pages:

::: UnorderedList
-   Inline scripts

-   Remotely loaded code

-   [eval()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   User-provided scripts
:::
::::

In order to use these tools, you must run an extension page in a [sandbox]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm96}. Doing so will allow the page to use the disallowed features listed above, but at a cost -- the page will lose all access to the WebExtensions APIs.

:::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par84 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB88 .pagebreak aria-label="88" role="doc-pagebreak" title="88"}The following example defines the popup page as a sandboxed [page]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm97}. When opening the popup page, the page demonstrates the sandbox differences:

::: UnorderedList
-   Inline [\<script\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is allowed to run.

-   [chrome.runtime]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is not defined.

-   [eval()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is allowed.
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Par88 .Para}
::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"default_popup\": \"popup.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"sandbox\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"pages\": \[\"popup.html\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 4-2a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<!\-- Without sandboxing, inline scripts are disallowed\--\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
      // Without sandboxing, hasAPIAccess is true
:::

::: FixedLine
      document.body.innerHTML += JSON.stringify({
:::

::: FixedLine
        hasAPIAccess: typeof chrome?.runtime !== \"undefined\",
:::

::: FixedLine
      });
:::
::::::::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
      // Without sandboxing, eval() is disallowed
:::

::: FixedLine
      eval();
:::

::: FixedLine
    \</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 4-2b]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.html
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_PB89 .pagebreak aria-label="89" role="doc-pagebreak" title="89"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you were presented with an overview of how all the elements of a browser extension work in concert. With this knowledge, you should now be able to analyze how a given extension moves information around and makes API calls. This chapter also equips you to more effectively be able to plan for how to take an idea for a browser extension and turn it into actual code. You should also have a good understanding of how the browser extension file server can deliver files in various ways, as well as the tradeoffs involved when declaring a sandboxed page.

The next chapter will cover all the fields that can appear in an extension manifest as well as how each field's definition can control the behavior of an extension.
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml}

::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 5. The Extension Manifest {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB91 .pagebreak aria-label="91" role="doc-pagebreak" title="91"}The [extension manifest]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1} is the blueprint of the browser extension. Broadly speaking, it is a config file containing a collection of key-value pairs that dictate what the extension can do and in what fashion.

The content of a manifest differs between manifest versions and browsers. Not all properties can be used by all browsers; some browsers will partially support a property, or even not support it at all.

[MDN]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2} offers a very good table of manifest property support here: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/manifest.json#browser_compatibility]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/manifest.json#browser_compatibility)]{.ExternalRef}.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This chapter omits manifest properties that are not widely supported by most web browsers (such as [theme]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [theme_experiment]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}), or are completely undocumented (such as [natively_connectable]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}). Furthermore, properties that are only supported in MV2 or have conflicting MV2 definitions will be marked as such.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## The Manifest File {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par6 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB92 .pagebreak aria-label="92" role="doc-pagebreak" title="92"}The [extension manifest]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3} is defined in a single [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file that lives in the root directory of the extension. This is the only file that is required for a browser extension to be considered valid:

:::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
simple-extension-directory/
:::

::: FixedLine
└─ **manifest.json**
:::
:::::
::::::
:::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

This file is a JSONC (JSON with Comments) file, which follows standard JSON syntax but allows both [//]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}-prefixed comments and [/\* \*/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} block comments. If your text editor uses a standard JSON linter, it may flag these comments as invalid. You can safely ignore these errors, as all browsers and extension stores support comments in the manifest.
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par8 .Para}
The [JSON file]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4} contains a single object with a large number of required and optional properties that configure how the extension behaves. There are only three required properties:

::: UnorderedList
-   [[manifest_version]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6}, which indicates to the browser how the manifest should be interpreted. This has a large number of implications on the behavior of the extension.

-   [[version]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7}, which declares the version number of the extension. This is used by extension stores for differentiating between releases.

-   [[name]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8}, which declares the name of the extension. This is used by both the browser as well as extension stores as the top-line identifier of the extension.
:::
::::

Therefore, the simplest possible manifest would be the following:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par13 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB93 .pagebreak aria-label="93" role="doc-pagebreak" title="93"}*manifest.json*

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVP Extension\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"1.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
### Supporting Different Locales {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

Several manifest properties define strings and textual images that are visible to the user. In situations where the extension needs to support multiple languages based on the user's [locale]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9}, the manifest supports dynamically loading strings from message files based on the contextual user's locale.

To add locale support, message files must be added to a [\_locales]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [directory]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10}. This directory contains one directory for each locale you wish to support. Inside each of these subdirectories, a single [messages.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file defines the localized strings. The extension can then automatically load these strings.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par16 .Para}
Suppose the localized string [extensionName]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is defined in [\_locales]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This value can be retrieved in the following ways:

::: UnorderedList
-   In the manifest file, the browser dynamically selects the corresponding string value using the special [\_\_MSG_extensionName\_\_]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} identifier.

-   In extension JavaScript, the localized value can be retrieved with [chrome.i18n.getMessage(\"extensionName\")]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par19 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB94 .pagebreak aria-label="94" role="doc-pagebreak" title="94"}The following example shows a simple manifest that supports English and French locales:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
localized-extension-directory/
:::

::: FixedLine
├─ manifest.json
:::

::: FixedLine
└─ **\_locales/**
:::

::: FixedLine
   ├─ **en/**
:::

::: FixedLine
   │  └─ **messages.json**
:::

::: FixedLine
   └─ **fr/**
:::

::: FixedLine
      └─ **messages.json**
:::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

The manifest file would be written as follows:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par21 .Para}
*manifest.json*

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": **\"\_\_MSG_extensionName\_\_**\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"1.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"default_locale\": \"en\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

When using locales, the [default_locale]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11} is required in your manifest. The required formatting of the [messages.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} files is shown below:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par23 .Para}
*\_locales/en/messages.json*

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"extensionName\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"message\": \"Hello world!\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par24 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB95 .pagebreak aria-label="95" role="doc-pagebreak" title="95"}*\_locales/fr/messages.json*

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"extensionName\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"message\": \"Bonjour le monde!\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

Locales and internationalization is a broad and deep subject. Refer to [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/Internationalization]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/Internationalization)]{.ExternalRef} for additional details on locale placeholders, additional message properties, and more.
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The [WebExtensions i18n API]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12} and [extension CSS files]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13} can also use locale strings defined in these [messages.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} files. Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
### Match Patterns and Globs {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

Several properties in the manifest can use match patterns and globs to specify multiple URLs or files at once.

::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
#### File Path Match Patterns {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par28 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB96 .pagebreak aria-label="96" role="doc-pagebreak" title="96"}Properties such as [[web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17} can use match patterns to select single files or entire directories. As is the case with all file paths in the manifest file, they are resolved with respect to the root directory of the extension. The syntax allows for the use of [\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} wildcards to match zero to many characters. The following are some examples of valid file match patterns:

::: UnorderedList
-   [\"/\*\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all files.

-   [\"/foo/\*\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all files in the [foo]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} directory.

-   [\"/foo/\*.png\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all PNG files in the [foo]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} directory.

-   [\"/foo/bar.png\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects a single PNG file in the [foo]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} directory.
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
#### URL Match Patterns {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par33 .Para}
[Properties]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20} such as [content_scripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} can use match patterns to whitelist single URLs or entire web origins. The syntax allows for the use of [\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} wildcards to match zero to many characters. The following are some examples of valid URL match patterns:

::: UnorderedList
-   [\"\<all_urls\>\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all URLs with a protocol of [http://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [https://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [ws://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [wss://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [ftp://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [data://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, or [file://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

-   [\"https://\*/\*\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all URLs with an [https://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} protocol.

-   [\"\*://example.com/\*\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all URLs with an [example.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} origin.

-   []{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB97 .pagebreak aria-label="97" role="doc-pagebreak" title="97"}[\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[https://example.com/foo/\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all [example.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} URLs with a [foo/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} path prefix.

-   [\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[https://example.com/foo/bar]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects a single URL.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For specific details on the rules for match patterns, refer to [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/mv3/match_patterns/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/mv3/match_patterns/)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
#### URL Globs {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par40 .Para}
[Properties]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23} such as [content_scripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} can use globs to add additional filtering to URL match patterns. Globs are an extension of URL match patterns; their syntax allows for the use of [\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} wildcards to match zero to many characters, as well as [?]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} to match exactly one character:

::: UnorderedList
-   [\"\*://example.com/\*\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all URLs with an [example.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} origin.

-   [\"\*://example.???/\*\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} selects all URLs with an [example.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} origin followed by a three-character TLD, such as [example.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [example.org,]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [example.gov]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For specific details on the rules for globs, refer to [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/mv3/content_scripts/#matchAndGlob]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/mv3/content_scripts/#matchAndGlob)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::::
::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
## Manifest Version {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB98 .pagebreak aria-label="98" role="doc-pagebreak" title="98"}The [*manifest version*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25} defines the structure and capabilities of browser extensions, serving as the blueprint for how extensions interact with the browser and its underlying APIs. Over a decade ago, [Manifest V2 (MV2)]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27} was introduced, offering developers significant flexibility to create powerful extensions. [Manifest V3 (MV3)]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29}, a modernization of this framework, brings stricter rules and new paradigms such as service worker-based background scripts and declarative APIs for network requests. While to some extent these changes improve security and efficiency, they are highly controversial for limiting some of the functionality that made MV2 so versatile. Today, MV3 is the standard for building browser extensions, reflecting the evolving priorities of browser vendors, particularly in enhancing user safety and system performance.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par45 .Para}
Key differences between Manifest V2 and Manifest V3 include

::: UnorderedList
-   :::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par46 .Para}
    [**Service workers**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30}**:** Persistent background scripts from MV2 have been replaced by service workers, which conserve resources by starting and stopping automatically but complicate tasks requiring long-lived connections or global state. This also has some downstream effects:

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   [**Offscreen support (Chromium only)**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31}**:** MV3 allows service workers to access [OffscreenCanvas]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and the [offscreen]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API for graphics rendering tasks, replacing DOM-based methods previously available in MV2.

    -   [**Persistent storage**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32}**:** In Manifest V2, background pages were persistent and allowed global variables and synchronous access to [localStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. In Manifest V3, background pages are replaced []{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB99 .pagebreak aria-label="99" role="doc-pagebreak" title="99"}by short-lived service workers, which cannot access [localStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Extensions must now use asynchronous storage like [chrome.storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or IndexedDB to maintain persistent data.

    -   [**Timer API changes**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33}**:** The lack of persistent background scripts means traditional timer APIs like [setInterval]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [setTimeout]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} may not work as expected. Developers must use alternatives like the Alarms API to handle timed events reliably.
    :::
    ::::

-   [**DeclarativeNetRequest API**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34}**:** MV3 replaces the dynamic blocking [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API with the static [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API, offering improved performance but reducing flexibility. MV3 also enforces a global cap on the number of network filtering rules across all installed extensions.

-   [**Content Security Policy**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35}**:** MV3 enforces stricter security by disallowing inline scripts, remote scripts, and user-provided code, addressing vulnerabilities but severely restricting certain use cases such as userscript extensions. (The [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API is an exception to this.)

-   [**Restructured permissions**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36}**:** MV3 introduces more granular and transparent permission models to give users greater control over what extensions can access.
:::
::::

The previous edition of this book included detailed coverage of the differences between MV2 and MV3. In 2025, the reality for extension developers is that MV2, while still supported by some marginal browsers out of protest, is obsolete. As a result, the majority of MV2 coverage in the previous edition of this book has been phased out.
:::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
## Manifest Properties {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB100 .pagebreak aria-label="100" role="doc-pagebreak" title="100"}This section contains a comprehensive list of manifest properties and how their values should be defined. Many of these properties are linked to additional browser permissions or parts of the WebExtensions API; each section will direct you to the portion of the book detailing the interplay of the manifest property value, required permissions, and the involved APIs.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par55 .Para}
When applicable, manifest properties will include the following [labels]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}:

::: UnorderedList
-   **MV2 only:** This manifest property is only available in Manifest V2.

-   **MV3 only:** This manifest property is only available in Manifest V3.

-   **MV2/MV3 breaking change:** This manifest property is used differently between Manifest V2 and V3, meaning that different manifests will need to be generated to support each version.

-   **Firefox only:** This property is only available in Mozilla Firefox browsers.

-   **Chromium browsers only:** This property is only available in Chromium browsers (e.g., Chrome, Brave, Opera, Edge).

-   **Chrome OS only:** This property is only available in Chrome OS extensions.

-   **Non-Chromium browsers only:** This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38} is not available in Chromium browsers.

-   **Deprecated:** This property should no longer be used.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par64 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB101 .pagebreak aria-label="101" role="doc-pagebreak" title="101"}If you wish to inspect the browser source code involved with parsing manifest properties, use the following resources:

::: UnorderedList
-   [Chromium manifests]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40}: [[[[https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/main/chrome/common/extensions/api/\_manifest_features.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/main/chrome/common/extensions/api/_manifest_features.json)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [Firefox manifests]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42}: [[[[https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/source/browser/components/extensions/schemas]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/source/browser/components/extensions/schemas)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar7 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

As you read through this chapter, you will quickly discover that a wide range of manifest properties are incompatible between browsers and manifest versions. For an extension to support multiple browsers and/or manifest versions, you will need to generate multiple manifests for each. This is a complex subject and is covered in more detail in Chapter [[[13]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
### action {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

**MV3 Only**

::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par69 .Para}
This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43} defines an object containing values that dictate the behavior and appearance of the extension icon in the browser toolbar (Figure [[5-1](#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef}). This is the Manifest V3 replacement for the [browser_action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [page_action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} properties in Manifest V2.[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB102 .pagebreak aria-label="102" role="doc-pagebreak" title="102"}

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_5_Chapter/534317_2_En_5_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser toolbar screenshot showing an extension icon labeled &quot;M&quot; with a tooltip displaying &quot;My Example Extension.&quot; The cursor is hovering over the icon, indicating interaction. Other visible icons include a star, a folder, and a user profile." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 5-1</span>
<p>The extension toolbar icon with hover title revealed</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

The [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property is shown below with a fully defined example value:

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par71 .Para}
*action property in* [*manifest.json*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"action\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_icon\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"16\": \"icons/icon-16.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"32\": \"icons/icon-32.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"64\": \"icons/icon-64.png\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_popup\": \"popup/popup.html\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_title\": \"My Example Extension\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"browser_style\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"theme_icons\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"light\": \"icons/icon-16-light.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"dark\": \"icons/icon-16.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"size\": 16**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"light\": \"icons/icon-32-light.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"dark\": \"icons/icon-32.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"size\": 32**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par72 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB103 .pagebreak aria-label="103" role="doc-pagebreak" title="103"}The [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} object supports the following properties:

::: UnorderedList
-   [**default_icon**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46}**:** Image assets for the browser toolbar button, specifying multiple sizes for different screen resolutions or zoom levels

-   :::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par74 .Para}
    Zero or one of the following:

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   [**default_popup**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48}**:** The popup HTML file that should be rendered when the toolbar button is clicked.

    -   [**side_panel**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50}**:** Defines the behavior for the side panel. The [default_path]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} specifies the HTML file to display in the side panel when the extension is active.
    :::
    ::::

-   [**default_title**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52}**:** The alt text or tooltip displayed when the user hovers over the toolbar button

-   []{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB104 .pagebreak aria-label="104" role="doc-pagebreak" title="104"}[**browser_style**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54}**:** A flag to apply vendor-specific browser CSS styling, ensuring a native look and feel (Firefox only)

-   [**theme_icons**]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56}**:** Image assets for system themes like light or dark mode, allowing the toolbar icon to match the user's system preferences
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par80 .Para}
When the user clicks the toolbar button, the [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} definition can trigger the following behaviors:

::: UnorderedList
-   **Open a popup**[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57}**:** If [default_popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is defined, clicking the button will render the specified HTML file.

-   **Display a side panel**[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58}**:** If the [side_panel]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property is defined, users can access the side panel via the extension's icon or browser UI. The specified [default_path]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is loaded into the panel, offering a persistent interface for the extension.

-   **Send an event to the background script**[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59}**:** If neither [default_popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} nor [side_panel]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is not defined, clicking the button triggers an [onClicked]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} event in the background script. This allows for custom behavior, such as navigating to a specific URL or toggling extension functionality.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar8 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[7]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on popups and side panels.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
#### default_icon {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB105 .pagebreak aria-label="105" role="doc-pagebreak" title="105"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61} tells the browser where to locate icons for the toolbar via relative paths to image files. There are two ways to define this property: either provide PNG or JPEG image icons and allow the browser to intelligently select which icon it would prefer or provide a single vector image.

Providing one or multiple image [icons]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63} allows the browser to decide which icon is best for the current hardware configuration.
