### file_system_provider_capabilities {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec48Heading .Heading}

**ChromeOS Only**

**MV3 Only**

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB142 .pagebreak aria-label="142" role="doc-pagebreak" title="142"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm268}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm269} defines an object containing properties that govern how the extension can interact with the host device's filesystem via the File System Provider API. This is only applicable on ChromeOS devices. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par272 .Para}
*Example value for* [*file_system_provider_capabilities*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm270}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm271}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC38 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"file_system_provider_capabilities\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"configurable\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"watchable\": false,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"multiple_mounts\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"source\": \"network\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar33 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The ChromeOS file manager and filesystem are out of the scope of this book. For documentation, refer to [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/fileSystemProvider/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/fileSystemProvider/)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec49 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec49Heading"}
### homepage_url {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec49Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB143 .pagebreak aria-label="143" role="doc-pagebreak" title="143"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm272}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm273} provides an extension homepage URL for display in the browser. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par275 .Para}
*Example value for* [*homepage_url*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm274}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm275}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC39 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"homepage_url\": \"https://www.buildingbrowserextensions.com\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar34 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

If the [developer.url]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm276} is defined, it will override this value. This is not supported in Chrome or Safari.
::::
:::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec50 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec50Heading"}
### host_permissions {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec50Heading .Heading}

**MV3 Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm277}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm278} defines the host match patterns that the extension requires to run. If the web page matches one or more patterns in this list, the extension will have the ability to read or modify host data, such as [cookies]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [tabs.executeScript]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par279 .Para}
*Example value for* [*host_permissions*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm279}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm280}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC40 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"host_permissions\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"\*://developer.mozilla.org/\*\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"\*://developer.chrome.com/\*\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB144 .pagebreak aria-label="144" role="doc-pagebreak" title="144"}Importantly, the [[host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm281}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm282} values are unrelated to the [content_scripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} pattern matchers. Pattern matchers that control which pages should have content scripts injected are defined inside the [content_scripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm283}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm284}.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar35 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[10]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details on extension permissions.
::::
::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec51 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec51Heading"}
### icons {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec51Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm285} defines an object containing values that dictate the extension's primary [icon]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm286}. This icon is used both during installation as well as in various extension marketplaces. At a minimum, you should define a 128x128 raster image (JPEG, PNG, BMP, or ICO image). If you wish to support all marketplaces and browsers, you should define four PNG images with dimensions 16x16, 32x32, 48x48, and 128x128.

The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par284 .Para}
*Example value for icons*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm287}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm288}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC41 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"icons\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"16\": \"assets/icons/icon-16.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"32\": \" assets/icons/icon-32.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"48\": \" assets/icons/icon-48.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"128\": \" assets/icons/icon-128.png\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar36 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB145 .pagebreak aria-label="145" role="doc-pagebreak" title="145"}Prefer to use raster image formats (PNG, JPEG) vs. vector formats (SVG). Vector format support is uneven across browsers.
::::
::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec52 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec52Heading"}
### import {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec52Heading .Heading}

**ChromeOS Only**

Part of the Shared Modules [specification]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm289}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm290}, see *export*. This field should not be used, as the Chrome Web Store does not allow the submission of shared modules.
:::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec53 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec53Heading"}
### incognito {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec53Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm291}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm292} allows you to define how the extension can interact with private browsing windows. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par289 .Para}
*Example value for* [*incognito*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm293}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm294}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC42 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"incognito\": \"spanning\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par290 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB146 .pagebreak aria-label="146" role="doc-pagebreak" title="146"}The [incognito]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm295}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm296} can have one of three values:

::: UnorderedList
-   [spanning]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} allows the extension to treat both private and non-private pages in the same manner. It can differentiate between the two via the [chrome.windows.getLastFocused()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method. This value is the default.

-   [split]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} will partition the extension into two separate parts: one part handles private pages, and one part handles non-private pages. These parts behave as two separate extension instances.

-   [not_allowed]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [disables]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm297}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm298} the extension on private browsing.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar37 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Support for this property is fragmented between browsers; refer to [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/manifest/incognito]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/manifest/incognito)]{.ExternalRef} and [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/manifest.json/incognito]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/manifest.json/incognito)]{.ExternalRef} for details.
::::
:::::::::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec54 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec54Heading"}
### key {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec54Heading .Heading}

**Chromium Browsers Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm299}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm300} specifies the extension's cryptographic key, from which the 32-character extension ID is deterministically derived by taking the first 32 characters of the base16-encoded SHA-256 hash of [key]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. If the [key]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is not provided, the browser will automatically generate a key and corresponding extension ID.

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par297 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB147 .pagebreak aria-label="147" role="doc-pagebreak" title="147"}*Example value for key*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm301}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm302}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC43 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"key\": \" MIIEvQIBADANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAASCBKcwggSjAgEAAoIBAQDFQPtUi3d/9KPDPV9OOCJ/Cj\...\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar38 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This is only needed in the rare circumstance where you wish to explicitly define the extension ID for local development at load time. This value is not used when deploying to a web store.
::::
:::::::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec55 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec55Heading"}
### manifest_version {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec55Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm303}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm304} indicates to the browser how the manifest should be interpreted. As described earlier in the chapter, this integer has significant implications for how the overall manifest file must be structured. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par300 .Para}
*Example value for* [*manifest_version*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm305}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm306}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC44 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"manifest_version\": 3**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar39 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB148 .pagebreak aria-label="148" role="doc-pagebreak" title="148"}[Chromium]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm307} is at the final stages of phasing out Manifest V2 entirely.
::::
:::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec56 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec56Heading"}
### minimum_chrome_version {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec56Heading .Heading}

**Chromium Browsers Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm308}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm309} defines the minimum version of Chrome required for the extension. [Non-Chromium browsers]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm310} will ignore this property. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par304 .Para}
*Example value for* [*minimum_chrome_version*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm311}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm312}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC45 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"minimum_chrome_version\": \"90\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec57 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec57Heading"}
### nacl_modules {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec57Heading .Heading}

**Deprecated**

This property was used for [Native Client support (NaCl)]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm313}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm314}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm315}; its use is deprecated.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar40 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Native Client was a sandbox for running compiled C and C++ code in the browser efficiently and securely, independent of the user's operating system. It was deprecated in 2020 and support ended in June 2021.
::::
:::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec58 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec58Heading"}
### name {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec58Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB149 .pagebreak aria-label="149" role="doc-pagebreak" title="149"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm316}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm317} defines the formal name of the extension. This value is displayed in the browser as well as in the extension marketplaces. This name should not exceed 75 characters. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par309 .Para}
*Example value for* [*name*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm318}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm319}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC46 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"name\": \"Building Browser Extensions\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec59 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec59Heading"}
### oauth2 {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec59Heading .Heading}

**Chromium Browsers Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm320}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm321} is used to register your extension as an OAuth2 client. Its value is an object with the OAuth2 client ID and scopes:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par312 .Para}
*Example value for oauth2*

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC47 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"oauth2\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"client_id\": \"oAuthClientID.apps.googleusercontent.com\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"scopes\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"https://www.googleapis.com/auth/contacts.readonly\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar41 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB150 .pagebreak aria-label="150" role="doc-pagebreak" title="150"}Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details on setting up authentication for your extension.
::::
::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec60 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec60Heading"}
### offline_enabled {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec60Heading .Heading}

**Deprecated**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm322}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm323} is deprecated and should not be used. In legacy code bases, it indicated if the extension was expected to work offline.
:::

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec61 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec61Heading"}
### omnibox {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec61Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm324}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm325} enables the browser's native omnibox interface by defining its entry keyword. The value is an object containing a single [keyword]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par317 .Para}
*Example value for* [*omnibox*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm326}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm327}

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC48 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"omnibox\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"keyword\": \"bbx\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par318 .Para}
Suppose a user has an extension installed with the omnibox configuration shown above. The [omnibox]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm328}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm329} interface works as follows:

::: OrderedList
1.  ::: ItemNumber
    1\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The user focuses on the empty browser address bar.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

2.  ::: ItemNumber
    2\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    The user types "[bbx]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}" followed by a space.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

3.  ::: ItemNumber
    3\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    []{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB151 .pagebreak aria-label="151" role="doc-pagebreak" title="151"}The browser opens the omnibox interface (Figure [[5-10](#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Fig10)]{.InternalRef}).
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::
:::

<figure id="Fig10" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_MO10" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_5_Chapter/534317_2_En_5_Fig10_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser screenshot showing a search bar with the text &quot;My Example Extension&quot; and a dropdown list of search suggestions. The suggestions include &quot;BBC - Broadcasting company,&quot; &quot;bbxbrand,&quot; &quot;BBX Capital - Company,&quot; &quot;bbx logistics properties,&quot; &quot;BBC Sport,&quot; &quot;bbx martial arts - Black Belt Excellence - 151 Ogden Ave, Westmont, IL,&quot; and &quot;BBC.com - bbc.com.&quot; The URL bar displays &quot;about:blank.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 5-10</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm330">omnibox interface</span><span id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm331"></span><span id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm332"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar42 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The extension can control the omnibox behavior with the Omnibox API. This is covered in depth in Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec62 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec62Heading"}
### optional_host_permissions {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec62Heading .Heading}

**MV3 Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm333}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm334} defines the host match patterns that the extension does not explicitly require to run, but that the user can opt into. The match pattern behavior and syntax are identical to [host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. The [permission]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm335}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm336} granting user interface is identical to [optional_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec63 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec63Heading"}
### optional_permissions {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec63Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB152 .pagebreak aria-label="152" role="doc-pagebreak" title="152"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm337}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm338} defines the permissions that the extension does not require to run correctly, but that it can prompt the user for access at runtime. This property is often used to add extension permissions in subsequent releases without requiring that all users grant access to a new permission. Not all permission types are eligible to appear in this array. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par326 .Para}
*Example value for* [*optional_permissions*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm339}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm340}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC49 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"optional_permissions\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"cookies\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"history\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"notifications\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar43 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[10]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details on extension permissions.
::::
:::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec64 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec64Heading"}
### options_page {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec64Heading .Heading}

**Deprecated**

This is a deprecated [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm341}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm342}. Use [options_ui]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} instead.
:::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec65 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec65Heading"}
### options_ui {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec65Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB153 .pagebreak aria-label="153" role="doc-pagebreak" title="153"}This property tells the browser where to locate the HTML file that should render as the options page when opened either programmatically, by URL, or via the toolbar context menu. It is an object that typically only contains a single [page]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property but also supports additional properties in some browsers. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par331 .Para}
*Example value for* [*options_ui*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm343}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm344}

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC50 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"options_ui\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"page\": \"components/options/options.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec66 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec66Heading"}
#### browser_style {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec66Heading .Heading}

**Firefox Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm345}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm346}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm347} is a boolean that tells the browser if it should inject a stylesheet in the options page to [style]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm348}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm349}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm350} it consistently with the browser. These stylesheets can be viewed in any Firefox browser at [chrome://browser/content/extension.css]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, or [chrome://browser/content/extension-mac.css]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} on macOS. The property defaults to [false]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par334 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB154 .pagebreak aria-label="154" role="doc-pagebreak" title="154"}*Example value for* [*browser_style*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm351}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm352}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm353}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC51 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"options_ui\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"page\": \"components/options/options.html\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"browser_style\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec67 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec67Heading"}
#### open_in_tab {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec67Heading .Heading}

**Firefox Only**

::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par336 .Para}
This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm354}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm355}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm356} is a boolean that tells the browser if it should open the options page in a normal browser tab, rather than in an embedded options page (Figure [[5-11](#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Fig11)]{.InternalRef}). The property defaults to [true]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

<figure id="Fig11" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_MO11" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_5_Chapter/534317_2_En_5_Fig11_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Chrome browser window displaying the &quot;Extensions&quot; page. A modal titled &quot;Example Chrome Extension&quot; is open, showing an &quot;Options Page&quot; with a description and a code snippet: `chrome.runtime.openOptionsPage()`. Three buttons are visible: &quot;Open with anchor href,&quot; &quot;Open with window.open(),&quot; and &quot;Select demo.&quot; The page includes a book cover image titled &quot;Building Browser Extensions.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 5-11</span>
<p>An embedded options <span id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm357">interface</span><span id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm358"></span><span id="534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm359"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par338 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB155 .pagebreak aria-label="155" role="doc-pagebreak" title="155"}*Example value for* [*options_ui*]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm360}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm361}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm362}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC52 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"options_ui\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"page\": \"components/options/options.html\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"open_in_tab\": false,**
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar44 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[7]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on options pages.
::::
:::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec68 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec68Heading"}
### page_action {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec68Heading .Heading}

**MV2 Only**

The [page_action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm363}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm364} was replaced by [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in Manifest V3. The property values and behaviors are effectively identical. If using Manifest V2, refer to the [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property for details on how to assign a value for this property.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar45 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This property is an artifact of extension tooling for older browser versions. [browser_action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [page_action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} are redundant in all modern browsers. As a result, these properties have been merged into the [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property, which is identical in format and behavior.
::::
:::::

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec69 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec69Heading"}
### permissions {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec69Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB156 .pagebreak aria-label="156" role="doc-pagebreak" title="156"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm365}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm366} defines the permissions that the extension requires to run correctly. All permission types are eligible to appear in this array. Upon installation and updates, all additions to this array will require that users explicitly grant access to the newly added permission in a popup dialog. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par344 .Para}
*Example value for permissions*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm367}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm368}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC53 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"permissions\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"activeTab\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"declarativeNetRequest\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"geolocation\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

In [Manifest V2]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm369}, the permissions array also included URL pattern matchers that are now defined in the [host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property. Modern [extensions]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm370}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm371} should now be placing URL pattern matchers only in the [host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [optional_host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} properties.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar46 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[10]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details on extension permissions.
::::
:::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec70 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec70Heading"}
### platforms {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec70Heading .Heading}

**Deprecated**

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB157 .pagebreak aria-label="157" role="doc-pagebreak" title="157"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm372}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm373} was used for [Native Client support (NaCl)]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm374}; its use is deprecated.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar47 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Native Client was a sandbox for running compiled C and C++ code in the browser efficiently and securely, independent of the user's operating system. It was deprecated in 2020 and support ended in June 2021.
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec71 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec71Heading"}
### replacement_web_app {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec71Heading .Heading}

**Deprecated**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm375}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm376}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm377} was used as part of Google's supported transition from Chrome Apps in 2016. Its purpose was so that [Chrome Apps]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm378}, which shared APIs with web extensions, could direct their users to a replacement Progressive Web Application via an [installReplacementWebApp()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method. No modern extensions will have a use for this property.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar48 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Read more about the transition from Chrome Apps here: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/apps/migration/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/apps/migration/)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec72 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec72Heading"}
### requirements {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec72Heading .Heading}

**Chromium Browsers Only**

**Deprecated**

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB158 .pagebreak aria-label="158" role="doc-pagebreak" title="158"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm379}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm380} defines the browser technologies required by the extension. If a user's device does not meet the requirements, the Chrome Web Store will inform them that their device lacks the needed technology to run the extension. Currently, the only actively supported property is "3D." The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par356 .Para}
*Example value for requirements*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm381}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm382}

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC54 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"requirements\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"3D\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"features\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"webgl\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec73 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec73Heading"}
### sandbox {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec73Heading .Heading}

**Chromium Browsers Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm383}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm384} defines which pages should render in sandbox mode. Its value is an object with a single [page]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} array containing paths to each file that should render in sandbox mode. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par359 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB159 .pagebreak aria-label="159" role="doc-pagebreak" title="159"}*Example value for sandbox*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm385}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm386}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC55 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"sandbox\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"pages\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"components/popup/popup.html\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"components/options/options.html\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar49 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For additional details on sandboxing pages, refer to Chapter [[[4]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec74 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec74Heading"}
### short_name {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec74Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm387}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm388} defines the secondary name of the extension that will be used in contexts where the [name]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property is too long, such as the side panel or Omnibox. This name should not exceed 12 characters. If this is not provided, the browser will simply truncate the [name]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par362 .Para}
*Example value for short_name*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm389}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm390}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC56 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"short_name\": \"BBX\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec75 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec75Heading"}
### side_panel {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec75Heading .Heading}

**Chrome Only**

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB160 .pagebreak aria-label="160" role="doc-pagebreak" title="160"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm391}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm392} specifies the HTML file that should render as a side panel when the extension's side panel is opened. The side panel provides an area for displaying extension-specific content alongside the current browser tab. This property can also be dynamically set or updated via the WebExtensions API using the [chrome.sidePanel.setPanelOptions()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method. Here's an example of how this property can be configured:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par365 .Para}
*Example value for default_side_panel*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm393}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm394}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC57 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"side_panel\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"default_panel\": \"components/side_panel/side_panel.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::

By defining [side_panel]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm395}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm396}, the browser knows where to locate and display the side panel's content when triggered.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar50 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[7]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on side panels.
::::
::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec76 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec76Heading"}
### storage {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec76Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm397}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm398} specifies the schema file for managed storage, which is involved with the [storage.managed]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm399}. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par369 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB161 .pagebreak aria-label="161" role="doc-pagebreak" title="161"}*Example value for storage*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm400}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm401}

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC58 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"storage\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"managed_schema\": \"schema.json\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar51 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Browsers handle managed storage differently. Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details.
::::
:::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec77 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec77Heading"}
### system_indicator {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec77Heading .Heading}

**Deprecated**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm402}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm403} is deprecated and should not be used.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar52 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to [[[[https://bugs.chromium.org/p/chromium/issues/detail?id=142450]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://bugs.chromium.org/p/chromium/issues/detail?id=142450)]{.ExternalRef} for a history of this property.
::::
:::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec78 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec78Heading"}
### tts_engine {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec78Heading .Heading}

**Chromium Browsers Only**

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm404}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm405} is used to declare all the voices and voice configurations the extension wishes to use for the browser's text-to-speech engine. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par376 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB162 .pagebreak aria-label="162" role="doc-pagebreak" title="162"}*Example value for tts_engine*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm406}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm407}

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC59 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"tts_engine\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"voices\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"voice_name\": \"Alice\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"lang\": \"en-US\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"event_types\": \[\"start\", \"marker\", \"end\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"voice_name\": \"Pat\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"lang\": \"en-US\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"event_types\": \[\"end\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar53 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For details on Chrome's text-to-speech API, refer to [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/ttsEngine/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/ttsEngine/)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec79 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec79Heading"}
### update_url {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec79Heading .Heading}

**Chromium Browsers Only**

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB163 .pagebreak aria-label="163" role="doc-pagebreak" title="163"}This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm408}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm409} defines the URL from which the browser should request updates for the extension. The [update_url]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is utilized by all Chromium-based extensions. For extensions hosted on the Chrome Web Store, this URL is automatically managed and supplied by the store itself. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par380 .Para}
*Example value for update_url*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm410}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm411}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC60 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"update_url\": \"https://example.com/updates.xml\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar54 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[13]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details on self-hosting extensions.
::::
:::::::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec80 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec80Heading"}
### version {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec80Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm412}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm413} defines the formal version number of the extension. It is used to both uniquely identify different releases of your extension as well as to determine ordinality of those releases.

Browsers set different requirements for this value; for example, the [Chrome version validator]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm414} is more restrictive than Firefox. For simplicity and cross-browser compatibility, it is strongly recommended that you conform to the semantic versioning standards described at [[[[https://semver.org/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://semver.org/)]{.ExternalRef}. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par384 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB164 .pagebreak aria-label="164" role="doc-pagebreak" title="164"}*Example value for version*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm415}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm416}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC61 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"version\": \"1.5.0\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar55 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The [MAJOR.MINOR]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.BUILD]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} are all acceptable version formats that all browsers will accept.
::::
:::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec81 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec81Heading"}
### version_name {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec81Heading .Heading}

This [property]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm417}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm418} defines a descriptor for the extension version. It allows you to add in handles like [beta]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [rc1]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} that offer additional information about the release but do not conform to the browser's extension version validators. The following is an example of how this property can be used:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par387 .Para}
*Example value for version_name*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm419}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm420}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC62 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"version_name\": \"1.5.0 beta\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec82 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec82Heading"}
### web_accessible_resources {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec82Heading .Heading}

**MV2/MV3 Breaking Change**

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB165 .pagebreak aria-label="165" role="doc-pagebreak" title="165"}By default, files inside an extension package are not accessible from outside the extension. This property defines which [extension]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm421}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm422} files can be accessed from a web page or external extension via an access policy. Any file that is part of an extension is eligible to be listed under [web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This property is intended to allow for images, JS, CSS, HTML, and other static assets in the [extension]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm423}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm424} to be added into a web page or an external extension.

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar56 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The property is used in both Manifest V2 and V3, but the Manifest V3 specification adds extra controls for managing an access control list for each resource. This section only covers the MV3 formatting.
::::

The following is an example of how this property can be used:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par392 .Para}
*Definition of web_accessible_resources*[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm425}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm426} *that gives the full* example.com *origin the ability to the extension\'s images*

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC63 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"web_accessible_resources\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"resources\": \[ \"/static/images/\*\" \],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"matches\": \[ \"\*://\*.example.com/\*\" \]**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par393 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB166 .pagebreak aria-label="166" role="doc-pagebreak" title="166"}The value must be an array of objects with the following properties:

::: UnorderedList
-   [[resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm427} is the list of patterns that match files in the extension. This is equivalent to the full [web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property in Manifest V2. This property is required.

-   [matches]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [extension_ids]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [control]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm428} where the resources list is accessible. Exactly one of these properties must be defined. [matches]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm429}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm430} is an array of patterns that match web page origins eligible to access the resource list. [extension_ids]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is an array of extension IDs eligible to access the resource list.

-   [[use_dynamic_url]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm431} is an optional boolean that, when [true]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, will instruct the browser to rotate the URL of the resources each session. This property defaults to [false]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Because extension IDs are static throughout the lifetime of that extension, any resource by default will have the same URL between releases. Enabling this property makes the URL of that resource unpredictable.
:::
::::

In practice, accessing these exposed files is accomplished by loading them from the [extension]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm432}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm433} file server (discussed in Chapter [[[4]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_4_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}) via [fetch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Knowledge of the file path is a prerequisite, as there is no file index or extension file discovery [mechanism]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm434}[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm435}.

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Par398 .Para}
Continuing from the above example, a content script running on [www.example.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} would be able to load an image as follows:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PC64 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const imgPath = \"/static/images/banana.png\";
:::

::: FixedLine
const imgUrl = chrome.runtime.getURL(imgPath);
:::
:::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Option 1: load via fetch()
:::

::: FixedLine
const imgResponse = fetch(imgUrl);
:::
:::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Option 2: load via \<img\> element
:::

::: FixedLine
document.querySelector(\"img\").src = imgUrl;
:::
:::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_FPar57 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_PB167 .pagebreak aria-label="167" role="doc-pagebreak" title="167"}Once an extension file becomes accessible to a web page via [web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the extension is unable to differentiate between a [[fetch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm436} request from a content script vs. the page itself. If you don\'t wish to directly expose files inside the extension to the host page, pass files to a content script via extension messaging or extension storage instead.
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec83 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec83Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml_Sec83Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you were guided through what a manifest does and how to create one. The chapter took you through all possible manifest properties, what they do, how they should be defined, what APIs they work with, and which browsers can use them. When you come across a manifest property, you should be able to refer to the relevant section in this chapter to quickly decipher what that property is doing.

The next chapter will explore background scripts. It will cover in depth how they work, how best to build them, and how they behave as the nerve center of browser extension.
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml}

::::: ChapterContextInformation
:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_b979-8-8688-1594-2_6 .ContextInformation}
::: ChapterCopyright
© The Author(s), under exclusive license to APress Media, LLC, part of Springer Nature 2025
:::

[M. Frisbie]{.ContextInformationAuthorEditorNames}[[Building Browser Extensions]{.BookTitle}]{.ContextInformationBookTitles}[<https://doi.org/10.1007/979-8-8688-1594-2_6>]{.ChapterDOI}
::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 6. Background Scripts {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB169 .pagebreak aria-label="169" role="doc-pagebreak" title="169"}[Browser extension developers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1} often find the need to execute JavaScript in the background of a browser. Background scripts, as their name suggests, are the answer to this problem: they are a separate JavaScript runtime that can communicate with all the different pieces of the extension, add handlers for many different browser events, and operate without dependence on any web page or extension user interface.

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This chapter will focus on the Manifest V3 implementation of background scripts as service workers. Manifest V2 featured *background pages*, which were considerably different and, in many ways, more useful. Chromium browsers have largely phased out support for them, so I do not recommend building a browser extension that relies on background pages.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Web Page Service Workers vs. Extension Service Workers {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

A common point of confusion for web developers new to browser extensions is the nature of background service workers. Although web service workers and extension service workers share their underlying infrastructure, the manner in which they are declared and used is radically different.

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB170 .pagebreak aria-label="170" role="doc-pagebreak" title="170"}Compare the following two examples, a simple web page service worker and a simple browser extension service worker:

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par6 .Para}
[*Sample web page service worker*]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Establish a cache name
:::

::: FixedLine
const cacheName = \'cache_v1\';
:::

::: FixedLine
// Assets to precache
:::

::: FixedLine
const precachedAssets = \[
:::

::: FixedLine
  \'/img1.jpg\',
:::

::: FixedLine
  \'/img2.jpg\',
:::

::: FixedLine
  \'/img3.jpg\'
:::

::: FixedLine
\];
:::

::: FixedLine
self.addEventListener(\'install\', (event) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Precache assets on install
:::

::: FixedLine
  event.waitUntil(caches.open(cacheName).then((cache) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return cache.addAll(precachedAssets);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }));
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
self.addEventListener(\'fetch\', (event) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Test for precached assets match
:::

::: FixedLine
  const url = new URL(event.request.url);
:::

::: FixedLine
  const isPrecachedRequest =
:::

::: FixedLine
      precachedAssets.includes(url.pathname);
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (isPrecachedRequest) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Grab the precached asset from the cache
:::

::: FixedLine
    event.respondWith(caches.open(cacheName).then((cache) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      return cache.match(event.request.url);
:::

::: FixedLine
    }));
:::

::: FixedLine
  } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Go to the network
:::

::: FixedLine
    return;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB171 .pagebreak aria-label="171" role="doc-pagebreak" title="171"}This simple web page service worker defines an image cache. On an [install]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [event]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3}, it preloads the images into the cache. When the service worker observes a [fetch]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} event matching the URL for one of those images, it intercepts the request and returns the cached value.

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par8 .Para}
[*Sample extension service worker*]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4}

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// This will run on an install or update
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\'Installed or updated!\');
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// This will run when a bookmark is created.
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.bookmarks.onCreated.addListener(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\'Created a new bookmark!\');
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
const filter = {
:::

::: FixedLine
  url: \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      urlMatches: \'https://www.example.com/\',
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
};
:::

::: FixedLine
// This will run when the example site is visited
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.webNavigation.onCompleted.addListener(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Loaded the example site!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
}, filter);
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB172 .pagebreak aria-label="172" role="doc-pagebreak" title="172"}This simple extension service worker sets listeners for the [runtime.onInstalled]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [bookmark.onCreated]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [webNavigation.onCompleted]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} events. Each handler prints a log statement to the console when those events are fired.

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par10 .Para}
When comparing the two [examples]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5} from above, you will notice the following:

::: UnorderedList
-   Both service worker scripts are largely **composed of event handlers**.

-   Both service workers are **listening for an installation event**.

-   Both service workers can **access network requests**.
:::
::::

With these code similarities in mind, let's explore the ways that these service worker contexts are alike.

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
### Service Worker Similarities {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par15 .Para}
To frame the similarities between these two implementations, we'll turn to the W3C specification for service workers. It begins with the following [high-level characterization]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6}:

::: UnorderedList
-   The core of this specification is a worker that wakes to receive events.
:::
::::

In this respect, a web page service worker and an extension service worker are in fact serving the same purpose. What's more, the distinction between a "web page" service worker and an "extension" service worker only describes how the service worker is deployed *in that context*; the underlying service worker platform remains unchanged.

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
#### Single Occupancy {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

For both service worker [contexts]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8}, the browser will only ever instantiate a single service worker per script. When installing updates to service []{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB173 .pagebreak aria-label="173" role="doc-pagebreak" title="173"}workers, the browser will carefully swap out old service workers for new ones while ensuring there is only ever a single service worker active at any given time. This is critically important in both contexts: multiple service workers would cause complete chaos with caching, network interception, and event handling.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
#### Initialization and Lifecycle {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

A service [worker]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9} is initialized once when the extension is first installed or when a new version is deployed. After that, it may go idle and be terminated by the browser, then re-executed later in response to events. Each time the script runs, the browser re-evaluates it from the top.

While the meaning of "installation" differs slightly between web pages and extensions, the behavior of installation-related event handlers is the same. Developers should treat the onInstalled event as a one-time [opportunity]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10} to run setup code that should not be repeated. All other code in the service worker should assume it may run multiple times across the extension's lifecycle.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
#### Top-Level Event Handling {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

Because the service [worker]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11} is expected to undergo a repetitive cycle of becoming idle, being terminated, and being re-activated, service worker script structures must account for this. Events that are fired by the browser and passed to the service worker must have handlers added in the first turn of the event loop -- otherwise, there is a risk of missing the event. The reason for this is that service workers are often woken in direct response to an event being fired. The browser will wake the service worker, execute one turn of the event loop, and then fire the queued event in the service worker. If the handler for that event is not present at the end of that turn, the event may pass through unhandled.
:::

::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
#### Async Messaging {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par22 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB174 .pagebreak aria-label="174" role="doc-pagebreak" title="174"}Service [workers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12} must use a form of asynchronous messaging to communicate with other parts of the browser.

::: UnorderedList
-   For web page service workers, this takes the form of [postMessage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or the MessageChannel API.

-   For browser extensions, this takes the form of [runtime.sendMessage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [tabs.sendMessage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [runtime.connect()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, or [tabs.connect()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::
:::::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
### Service Worker Differences {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

Although web page service workers and extension service workers use the same underlying infrastructure, they differ in a handful of important ways.

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
#### Registration {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

Web page service [workers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13} must be registered from a page-level script. An example of this is shown below:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par27 .Para}
*Example web page service worker* [*registration*]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14}

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
if (\'serviceWorker\' in navigator) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **navigator.serviceWorker.register(\'/sw.js\')**
:::

::: FixedLine
  .then((registration) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    registration.addEventListener(\'updatefound\', () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      console.log(\'A new service worker is being installed:\');
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::

::: FixedLine
  })
:::

::: FixedLine
  .catch((error) =\> {
:::
:::::::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.error(\`Service worker registration failed: \${error}\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
} else {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.error(\'Service workers are not supported.\');
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB175 .pagebreak aria-label="175" role="doc-pagebreak" title="175"}For extension service [workers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}, the only step needed to register a service worker is to specify the [background.service_worker]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} script in the manifest. Web page service worker registration includes the [installing/waiting/active]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} states that are not meaningful for extension service workers.

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

For additional information on web page service workers, refer to [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Service_Worker_API]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Service_Worker_API)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
#### Purpose {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

The most important difference between web page service workers and extension service [workers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16} is their primary purpose. Web page service workers are equipped to behave as a cache, and this is their most common use case. They're able to conditionally intercept network requests and return cached content. Service workers can also be used to build [Progressive Web Applications (PWAs)]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17}, web pages that emulate app behavior with installation, offline capabilities, and server-sent push notifications.

Because browser extension scripts are assets and all served from the browser, there is no longer a need to cache these resources, as they are not being loaded from a remote server. Furthermore, extension UIs like popup pages are perfectly capable of operating normally offline without the use of a background service worker. Instead, extension service [workers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18} are primarily tasked with handling the wide range of events that are fired by the browser and the WebExtensions API.

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB176 .pagebreak aria-label="176" role="doc-pagebreak" title="176"}For the remainder of this chapter, an extension service worker will be simply referred to as a "service worker."
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
## Manifest V2 vs. Manifest V3 {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

Manifest V3 transformed [background scripts]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19} considerably. Manifest V2 background scripts were treated as *background pages*: JavaScript that executed in a headless web page. These background pages were also capable of running indefinitely, meaning they were suitable for managing long-running operations and web requests. In Manifest V3, [background scripts]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20} now execute as *service workers*. These workers are more lightweight, but they are diminished in a number of important ways.

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
### Scripts vs. Service Workers {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

In Manifest V2, the manifest [background]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22} value could be passed an array of scripts:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par35 .Para}
*Manifest V2 manifest.json*

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 2,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"scripts\": \[\"bg1.js\", \"bg2.js\", \"bg3.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB177 .pagebreak aria-label="177" role="doc-pagebreak" title="177"}These [scripts]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24} were loaded in the order they appeared in the array, and all executed inside the same background page. In Manifest V3, the background script is a single service worker:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par37 .Para}
*Manifest V3 manifest.json*

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"bg.js\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
### JavaScript Imports {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

In Manifest [V2]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26}, the only way to use a JavaScript [import]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} was to use the [background.page]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property and to use script tags in the background page HTML:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par39 .Para}
*manifest.json enabling a background HTML page*[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 2,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"page\": \"bg.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par40 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB178 .pagebreak aria-label="178" role="doc-pagebreak" title="178"}*bg.html*

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<!\-- modules allowed in this JS file \--\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\<script type=\"module\" src=\"bg.js\"\>\</script\>**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

In Manifest [V3]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30}, you can simply set the [background.type]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property to [\"module,\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} shown here:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par42 .Para}
*Manifest V3 manifest.json*[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32} *allowing for the import keyword*

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"bg.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"type\": \"module\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
