:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
### No Access to DOM and Limited Global APIs {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par43 .Para}
In Manifest V2, background [pages]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33} are effectively headless web pages with full access to a DOM and a web page global object. In Manifest V3, service workers have no access to a DOM and the global object is the [ServiceWorkerGlobalScope]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, which is missing many of the APIs available in the web page global object. This has some important implications:[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB179 .pagebreak aria-label="179" role="doc-pagebreak" title="179"}

::: UnorderedList
-   **No access to the document object.** The DOM and all its associated methods are no longer available. This also means the background service worker can no longer create and cache assets.

-   **No ability to render content for display in other contexts.** Rendering content in the headless page was useful in some situations but is no longer possible. Partial workarounds include using a third-party library like [jsdom]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or using the [OffscreenCanvas]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API.

-   **No access to the window object.** This [means]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34} that methods like [window.open()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} are no longer available.

-   **No ability to play or capture media directly in a service worker.** Doing so in Manifest V3 requires a host active content script or an extension view to grant access to the media APIs.

-   **No access to localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies.**

-   **No ability to use XMLHttpRequest**[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35}**.** [fetch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} should be used.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

In place of background DOMs, the Offscreen Document API (Chromium browsers only) can be used to address some of these limitations. Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details on how to use this API.

Losing access to the window object is especially problematic when attempting to authenticate from the service worker, as third-party authentication libraries frequently rely on opening a new window for the OAuth flow. Refer to Chapter [[[11]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more coverage on this topic.
::::
:::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
### Nonpersistent {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par52 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB180 .pagebreak aria-label="180" role="doc-pagebreak" title="180"}Manifest [V2]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37} allowed for background pages to be "persistent," which effectively meant they would run indefinitely as long as the host browser program was open. In Manifest V3, this ability is removed. This has several implications:

::: UnorderedList
-   [setTimeout()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} **and** [setInterval()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} **will no longer reliably execute.** These methods are still available. However, if the service worker terminates before the scheduled function executes, it will silently be skipped. The [chrome.alarms]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API replaces some of this functionality, but it cannot be used for time intervals less than a minute.

-   **Long-running queries and websockets may be killed.** The service worker will ignore [open]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39} network connections when determining if a service worker is idle, meaning that these connections may be severed if the service worker terminates while the connections are still active.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

There are some tricks later on in this chapter that can extend the life of a service worker.
::::
:::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
### No Shutdown Event {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

Manifest [V2]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41} allowed the developer to add handler for [runtime.onSuspend]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [runtime.onSuspendCanceled]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} events. This gave the extension opportunity to do some clean up. In Manifest V3, service workers have []{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB181 .pagebreak aria-label="181" role="doc-pagebreak" title="181"}no handler for a shutdown event, meaning that service worker scripts must eagerly perform tasks with the expectation that shutdowns can unexpectedly occur.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec16Heading"}
### No Programmatic Background Access {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16Heading .Heading}

In Manifest [V2]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42}, it was possible to access the background window [object]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43} from an extension interface in the foreground using [chrome.extension.getBackgroundPage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. In Manifest V3, this ability was removed.
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec17Heading"}
## Working with Background Scripts {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par58 .Para}
Let's begin by creating a simple browser extension with a background script. This is accomplished by defining the [background.service_worker]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property in the manifest:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"background\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"service_worker\": \"background.js\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB182 .pagebreak aria-label="182" role="doc-pagebreak" title="182"}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Initialized background script!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener((details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (details.reason ===
:::

::: FixedLine
      chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.INSTALL) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.log(\"Installed background script!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::

This simple background script logs to the console, sets an install handler, and exits. When the [install]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} event fires, the handler logs to the console.

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec18Heading"}
### Inspecting Background Service Workers {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par60 .Para}
After installing this extension in your browser, your extension management page will show a new card for the [extension]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46} that includes a *service worker* link (Figure [[6-1](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB183 .pagebreak aria-label="183" role="doc-pagebreak" title="183"}

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_6_Chapter/534317_2_En_6_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome Extensions page showing one extension named &quot;MVX&quot; version 0.0.1. The extension ID is &quot;pjnneomfgnleghpjhcegfnamkolgcagh.&quot; Options to &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update&quot; are visible at the top. The extension card includes buttons for &quot;Details&quot; and &quot;Remove,&quot; and a toggle switch is set to on. The page is in Developer mode." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 6-1</span>
<p>The installed extension with background service <span id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47">worker</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par61 .Para}
You can inspect the background script by clicking the *service worker* link (Figure [[6-2](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Fig2)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB184 .pagebreak aria-label="184" role="doc-pagebreak" title="184"}

<figure id="Fig2" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_MO2" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_6_Chapter/534317_2_En_6_Fig2_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser&#39;s DevTools console displaying two log messages: &quot;Initialized background script!&quot; and &quot;Installed background script!&quot; Each message is linked to &quot;background.js&quot; with line numbers 1 and 4, respectively. The interface includes options for filtering and setting log levels, with no issues reported." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 6-2</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48">console output</span> of the background service worker</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This devtools interface is very useful for inspecting and debugging your service worker, but keep in mind that leaving this window open will prevent the browser from suspending the service worker. Unintentionally leaving a devtools window open can cause unexpected extension behavior when attempting to reload or debug background scripts.
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par63 .Para}
To view the state of all service workers in the browser without interfering with their normal lifecycle, navigate your browser to [chrome://serviceworker-internals/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, shown in Figure [[6-3](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Fig3)]{.InternalRef}.[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB185 .pagebreak aria-label="185" role="doc-pagebreak" title="185"}

<figure id="Fig3" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_MO3" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_6_Chapter/534317_2_En_6_Fig3_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome ServiceWorker internals page. It shows a registration entry for a service worker with details such as scope, origin, and top-level site, all linked to a specific Chrome extension. The installation status is activated, and the running status is indicated as running. Additional information includes fetch handler existence, script path, version ID, and renderer process ID. Options to stop, inspect, or unregister the service worker are available." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 6-3</span>
<p><span id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49">Chrome’s service worker internals page</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar7 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This page will show *all* service workers your browser has installed -- not just for extensions. Recall that the browser does not make a distinction between a web page service worker and an extension service worker.
::::
::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec19Heading"}
### Service Worker Errors {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar8 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB186 .pagebreak aria-label="186" role="doc-pagebreak" title="186"}For this section, you must begin with the extension installed as shown in Example 6-1 *and* all devtools windows closed.
::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par66 .Para}
Modify the background [script]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51} from Example 6-1 as shown here:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Initialized background script!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener((object) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Installed background script!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
**throw new Error(\"foo\");**
:::
::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-2]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par67 .Para}
Once the background script is saved, click the reload button in the extension card. You will see the service worker immediately becomes inactive and an *Errors* button appears (Figure [[6-4](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Fig4)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB187 .pagebreak aria-label="187" role="doc-pagebreak" title="187"}

<figure id="Fig4" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_MO4" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_6_Chapter/534317_2_En_6_Fig4_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome Extensions page showing an extension named &quot;MVX&quot; version 0.0.1. The extension card displays its ID and a link to inspect views with &quot;service worker (Inactive)&quot; status. Options to &quot;Details,&quot; &quot;Remove,&quot; and &quot;Errors&quot; are visible. The page includes buttons for &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update,&quot; with &quot;Developer mode&quot; toggled on." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 6-4</span>
<p>The extension <span id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52">Errors</span><span id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53"></span> button appears</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par68 .Para}
Click the Errors [button]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55} to reveal the error view (Figure [[6-5](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Fig5)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB188 .pagebreak aria-label="188" role="doc-pagebreak" title="188"}

<figure id="Fig5" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_MO5" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_6_Chapter/534317_2_En_6_Fig5_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Chrome browser window displaying the Extensions page in Developer mode. The page shows an error message: &quot;Service worker registration failed. Status code: 15.&quot; Below, a JSON code snippet is visible, highlighting &quot;background.js&quot; in yellow. The JSON includes fields like &quot;name,&quot; &quot;version,&quot; &quot;manifest_version,&quot; and &quot;background.&quot; An &quot;Uncaught Error: foo&quot; message is also present. The interface includes buttons for &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 6-5</span>
<p>The extension error view</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

You will see the expected "foo" [error]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57} readout at the bottom, but also that the browser is telling us that the service worker failed to register. This is very important: **when a service worker throws an error in the first turn of the event loop, the service worker will fail to register. This means the extension will not install or ever operate.**

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar9 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The [errors page]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58} is especially useful in situations where the background script fails to register. When registration fails, since the service worker does not exist, you will not be able to inspect the error output of the service worker. Instead, the errors page will contain the error readout to assist in debugging.
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec20Heading"}
### Service Worker Termination {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar10 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB189 .pagebreak aria-label="189" role="doc-pagebreak" title="189"}For this section, you must keep all devtools windows closed to allow the service worker to become idle.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par72 .Para}
Install the following [extension]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59}:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"action\": {},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"background\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"service_worker\": \"background.js\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-3a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Initialized background script!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener((object) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Installed background script!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.action.onClicked.addListener(() =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **console.log(\"Clicked toolbar icon!\");**
:::

::: FixedLine
**});**
:::

::: FixedLine
**let elapsed = 0;**
:::

::: FixedLine
**setInterval(() =\> console.log(\`\${++elapsed}s\`), 1000);**
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-3b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB190 .pagebreak aria-label="190" role="doc-pagebreak" title="190"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

This background [script]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63} will log to the console every 1000 ms until the service worker is terminated. (Recall that [setInterval()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} will not prevent a service worker from becoming idle.) It will also log to the console whenever the toolbar icon is clicked.

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par74 .Para}
After reloading the extension, open the [chrome://serviceworker-internals]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} page to observe the log output. The [browser]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm64} sees that this extension is not actively doing work and therefore is considered to be *idle*. After approximately 30 seconds, you will see the browser stop the service worker (Figure [[6-6](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Fig6)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig6" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_MO6" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_6_Chapter/534317_2_En_6_Fig6_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome service worker internals page. It displays details about a service worker registration, including scope, storage key, origin, and top-level site. The status shows the service worker is activated but not running. Additional information includes registration ID, navigation preload settings, script source URL, and log messages. The interface is part of the Chrome browser, with tabs and address bar visible." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 6-6</span>
<p>The browser stops the service worker after being idle for 30 seconds</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par75 .Para}
The extension management [page]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm65}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm66} will also reflect this (Figure [[6-7](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Fig7)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB191 .pagebreak aria-label="191" role="doc-pagebreak" title="191"}

<figure id="Fig7" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_MO7" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_6_Chapter/534317_2_En_6_Fig7_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome Extensions page showing an extension named &quot;MVX&quot; version 0.0.1. The extension ID is &quot;pjnneomfgnleghpjhcegfnamkolgcagh.&quot; Options to &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update&quot; are visible at the top. The extension card includes buttons for &quot;Details&quot; and &quot;Remove,&quot; and a toggle switch is set to active. The page is in Developer mode." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 6-7</span>
<p>The inactive service <span id="534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm67">worker</span> as shown on the extension management page</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

Because you set a handler for the [action.onClicked]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the [browser]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm68} will wake the service worker, re-run the script, and restart the counter once you click the toolbar icon.

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar11 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Carefully observe the console output after clicking the toolbar icon button. You will notice that the global state in the service worker has been reset.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec21Heading"}
## Common Patterns {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB192 .pagebreak aria-label="192" role="doc-pagebreak" title="192"}In this section, we will explore a handful of patterns used in background service workers.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec22Heading"}
### Event Handler {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par79 .Para}
The WebExtensions [API]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70} allows the service worker to listen for a huge range of different browser events. Since the API is accessible in content scripts, extension UIs, and background scripts, these events can technically be handled in any of these places. However, since content scripts and extension UIs are ephemeral, only the background script *guarantees* that the event will be handled. The following example extension sets up a background script to handle a myriad of different API events:

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC14 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"alarms\", \"tabs\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"commands\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"foobar\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"suggested_key\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
        \"default\": \"Ctrl+Shift+J\",
:::

::: FixedLine
        \"mac\": \"MacCtrl+Shift+J\"
:::

::: FixedLine
      },
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"description\": \"Perform foobar action\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-4a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB193 .pagebreak aria-label="193" role="doc-pagebreak" title="193"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC15 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Handles extension install
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener((details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (details.reason ===
:::

::: FixedLine
      chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.INSTALL) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.log(\"Extension installed!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Handles alarm every minute
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.alarms.create(\"My alarm\", { periodInMinutes: 1 });
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.alarms.onAlarm.addListener((alarmInfo) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\`Alarm fired: \${alarmInfo.name}\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Handles tab state changes
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.tabs.onUpdated.addListener(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Tabs updated\");
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Handles when Ctrl+Shift+J is typed
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.commands.onCommand.addListener((command) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\`Command: \${command}\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Handles a click on the toolbar icon
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\`Clicked toolbar icon\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-4b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm71}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB194 .pagebreak aria-label="194" role="doc-pagebreak" title="194"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

Of course, this code could be transplanted into a popup script as is. However, the events would *only* be handled if the popup was open. Placing handlers in the background script guarantees that the handlers will execute.

If the service [worker]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74} is terminated, the browser will restart the service worker, run one turn of the event loop to set all the handlers, and only then will it fire all the events inside the service worker. In this way, you can guarantee event handling by assigning handlers at the top level.
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec23Heading"}
### Secret Management and Authentication {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par82 .Para}
Extensions frequently need to manage [authentication]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76} and secret values apart from untrusted web pages. This is especially true in situations where a content script needs to communicate with a server. Sending the request from the content script will usually not work, as the content script's network requests are still bound by the host page's cross-origin restrictions. Furthermore, collecting credentials or storing the authentication secrets in the content script is a *terrible* idea, as the page's DOM and storage APIs are shared by the host page:

::: UnorderedList
-   If a content script were to place a [JSON Web Token (JWT)]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77} into [localStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the host page's JavaScript could access that JWT.

-   If a content script collects credentials in [\<input\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} tags in the host page, the host page is capable of reading the values out of those HTML elements.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par85 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB195 .pagebreak aria-label="195" role="doc-pagebreak" title="195"}The following is an example of a secure authentication flow:

::: OrderedList
1.  ::: ItemNumber
    1\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Collect the user's credentials in a trusted extension UI, such as a popup.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

2.  ::: ItemNumber
    2\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Authenticate using the credentials provided inside the trusted page.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

3.  ::: ItemNumber
    3\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Store the authentication token with the [chrome.storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par89 .Para}
After performing these steps, your background service worker can access this authentication token and send authenticated requests to the remote server. A [content script]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm78} can indirectly send authenticated requests via a message relay:

::: OrderedList
1.  ::: ItemNumber
    1\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Content script]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm79} uses extension message API to send a message to the background indicating the type of authenticated request it wishes to send.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

2.  ::: ItemNumber
    2\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Background script]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm80} accesses the authentication token from storage and sends the authenticated request.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

3.  ::: ItemNumber
    3\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    When the request returns, the background script collects the response and sends it back up to the requesting content script.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

4.  ::: ItemNumber
    4\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    [Content script]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm81} receives the response payload via the extension message API.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par94 .Para}
This strategy is advantageous for several reasons:

::: UnorderedList
-   The content script never has to collect credentials.

-   The [content script]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm82} never has to store secrets.

-   The content script is no longer bound by the host page's cross-origin restrictions.
:::
::::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec24Heading"}
### Message Hub {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par98 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB196 .pagebreak aria-label="196" role="doc-pagebreak" title="196"}Any piece of an [extension]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm83}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm84} can use the extension messaging API, but the background script is the only entity that guarantees the message will be handled. Furthermore, it also guarantees that the message will be handled exactly once. In this way, it is often useful for an array of content scripts to all connect to a single background service worker and use it as a message hub. The following example extension will create a message port on each page that is opened and repeatedly send [messages]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm85}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm86} to the background. Install the extension, open several tabs of [https://example.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and inspect the logs of the pages and the background service worker to observe the background independently communicating with each page.

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC16 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"css\": \[\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-5a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm87}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm88}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB197 .pagebreak aria-label="197" role="doc-pagebreak" title="197"}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC17 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Messages include information about the sender
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener**((request, sender, sendResponse) =\> {
:::
:::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// This handler will fire for all runtime.sendMessage payloads.
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Use a value in the payload to filter.
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (request.type === \"getCurrentTabId\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Send the tab\'s ID back to the content script
:::

::: FixedLine
    **sendResponse**({ currentTabId: sender.tab.id });
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::

::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Fires when the content script calls runtime.connect()
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onConnect.addListener**((port) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\`Connected to \${port.name}\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Messages sent from the content script
:::

::: FixedLine
  **port.onMessage.addListener**((msg) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.log(port.name, msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Subtract 1 and send value back up to content script
:::

::: FixedLine
    port.postMessage({ value: msg.value - 1 });
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-5b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm89}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm90}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC18 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function initializeCountdown(currentTabId) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // This will fire the runtime.onConnect event
:::

::: FixedLine
  // in the background
:::

::: FixedLine
  const port = chrome.runtime.connect({
:::

::: FixedLine
    name: \`Tab \${currentTabId}\`,
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
:::::::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  // Messages sent from the service worker
:::

::: FixedLine
  **port.onMessage.addListener**((msg) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.log(port.name, msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Keep passing the value to the background while \> 0
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (msg.value \> 0) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      port.postMessage({ value: msg.value });
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  // Start a countdown sequence every 1000ms
:::

::: FixedLine
  setInterval(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    const value = Math.floor(Math.random() \* 10) + 1;
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.log(\`New countdown sequence: \${value}\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Send the inital postMessage in the sequence
:::

::: FixedLine
    **port.postMessage**({ value });
:::

::: FixedLine
  }, 1000);
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::

::: FixedLine
// Send a call/response message to the background
:::

::: FixedLine
// to determine current tab\'s ID
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.sendMessage**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Providing a type allows the background to filter
:::

::: FixedLine
  // incoming messages
:::

::: FixedLine
  { type: \"getCurrentTabId\" },
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Background can reply to this message with the tab ID
:::

::: FixedLine
  (response) =\> initializeCountdown(response.currentTabId)
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::
::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-5c]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm91}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm92}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB198 .pagebreak aria-label="198" role="doc-pagebreak" title="198"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec25Heading"}
### Storage Manager {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25Heading .Heading}

For [extensions]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm93}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm94} that need to store large amounts of structured data, the background service worker can make use of IndexedDB. Popups, options pages, and content scripts can all use the messaging API to read and write []{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB199 .pagebreak aria-label="199" role="doc-pagebreak" title="199"}to IndexedDB. The advantage of using the background as the [IndexedDB manager]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm95} is versioning: the browser guarantees one background service worker per extension at any time, so any IndexedDB [migrations]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm96}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm97} you need to run can safely execute inside the service worker without risking a versioning conflict.
:::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec26Heading"}
### Injecting Scripts {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par100 .Para}
Background service workers can programmatically [inject content scripts]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm98}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm99} into the page. This is very useful when you need to inject a content script conditionally or asynchronously. The following example injects a small content script when the toolbar icon button is clicked:

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC19 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"activeTab\", \"scripting\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-6a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC20 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function wipeOutPage() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  document.body.innerHTML = \"\";
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener((tab) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.scripting.executeScript({**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **target: { tabId: tab.id },**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **func: wipeOutPage,**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-6b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm100}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm101}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB200 .pagebreak aria-label="200" role="doc-pagebreak" title="200"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_FPar12 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[8]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on this.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec27Heading"}
### Sniffing Web Traffic {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par102 .Para}
The background service [worker]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm102}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm103} can track the pages the user is visiting and conditionally perform logic based on the details of that page. The following example logs the URL on every page visited and logs a special message only on a specific URL:

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC21 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"webNavigation\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-7a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm104}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm105}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB201 .pagebreak aria-label="201" role="doc-pagebreak" title="201"}

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC22 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const filter = {
:::

::: FixedLine
  url: \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      urlMatches: \"https://www.example.com/\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
};
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.webNavigation.onCompleted.addListener(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Visited the special site!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
}, filter);
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.webNavigation.onDOMContentLoaded.addListener((details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\`Loaded \${details.url}!\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-7b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec28Heading"}
### Installed/Updated Events {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par103 .Para}
[Developers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm106}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm107} often need to run a piece of code only when the extension is updated or installed. The [runtime.onInstalled]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [event]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm108} should be used for this purpose. It is capable of differentiating between install events and different update events via the [reason]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property. The following background script identifies when each install reason will execute:

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC23 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"This will run each time the service worker starts\");
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener((details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  switch (**details.reason**) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    case chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.INSTALL:
:::
:::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"This runs when the extension is newly installed.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
    case chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.CHROME_UPDATE:
:::
::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"This runs when a chrome update installs.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
    case chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.SHARED_MODULE_UPDATE:
:::
::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"This runs when a shared module update installs.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
    case chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.UPDATE:
:::
::::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"This runs when an extension update installs.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
    default:
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-8]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm109}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm110}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB202 .pagebreak aria-label="202" role="doc-pagebreak" title="202"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec29Heading"}
### Opening Tabs {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par104 .Para}
[Content scripts]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm111}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm112}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm113} are prevented from opening extension URLs, but they can delegate the opening to the background script. The following example adds two buttons to the page: one will fail to open the extension URL indirectly, and one sends a message to the background script to open the URL.

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC24 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"css\": \[\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"tabs\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-9a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm114}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm115}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB203 .pagebreak aria-label="203" role="doc-pagebreak" title="203"}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC25 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((msg) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  chrome.tabs.create({ url: msg.url });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-9b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC26 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const root = document.createElement(\"div\");
:::

::: FixedLine
root.innerHTML = \`
:::

::: FixedLine
\<div style=\"position:fixed;top:0;left:0\"\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<button id=\"direct-open\"\>THIS WILL NOT WORK\</button\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<button id=\"indirect-open\"\>THIS WILL WORK\</button\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</div\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
document.body.appendChild(root);
:::

::: FixedLine
const url = chrome.runtime.getURL(\"foobar.html\");
:::

::: FixedLine
document.querySelector(\"#direct-open\").addEventListener(\"click\", () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  window.open(url);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
document.querySelector(\"#indirect-open\").addEventListener(\"click\", () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ url });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-9c]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm116}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm117}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB204 .pagebreak aria-label="204" role="doc-pagebreak" title="204"}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC27 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>Foobar\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-9d]{.CaptionNumber}

[foobar.html]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm118}[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm119}
:::
::::
::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec30Heading"}
## Forcing Service Worker Persistence {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30Heading .Heading}

As described earlier in the chapter, service workers are designed to shut down frequently. For most extension [developers]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm120}, this is not an issue, as their needs can be adequately met by the service worker form factor. For the handful of situations where a persistent background script is *absolutely essential*, there are no officially supported remedies, but there are some unofficial strategies that can prolong the life of your service worker.

A [Chromium browser]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm121} will terminate a service worker when the browser decides the [service worker]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm122} is *inactive*. When this termination occurs, all active network requests are severed, all in-memory state in the service worker is disposed of, and all the service worker event handlers are destroyed.

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB205 .pagebreak aria-label="205" role="doc-pagebreak" title="205"}Precisely how the browser decides if a [service worker]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm123} is inactive is a function of multiple factors: time since recent network activity, time since last handled events, etc. In practice, service workers have a time to live (TTL) of 30 seconds. All forced persistence workarounds rely on the same overall strategy: trigger some service worker behavior inside this 30-second window that resets this 30 second timer. If the extension continually resets the TTL, the background service worker can theoretically remain active indefinitely.

The Chrome docs now officially recommend strategies for addressing this: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/develop/migrate/to-service-workers#keep_a_service_worker_alive_until_a_long-running_operation_is_finished]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/develop/migrate/to-service-workers#keep_a_service_worker_alive_until_a_long-running_operation_is_finished)]{.ExternalRef}. One of the strategies is adapted below, which uses a side effect of the [chrome.runtime.getPlatformInfo]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method to reset the service worker's TTL:

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par109 .Para}
::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PC28 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Function to keep the extension alive by periodically
:::

::: FixedLine
// calling chrome.runtime.getPlatformInfo every 20s
:::

::: FixedLine
const keepAlive = () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  return setInterval(chrome.runtime.getPlatformInfo, 20e3);
:::

::: FixedLine
};
:::
::::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Invoke keepAlive function when onStartup event fires
:::

::: FixedLine
// during browser startup or extension is re-enabled
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onStartup.addListener(keepAlive);
:::
::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Invoke the keepAlive function when the script is loaded
:::

::: FixedLine
// on service worker startup.
:::

::: FixedLine
keepAlive();
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 6-10]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_PB206 .pagebreak aria-label="206" role="doc-pagebreak" title="206"}
::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Par110 .Para}
To see this in action:

::: UnorderedList
-   Install the extension and ensure all devtools windows are closed.

-   Open the [chrome://serviceworker-internals]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} page. You will see the extension continue to log to the console indefinitely.
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec31Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you learned all about how extension code can run in the background in the browser. You were shown the similarities and differences between service workers that run in a web page vs. in an extension, as well as all the important changes between background scripts in Manifest V2 and V3. Next, we went through all the basics of inspecting and debugging background scripts. Finally, you were shown a variety of common patterns that developers use in background scripts, as well as a clever strategy for extending the lifetime of a service worker.

In the next chapter, you will learn about the primary user interface components of extensions: extension UIs.
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml}

::::: ChapterContextInformation
:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_b979-8-8688-1594-2_7 .ContextInformation}

:::

[M. Frisbie]{.ContextInformationAuthorEditorNames}[[Building Browser Extensions]{.BookTitle}]{.ContextInformationBookTitles}[<https://doi.org/10.1007/979-8-8688-1594-2_7>]{.ChapterDOI}
::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 7. Extension UIs {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB207 .pagebreak aria-label="207" role="doc-pagebreak" title="207"}A browser extension typically needs a [user interface (UI)]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1} to let users control its behavior. The most natural place for this is an *extension UI*[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2}, a special type of user interface served and managed by the extension. These UIs look and function like regular web pages but can display in different locations within the browser and have access to exclusive JavaScript APIs for interacting with the extension and the browser itself.

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The term *extension UI*[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3} can sometimes include content script UIs, or browser elements controlled via the WebExtensions API like context menus or omnibox. In this book, extension UI specifically refers to any extension component served as an HTML web page from the extension itself -- so content scripts and non-HTML elements are not included.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Introduction to Extension UIs {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

Extension UIs can take on different forms depending on their intended purpose. Some appear as popups attached to the browser's toolbar, while others manifest as full-page dashboards, sidebar panels, or embedded elements within web pages. Despite these variations, they all share the common trait of being directly managed by the extension, giving developers fine-grained control over their behavior, appearance, and interaction with the browser.

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par5 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB208 .pagebreak aria-label="208" role="doc-pagebreak" title="208"}To understand this better, it's important to distinguish between managed pages and extension UIs:

::: UnorderedList
-   [*Managed pages*]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4} are HTML pages served from an installed extension. They have access to WebExtensions APIs but are otherwise identical to regular web pages.

-   *Extension UIs* are special browser containers controlled by the extension. These are only allowed to load managed pages, and the containers have rules about how and when they appear.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par8 .Para}
Extension UIs come in different flavors:

::: UnorderedList
-   The [*popup UI*]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6} is a transient interface that opens with a user action, usually when the user clicks the extension's toolbar icon. It automatically closes when it loses focus, meaning users cannot interact with other browser content in the same window while keeping the popup open.

-   The [*options UI*]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8} can be opened when the user selects "Options" from various browser-controlled extension management menus. This UI can be rendered as either a stand-alone tab or an in-browser dialog overlay.

-   The [*side panel UI*]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10} is a persistent, resizable interface that remains open alongside the main web content. It does not interfere with page navigation and can update dynamically based on the currently active tab. The panel stays open until the user manually closes it.

-   *Devtools pages*[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12} integrate into the browser's Developer Tools and remain active as long as DevTools is open. These pages do not persist across sessions and are specific to the tab where DevTools is opened.
:::
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
### Managed Pages {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par13 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB209 .pagebreak aria-label="209" role="doc-pagebreak" title="209"}As the name suggests, a *managed page* is simply a page that is served and managed by an [extension]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14}. A special browser URL will send the request to the extension instead of out to the Internet, and any page the extension sends back will be granted special access to extension APIs. Let's start with the simplest possible managed page setup, an extension with just two files:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}
:::
::::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is a managed page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

[page.html]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16}
:::
::::
::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

There is nothing in the code explicitly connecting these two files; their only association is by virtue of mutual inclusion in the installed extension's file bundle. If a user installed this extension and knew the special extension origin, they could append the path to this file and visit that URL, and this managed page would render. For example, in the Chrome browser, the URL might be [chrome-extension://haboidkdkbnpiiggefkckbkgldkkdilm/page.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB210 .pagebreak aria-label="210" role="doc-pagebreak" title="210"}Every file included with an installed extension is accessible in the browser via its URL. However, web pages won't be able to access these files via URL unless explicitly granted access via the [web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} manifest key.
::::

The [browser]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17} understands that this HTML file is owned by the extension, and as a result, any JavaScript executed inside the page can access WebExtensions APIs that are not available to rank-and-file web pages. Furthermore, these managed pages are not restricted to render as browser tabs; they can be loaded inside special extension-specific rendering containers called extension UIs.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
#### Navigation Restrictions {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par17 .Para}
One important browser [behavior]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18} to note is that web pages are forbidden by the browser from opening extension URLs. Consider the extension in Example 7-2, which sends a function to the active page to attempt to open a managed page when the extension's toolbar button is clicked:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"activeTab\", \"scripting\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-2a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB211 .pagebreak aria-label="211" role="doc-pagebreak" title="211"}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is a managed page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-2b]{.CaptionNumber}

page.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener((tab) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (!tab?.id) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::
:::::::

:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  chrome.scripting
:::

::: FixedLine
    .executeScript({
:::

::: FixedLine
      target: { tabId: tab.id },
:::

::: FixedLine
      func: () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
        **// This will fail**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **window.open(chrome.runtime.getURL(\"page.html\"));**
:::

::: FixedLine
      },
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-2c]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This extension must be used with a valid active tab such as [[[[https://blank.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://blank.org)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par19 .Para}
After clicking the toolbar [button]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19}, an error page will be displayed (Figure [[7-1](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB212 .pagebreak aria-label="212" role="doc-pagebreak" title="212"}

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying an error message indicating that a page has been blocked by Chrome. The URL shown is &quot;chrome-extension:/haboidkdkbnpijiggefkckbkgldkkdilm/page.html&quot;. The error code is &quot;ERR_BLOCKED_BY_CLIENT&quot;. A &quot;Reload&quot; button is visible below the message." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-1</span>
<p>Error page displayed when page script attempts to navigate to extension <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20">URL</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

Although the entire process is controlled by the extension, the browser prevents a web page from opening a managed page in a new tab. This restriction exists for security [reasons]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21}: if web pages could freely open new tabs, they could abuse this ability to disrupt the user's browsing experience. To successfully open the extension page, the extension must use the [tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission and explicitly create the new tab.
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
### Popup UI {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par21 .Para}
The *popup UI* functions like a temporary browser tab without any browser chrome (URL bar, browser controls, etc.). It overlays the current web page, popping over the content when activated and disappearing when clicked away. An example popup is shown in Figure [[7-2](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig2)]{.InternalRef}.[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB213 .pagebreak aria-label="213" role="doc-pagebreak" title="213"}

<figure id="Fig2" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO2" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig2_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a webpage with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The page features a large, dashed border rectangle in the center with the text &quot;This is the popup page!&quot; inside. The browser interface includes standard navigation buttons and a bookmarks bar." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-2</span>
<p>A <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22">simple popup page</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
#### Popup UI Properties {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par22 .Para}
The *popup page*[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23} renders inside the popup UI. In [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24}, the popup page is declared by specifying the path to the popup HTML file via the [action.default_popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} value. As described in Chapter 5, the [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} dictionary defines how the toolbar icon [button]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25} appears and behaves:

::: UnorderedList
-   If the [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} dictionary is defined with a [default_popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} value, clicking that button will open the popup.

-   If the [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} dictionary is defined *without* a [default_popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} value, clicking the toolbar icon button will instead call the [chrome.action.onClicked()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} handler wherever it is defined.
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par25 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB214 .pagebreak aria-label="214" role="doc-pagebreak" title="214"}Example 7-3 demonstrates how to set up an extension with a popup UI triggered by the toolbar [button]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26}:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"action\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_popup\": \"page.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-3a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is the popup page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-3b]{.CaptionNumber}

page.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

The popup [container]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28} will be sized to fit the content of the popup page, but browsers will handle the sizing rules slightly differently. For example, Google Chrome sets no upper bound on the maximum size of the popup, allowing it to even expand larger than the browser window itself. Conversely, Mozilla Firefox limits the popup to be no larger than 800px by 600px.

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

To handle these variable popup size limits, you should either set a fixed size, make the popup page responsive, or limit the amount of content that appears inside the popup.
::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB215 .pagebreak aria-label="215" role="doc-pagebreak" title="215"}The [popup UI]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29} is not restricted from navigating to other extension URLs, but navigating inside the popup is discouraged as the user is not afforded the normal URL UI controls.
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
#### Opening and Closing Popup Pages {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par29 .Para}
The [popup UI]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30} is closed by default and displays only when triggered. There are three ways of opening the popup UI:

::: UnorderedList
-   Clicking the toolbar icon button when the manifest has [action.default_popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} defined

-   Entering the key shortcut defined in the manifest's [commands.\_execute_action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} when [action.default_popup]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is defined

-   Programmatically via [chrome.action.openPopup()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} after an eligible user interaction
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par33 .Para}
Once the popup UI is [open]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31}, there are several ways to close it. From popup page's perspective, closing the popup UI is identical to a tab being closed. The following actions will trigger a popup close:

::: UnorderedList
-   Clicking the toolbar icon button when the popup is already open

-   Clicking [outside]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32} the opened popup window *and* inside the same browser window

-   Calling [window.close()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} from inside the popup JavaScript

-   Using the [escape]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} key
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB216 .pagebreak aria-label="216" role="doc-pagebreak" title="216"}Importantly, clicks outside the browser will *not* close the popup. This behavior allows you to open one popup page per window. It also means you must assume the user can have multiple popup pages open simultaneously.
::::
:::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
#### Changing the Popup Page {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par39 .Para}
You can change the default popup [page]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33} via the WebExtensions API. Any changes made will persist through browser sessions. The following snippet demonstrates how to change the popup page:

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.setPopup({
:::

::: FixedLine
  popup: chrome.runtime.getURL(\"new-popup.html\")
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::
:::::::
::::::::
:::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
#### Usage Strategies {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par40 .Para}
When using the [popup UI]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34}, you should follow these strategies:

::: UnorderedList
-   Popup pages are best used for user interfaces that the user needs to access quickly without losing the context of the current page that they are on.

-   As some browsers will restrict the page dimensions of the popup page, a good rule of thumb is that the interface should contain approximately as much content as a phone app.

-   Because popup pages will be closed and re-opened quickly, the popup page should efficiently render and not rely on long-running operations that may need to terminate early.

-   []{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB217 .pagebreak aria-label="217" role="doc-pagebreak" title="217"}When you need the popup page to be stateful or load data, it should aggressively utilize a caching strategy to avoid any UX slowdowns.

-   In situations where you need more screen real [estate]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35}, a popup page can offer links or buttons that will open other extension views in a separate browser tab.

-   The [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36} is *particularly* effective when paired with the popup UI. After a user clicks the extension's toolbar button to open the popup, this permission is automatically granted, allowing the extension to execute scripts, modify content, or retrieve page details without requiring persistent host permissions. The access lasts only until the popup is closed or the user navigates away, ensuring security while enabling useful interactions such as extracting text, modifying [styles]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}, or injecting UI elements dynamically.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::
