Refer to Example 7-2 to see the [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38} in action, and refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on popup API methods.
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
### Options UI {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par48 .Para}
The [*options UI*]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39} displays a managed page in one of two ways: as a stand-alone tab or within a modal overlay on the browser's extension manager page. When the manifest defines a page under [options_ui]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the browser opens it whenever the user selects "Options" from any extension menu (Figures [[7-3](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig3)]{.InternalRef}, [[7-4](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig4)]{.InternalRef}, and [[7-5](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig5)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig3" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO3" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig3_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a blank page with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The browser&#39;s extension menu is open, showing options for an extension labeled &quot;MVX,&quot; including &quot;Options,&quot; &quot;Remove from Chrome,&quot; &quot;Unpin,&quot; &quot;Manage Extension,&quot; and &quot;View Web Permissions.&quot; The interface includes standard browser navigation elements." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-3</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40">browser-controlled extension options menu</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig4" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO4" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig4_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A screenshot of a Chrome browser window displaying the Extensions page. A pop-up titled &quot;MVX&quot; is in the foreground, with the text &quot;This is the options page!&quot; inside. The background shows various extension settings and options, including buttons for &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update.&quot; The browser&#39;s address bar contains a URL related to Chrome extensions." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-4</span>
<p>The modal options page rendered inside the <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41">extension management interface</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig5" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO5" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig5_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser window displaying a page with the title &quot;Options&quot; and a URL starting with &quot;chrome-extension:&quot;. The page contains the text &quot;This is the options page!&quot; centered on a white background. The browser tabs show &quot;blank&quot; and &quot;Options&quot;. The window has a dashed border." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-5</span>
<p>The options page rendered in a <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42">tab</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
#### Options UI Properties {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par49 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB220 .pagebreak aria-label="220" role="doc-pagebreak" title="220"}When opened in a [tab]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43}, an options page behaves like any other managed page. When opened as a modal, the options modal container can be thought of as a simple browser tab without any browser chrome: no URL bar, no browser controls, etc. The following demo extension demonstrates some basic properties of options pages:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"1.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"options_ui\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"open_in_tab\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"page\": \"page.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-4a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB221 .pagebreak aria-label="221" role="doc-pagebreak" title="221"}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is the options page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-4b]{.CaptionNumber}

page.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par50 .Para}
In [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the options [page]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44} is declared by specifying the path to the options HTML file via the [options_ui.page]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} value. The options page default rendering can occur in one of two ways:

::: UnorderedList
-   If [options_ui.open_in_tab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is [true]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the options page by default will open in a browser tab.

-   If [options_ui.open_in_tab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is [false]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the options page by default will open in a modal over the browser's extension manager page.
:::
::::

If opened as a [modal]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45}, the options container will be sized to fit the content of the options page. The options page can specify body dimensions to grow or shrink as needed.
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
#### Opening and Closing Options Pages {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par54 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB222 .pagebreak aria-label="222" role="doc-pagebreak" title="222"}There are several ways to open the options [page]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46}. The following actions will open the options page and will always respect the [options_ui.open_in_tab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} value:

::: UnorderedList
-   Right-clicking the toolbar icon button and selecting *Options*

-   Calling [chrome.runtime.openOptionsPage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par57 .Para}
When the options [page]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47} is opened in a tab, it closes like a standard web page. When opened as a modal, the following actions will trigger an options close:

::: UnorderedList
-   Clicking the X button inside the options page modal

-   Calling [window.close()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} from inside the options JavaScript

-   Using the [escape]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} key when viewing the options modal

-   Closing the extension manager tab
:::
::::
:::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
#### Usage Strategies {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par62 .Para}
When developing options [pages]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48}, you should follow these strategies:

::: UnorderedList
-   Options pages are best used for user interfaces that contain controls for how the extension behaves.

-   The options page has no restrictions on content. However, since a very common path to accessing it is to right-click on the toolbar icon and select "Options," the options page should probably resemble a page with which to control extension options.

-   []{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB223 .pagebreak aria-label="223" role="doc-pagebreak" title="223"}There isn't much of an upside to using the modal [version]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49} of the extension page. Users are likely not familiar with the extension management interface, so displaying a modal view over it could be confusing. Stick to the tab UI unless there is a very good reason not to.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar7 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on options API methods.
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
### Side Panel UI {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

The [*side panel UI*]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51} is a persistent, resizable pane attached to the side of the browser window, providing an always-accessible interface alongside the main web page (Figure [[7-6](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig6)]{.InternalRef}). Unlike a popup, which temporarily overlays the page and disappears when clicked away, the side panel remains visible.

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar8 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The side panel UI is only available in [Chromium browsers]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52}. Firefox extensions can use sidebars, which are similar.
::::

<figure id="Fig6" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO6" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig6_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a webpage with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The page is mostly empty, with a side panel on the right outlined by a dashed border. Inside the panel, the text reads &quot;This is the side panel page!&quot; The browser interface includes standard navigation and tab controls." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-6</span>
<p>The extension side panel opened alongside a <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53">web page</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
#### Side Panel Properties {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par69 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB224 .pagebreak aria-label="224" role="doc-pagebreak" title="224"}When opened, the side [panel]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54} behaves as a persistent, resizable pane attached to the browser window, allowing users to interact with an extension alongside their browsing experience. In Example 7-5, the extension defines a side panel that opens upon a toolbar button click:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"permissions\": \[\"sidePanel\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"side_panel\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_path\": \"page.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-5a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB225 .pagebreak aria-label="225" role="doc-pagebreak" title="225"}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>This is the side panel page!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-5b]{.CaptionNumber}

page.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.sidePanel.setPanelBehavior({**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **openPanelOnActionClick: true**
:::

::: FixedLine
**});**
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-5c]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

In [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the side panel page is defined using the side_panel.default_path key. Unlike the popup UI and options [UI]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56}, which are configured declaratively in the manifest, the side panel is mostly configured imperatively via the [chrome.sidePanel]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API. Side panel UI also requires the [sidePanel]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission.

Additionally, extensions can use the [chrome.sidePanel.setOptions()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [method]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57} to control side panel behavior dynamically, such as specifying which sites can display the panel or whether it should be automatically opened for certain pages.
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
#### Opening and Closing the Side Panel {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par72 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB226 .pagebreak aria-label="226" role="doc-pagebreak" title="226"}There are several ways to open the side [panel]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58}. The following actions will trigger the side panel to open:

::: UnorderedList
-   Manually opening the side panel via the browser side panel menu

-   Clicking the extension's toolbar button, if the extension has set up the side panel as the default action via [chrome.sidePanel.setPanelBehavior()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   Calling [chrome.sidePanel.open()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and passing a tab or window id
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par76 .Para}
Once opened, the side panel remains visible until one of the following events occurs:

::: UnorderedList
-   Clicking the X button in the side panel header

-   Navigating to a web page where the extension has not enabled its side panel

-   Manually hiding the side panel through the browser's UI controls

-   Switching to a different side panel
:::
::::

Clicking the X [button]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59} is the only action that fully closes and unloads the side panel page. All other actions will merely hide the panel while preserving its state in memory (analogous to a suspended browser tab).
:::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec16Heading"}
#### Usage Strategies {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par82 .Para}
When using the [side panel UI]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60}, you should follow these strategies:

::: UnorderedList
-   The side panel is best suited for persistent user interfaces that users may need to access while browsing, without interrupting their workflow.

-   []{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB227 .pagebreak aria-label="227" role="doc-pagebreak" title="227"}The side panel page should be geared to operate in a tall, narrow aspect ratio, as this is the default size of the side panel. Since users can resize the side panel, the UI should be responsive and adaptable to varying widths.

-   The [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61} does not apply to the side panel UI, meaning it does not automatically gain access to the current web page when opened via toolbar button click.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar9 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on side panel API methods.
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec17Heading"}
### DevTools UI {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17Heading .Heading}

The [Chrome developer tools (DevTools)]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63} interface is complicated and information dense, so it relies on a hierarchical tabbed interface to break up these views into discrete pieces. [Browser extensions]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm64} can augment this developer tools interface by adding additional tabs and panels containing extension UIs.

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec18Heading"}
#### DevTools UI Properties {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18Heading .Heading}

When integrated into Chrome DevTools, an extension [DevTools]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm65} page behaves like a dedicated debugging tool. It does not have standard browser chrome (URL bar, browser controls) and operates within the DevTools environment. This allows extensions to interact with inspected pages, monitor network [activity]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm66}, and execute debugging tasks.
:::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec19Heading"}
#### Creating a DevTools Page {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB228 .pagebreak aria-label="228" role="doc-pagebreak" title="228"}Compared to other flavors of extension UI, extensions use an unusual [strategy]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm67} for adding extra interfaces to the browser's developer tools. A browser extension has the option to define the [devtools_page]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property inside the manifest. The HTML page referenced will render as a headless page each time the browser's developer tools is opened. This headless page has a single purpose: it will load and execute scripts that use the DevTools API to insert additional interfaces.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par90 .Para}
The names can be confusing, so it's best to learn by example. Here is a simple extension that provides a devtools page that does nothing:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC14 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"devtools_page\": \"page.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-6a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC15 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"page.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-6b]{.CaptionNumber}

page.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC16 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Use this script to access the Devtools API
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-6c]{.CaptionNumber}

page.js
:::
::::
:::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB229 .pagebreak aria-label="229" role="doc-pagebreak" title="229"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

Don't worry about loading this [extension]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm68}, it doesn't do anything yet.

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar10 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The headless devtools page only runs each time the developer tools is opened. This means that when you make changes to the added panels, they will not re-render even if the extension is updated or the page is reloaded. When making updates to devtools pages, you must close and reopen the devtools interface or reload the updated devtools frames.
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec20Heading"}
#### Adding Panels and Sidebars {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20Heading .Heading}

[Browser extensions]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69} can add two types of interfaces to the developer tools: panels and sidebars. They appear in different places inside the developer tools, but otherwise they behave identically. Both panels and sidebars have access to the same limited subset of the WebExtensions [API]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm71} as content scripts. Additionally, they are allowed to access the DevTools API.

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar11 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Most browser extension interfaces like popups and options pages are created declaratively: the manifest specifies an HTML file path as an entrypoint, and the browser understands how to interpret that and automatically incorporate the new interface. Conversely, devtools pages are created imperatively: to create an interface, you must call methods from the DevTools API and pass in an HTML file path.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21 .section .Section4 .RenderAsSection4 aria-labelledby="Sec21Heading"}
##### Adding a Panel {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par95 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB230 .pagebreak aria-label="230" role="doc-pagebreak" title="230"}To add a new [panel]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72} to the developer tools, use the [chrome.devtools.panels.create()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method. Modify the previous example as follows:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC17 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>I\'m the panel!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-7a]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC18 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.devtools.panels.create(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"Demo DevTools\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"panel.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::
::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-7b]{.CaptionNumber}

page.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par96 .Para}
The [create()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [method]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74} takes a title, an icon URL (here it is left blank), and a panel HTML file. With this extension loaded, close and reopen the developer tools and look to your panel menu. You will notice there is a new option called "Demo DevTools" (Figure [[7-7](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig7)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB231 .pagebreak aria-label="231" role="doc-pagebreak" title="231"}

<figure id="Fig7" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO7" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig7_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a web browser displaying a blank page with developer tools open. The developer tools panel shows HTML and CSS code, including a `&lt;body&gt;` tag with attributes like `bgcolor` and `text`. The CSS section highlights styles such as `background-color` and `color`. A dropdown menu in the developer tools is open, showing options like &quot;Network&quot; and &quot;Performance,&quot; with &quot;Demo DevTools&quot; selected. The URL bar displays &quot;blank.org.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-7</span>
<p>The extension devtools panel available as an option in the Chrome DevTools menu</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par97 .Para}
Click this option to reveal the new [panel]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75} (Figure [[7-8](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig8)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB232 .pagebreak aria-label="232" role="doc-pagebreak" title="232"}

<figure id="Fig8" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO8" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig8_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a webpage with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The page is mostly empty except for a developer tools panel on the right side. Inside the panel, there is a message saying &quot;This is the panel page!&quot; in bold text. The developer tools show tabs like &quot;Elements,&quot; &quot;Console,&quot; and &quot;Demo DevTools,&quot; with the &quot;Console&quot; tab active." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-8</span>
<p>The newly added Demo DevTools panel <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76">revealed</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22 .section .Section4 .RenderAsSection4 aria-labelledby="Sec22Heading"}
##### Adding a Sidebar {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par98 .Para}
[Sidebars]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm78} are slightly different from panels. Whereas a panel is a top-level interface in the developer tools, sidebars are interfaces that live alongside one of several existing interfaces inside devtools. There are two interfaces that you can add sidebars to:

::: UnorderedList
-   Elements

-   Sources
:::
::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB233 .pagebreak aria-label="233" role="doc-pagebreak" title="233"}To add a [sidebar]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm79}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm80}, you must call [createSidebarPane()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} on the corresponding property for that interface: [elements]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [sources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. The callback for this method is passed the new sidebar pane as an argument, which you can use to add your custom page via the [setPage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par102 .Para}
The following example modifies the previous extension to instead add two sidebars: one to the elements interface and one to the sources interface:

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC19 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.devtools.panels.**sources.createSidebarPane**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"Demo Sources Sidebar\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  (sidebar) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **sidebar.setPage(\"sources_sidebar.html\");**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.devtools.panels.**elements.createSidebarPane**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"Demo Elements Sidebar\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  (sidebar) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **sidebar.setPage(\"elements_sidebar.html\");**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-8a]{.CaptionNumber}

page.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC20 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>I\'m the elements sidebar!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-8b]{.CaptionNumber}

elements_sidebar.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB234 .pagebreak aria-label="234" role="doc-pagebreak" title="234"}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC21 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>I\'m the sources sidebar!\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-8c]{.CaptionNumber}

sources_sidebar.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par103 .Para}
Install this [extension]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm81}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm82} and reopen the developer tools. In the *Elements* and *Sources* tabs, you will see a new child option (Figures [[7-9](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig9)]{.InternalRef}, [[7-10](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig10)]{.InternalRef}, [[7-11](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig11)]{.InternalRef}, and [[7-12](#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Fig12)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig9" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO9" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig9_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a blank webpage with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; is open. The developer tools panel is visible on the right, showing the &quot;Elements&quot; tab. HTML code is displayed, highlighting the body tag with styles such as background-color and color. A context menu is open, showing options like &quot;Properties&quot; and &quot;Accessibility,&quot; with &quot;Demo Elements Sidebar&quot; selected." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-9</span>
<p>Revealing the new elements sidebar <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm83">option</span><span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm84"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig10" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO10" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig10_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a blank webpage with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The right side shows the browser&#39;s developer tools, specifically the &quot;Sources&quot; tab. The &quot;Demo Sources Sidebar&quot; tooltip is visible, indicating a feature demonstration. The developer tools panel includes sections like &quot;Breakpoints&quot; and &quot;Call Stack,&quot; with options for &quot;Console&quot; and &quot;Elements&quot; tabs." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-10</span>
<p>Revealing the new sources sidebar <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm85">option</span><span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm86"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig11" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO11" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig11_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a web browser displaying a blank page with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The browser&#39;s developer tools are open, showing the &quot;Elements&quot; tab. The sidebar contains the text &quot;This is the elements sidebar!&quot; in bold. The console section below is visible with no specific content displayed." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-11</span>
<p>Viewing the new elements sidebar <span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm87">option</span><span id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm88"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig12" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_MO12" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_7_Chapter/534317_2_En_7_Fig12_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displaying a blank webpage with the URL &quot;blank.org&quot; in the address bar. The developer tools are open, showing the &quot;Sources&quot; tab. The sidebar contains a message in a dashed box stating, &quot;This is the sources sidebar!&quot; Various options like &quot;Breakpoints&quot; and &quot;Console&quot; are visible, with some checkboxes unchecked. The interface includes tabs for &quot;Elements,&quot; &quot;Console,&quot; and &quot;Sources.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 7-12</span>
<p>Viewing the new sources sidebar option</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar12 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB237 .pagebreak aria-label="237" role="doc-pagebreak" title="237"}The [panel and sidebar views]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm89} will re-render the HTML page each time the tab is opened. For example, if you were to click into your custom panel, click over to the Elements tab, and click back over to the original panel, it will have rendered two separate times. Keep this in mind should you need to preserve state.
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec23Heading"}
#### Usage Strategies {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par105 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB238 .pagebreak aria-label="238" role="doc-pagebreak" title="238"}When using the DevTools page UI, you should follow these [strategies]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm90}:

::: UnorderedList
-   The DevTools UI is best suited for developer-focused tools that interact with a page's underlying structure, such as inspecting elements, debugging network requests, or monitoring JavaScript execution. It is designed for use within the Chrome DevTools panel and is not intended for general user-facing interfaces.

-   DevTools pages and sidebars should be optimized for a wide, horizontally oriented layout, as it typically appears in a dockable panel alongside the browser's debugging tools. Since users can resize or undock the panel, the [UI]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm91} should be flexible and responsive to different aspect ratios.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_FPar13 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on devtools API methods.
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec24Heading"}
## Detecting Extension UI State {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24Heading .Heading}

[Browser extensions]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm92}[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm93} lack a unified way of determining which of their UI components are currently active. Unlike traditional web pages, extension UIs can exist in various forms, and no single API provides a complete list of all open extension-related views. The [chrome.tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [chrome.windows]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} APIs can locate any active extension UI tabs, but any extension UIs that are not tabs will not be found with this API.

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB239 .pagebreak aria-label="239" role="doc-pagebreak" title="239"}To reliably track all active [extension UIs]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm94}, the background script can broadcast a "check" message to all extension UIs. Any UI that is currently active will respond, allowing the background script to build a complete list of detected UIs. Example 7-9 is an implementation of this [approach]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm95}:

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Par111 .Para}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC22 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
let detectedUIs = new Map();
:::
::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function checkOpenExtensionUIs() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  detectedUIs.clear();
:::
:::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  chrome.runtime.sendMessage({
:::

::: FixedLine
    action: \"check-ui\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }).catch(() =\> \[\]);
:::
::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  // Allow enough time for all extension UIs to respond
:::

::: FixedLine
  await new Promise((resolve) =\> setTimeout(resolve, 500));
:::
:::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  return \[\...detectedUIs.entries()\];
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((message, sender) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (message.action === \"ui-alive\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Record the extension UI information
:::

::: FixedLine
    detectedUIs.set(message.pageId, sender);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
setInterval(
:::

::: FixedLine
  async () =\> console.log(await checkOpenExtensionUIs()),
:::

::: FixedLine
  1000
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-9a]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PB240 .pagebreak aria-label="240" role="doc-pagebreak" title="240"}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_PC23 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// On load, each page generates its own ID
:::

::: FixedLine
const pageId = crypto.randomUUID();
:::
:::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((message) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (message.action === \"check-ui\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
    chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ action: \"ui-alive\", pageId });
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 7-9b]{.CaptionNumber}

page.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec25Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_7_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you learned about extension UIs, which are critical building blocks of browser extensions. The chapter took you through the details of how each one operates inside the browser, how they can fit into the user flow, and the advantages and disadvantages of using each one.

In the next chapter, you will learn about how content scripts can complement and enhance extension UIs, as well as enable an entirely new extension domain in which to architect user interfaces.
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml}

::::: ChapterContextInformation
:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_b979-8-8688-1594-2_8 .ContextInformation}
::: ChapterCopyright
© The Author(s), under exclusive license to APress Media, LLC, part of Springer Nature 2025
:::

[M. Frisbie]{.ContextInformationAuthorEditorNames}[[Building Browser Extensions]{.BookTitle}]{.ContextInformationBookTitles}[<https://doi.org/10.1007/979-8-8688-1594-2_8>]{.ChapterDOI}
::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 8. Content Scripts {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB241 .pagebreak aria-label="241" role="doc-pagebreak" title="241"}Content scripts are one of the most powerful and flexible tools available in browser extensions. Extensions allow you to inject JavaScript and CSS into a web page. The injected content can be as simple as a few behavioral or visual tweaks, or as complicated as an entire single page application.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Introduction to Content Scripts {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

In the context of browser extensions, the term "content [script]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1}" refers to either JavaScript or CSS injected by an extension into a web page. The most common way that content scripts are used is by declarative injection via the manifest. The manifest specifies the files that should be injected and the domains in which they should be injected, and the browser will inject them into the page. Injected JavaScript or [CSS]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2} is analogous to (but *not* identical, as you'll learn later in the chapter) dynamically inserting a [\<script\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [\<link\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} tag into the page.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par4 .Para}
The best way to wrap your head around content scripts is to see them in action. The following example demonstrates a simple extension that will inject JS and [CSS]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3} content scripts into all web pages. Once injected, this JS and CSS renders a simple widget that is fixed in place over the web page:[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB242 .pagebreak aria-label="242" role="doc-pagebreak" title="242"}

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Content Script Widget Demo\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"content_scripts\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"css\": \[\"content-script.css\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
document.body.insertAdjacentHTML(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"beforeend\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \`\<div id=\"my-widget\"\>Hello, world!\</div\>\`
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::
:::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
window.foo = \"bar\";
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

content-script.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
#my-widget {
:::

::: FixedLine
  position: fixed;
:::

::: FixedLine
  top: 0;
:::

::: FixedLine
  left: 0;
:::

::: FixedLine
  width: 200px;
:::

::: FixedLine
  height: 200px;
:::

::: FixedLine
  background: white;
:::

::: FixedLine
  border: 2px dashed black;
:::

::: FixedLine
  display: grid;
:::

::: FixedLine
  place-items: center;
:::

::: FixedLine
  z-index: 10000;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-1c]{.CaptionNumber}

content-script.css
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB243 .pagebreak aria-label="243" role="doc-pagebreak" title="243"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par5 .Para}
[Load]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5} this extension into your browser; then navigate to any website to see the widget (Figure [[8-1](#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_8_Chapter/534317_2_En_8_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Wikipedia homepage in a web browser. The page features the Wikipedia logo, a globe composed of puzzle pieces with various characters. Below the logo, there are links to Wikipedia in different languages, each with the number of articles available. A search bar is present at the bottom. A pop-up box on the left displays the text &quot;Hello, world!&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 8-1</span>
<p>The content script widget rendering over the page</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par6 .Para}
Some [behavioral aspects]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6} to note:

::: UnorderedList
-   When you inspect the page, you will see the created widget HTML, but not your injected content scripts (as [\<link\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [\<style\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, or [\<script\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}). The content scripts are projected into the page by the browser but not added to the DOM.

-   []{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB244 .pagebreak aria-label="244" role="doc-pagebreak" title="244"}The widget will render slightly *after* the web page. The browser controls precisely when to inject the content scripts into the page, and the default behavior is to inject when the document is "idle". Read on to learn more about the script injection timing.

-   The content script JavaScript is [storing]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7} a value in the global object, but when you open the developer console and check for that value in the *top* script context, it is [undefined]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. The default behavior is to separate the content script JS runtime and the page JS runtime, but to share a DOM. Read on to learn about script isolation.

-   Test out [different]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8} places the content script might be injected: [iframes]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [[[[https://chromewebstore.google.com/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://chromewebstore.google.com/)]{.ExternalRef}, other extension pages, and [about:blank]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. You'll notice that the widget doesn't render on any of these. Read on to learn about script injection rules.
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
## WebExtensions API Access {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par11 .Para}
Unlike extension UIs, content script JavaScript can only access a subset of the WebExtensions [API]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9}:

::: UnorderedList
-   [chrome.dom.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.i18n.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.storage.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.connect]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.getManifest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.getURL]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.id]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.onConnect]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.onMessage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.sendMessage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::
::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB245 .pagebreak aria-label="245" role="doc-pagebreak" title="245"}Should you require other [parts]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10} of the API, you can delegate to the background service worker by sending a message to trigger a remote procedure call. The background service worker can use the full API and send back the result.

Although content scripts can use [runtime.getURL()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} to retrieve the URL of an extension file, they are prevented from directly opening tabs of extension URLs. The tab will [open]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11}, but the page will not render and instead show a browser error. Content scripts run in an untrusted environment, and therefore, using [\<a href\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [window.open(\"chrome-extension://\...\")]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} to access extension pages would necessarily mean that the host pages could open those URLs too. As shown in Chapter [[[6]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_6_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}, the solution to this is to send a message to the background directing it to [open]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12} a new tab with the desired URL.
:::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
## Injected JavaScript and CSS Behavior {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

JavaScript and CSS [injected]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13} by extensions have some idiosyncratic behavior to keep in mind.

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
### CSS Injection Specificity {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

You will be [fighting]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14} the host page styling. This means to override page [CSS]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}, you will either need to use [!important]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or increase the content script specificity.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
### JavaScript Injection Context {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB246 .pagebreak aria-label="246" role="doc-pagebreak" title="246"}Injected [JavaScript]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17} has no ability to directly obtain information about how it was injected. [document.currentScript]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [import.meta.url]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and any other script properties that might offer some information in other contexts are [null]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or will throw errors.
:::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
### Static Asset URLs {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

:::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par27 .Para}
When loading static [assets]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18} into the page from your extension (images, fonts, etc.), you will need to reference the ID of your extension in the URL, for example:

::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome-extension://**jnofdoejfipgalklopidpdeofjebihcf**/myfile.png
:::
::::
:::::
::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par28 .Para}
To avoid hard-coding the extension ID in the content script JS or [CSS]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19}, you have two options:

::: UnorderedList
-   In JavaScript, use [**chrome.runtime.getURL()**]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20} to generate the full URL. For example, you would call [chrome.runtime.getURL(\"myfile.png\")]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

-   In CSS, use the **\_\_MSG\_@@extension_id\_\_** [localization]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21} placeholder to direct the browser to automatically replace the value with the extension ID. For example, you could use [background-image:url(\'chrome-extension://\_\_MSG\_@@extension_id\_\_/myfile.png\')]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in your CSS file. (Note: [T]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}his requires the image to be listed under [web_accessible_resources.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional})
:::
::::
:::::::::
::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
## Specialized Content Script Properties {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB247 .pagebreak aria-label="247" role="doc-pagebreak" title="247"}Content scripts can be customized to control when they run, where they inject, and how they interact with web pages. Developers can set injection timing, choose between isolated or page contexts, define matching rules for special pages like [about:blank]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, filter URLs, and specify whether scripts inject in all frames or just the top-level frame.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
### Injection Timing {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

The run_at [property]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22} in the manifest defines when a content script is injected into a page. Choosing the right timing can impact how the script interacts with elements, loads resources, and avoids conflicts with other scripts.

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par33 .Para}
The [run_at property]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23} accepts three possible values: [document_start]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [document_end]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [document_idle]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

::: UnorderedList
-   **document_start**: The script is injected after any CSS files have loaded but before the DOM is constructed or any other script executes.

-   **document_end**: The script is injected as soon as the DOM is fully constructed but before subresources such as images and frames finish loading.

-   **document_idle**: The [browser]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24} determines the optimal moment to inject the script, occurring sometime between document_end and just after the [window.onload]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} event fires. The precise timing depends on the document's complexity and load time, ensuring efficient page performance. Scripts running at [document_idle]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} do not need to listen for [window.onload]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} []{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB248 .pagebreak aria-label="248" role="doc-pagebreak" title="248"}since they are guaranteed to execute after the DOM is complete. If a script must run strictly after [window.onload]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the extension can verify if the event has already fired using the [document.readyState]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property.
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par37 .Para}
The following [example]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25} runs a content script at each of the three injection points and logs information about the state of the page.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Injection Timing Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"run_at\": \"document_start\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"run_at\": \"document_end\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"run_at\": \"document_idle\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-2a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB249 .pagebreak aria-label="249" role="doc-pagebreak" title="249"}

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \`readyState: \${document.readyState}\`,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \`Elements: \${document.getElementsByTagName(\"\*\").length}\`
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-2b]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26}
:::
::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par38 .Para}
This is the example output on google.com:

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
readyState: loading Elements: 1
:::

::: FixedLine
readyState: interactive Elements: 414
:::

::: FixedLine
readyState: interactive Elements: 445
:::
::::::
:::::::
::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

Unless you have a good reason, inject CSS content scripts as early as possible to avoid visible styling flashes between the page\'s initial styles and the applied content script CSS.

**Note** A content script can only be injected *once per injection point*. The browser will automatically ignore duplicate injection of the same script in the same injection point.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
### Script Worlds {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

The world [property]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28} in the manifest specifies the execution environment in which the script runs. This setting affects how the script interacts with the page, including whether it has access to the page's JavaScript variables and functions or if it operates in an isolated scope.

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par42 .Para}
The [world]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property accepts two values:

::: UnorderedList
-   **ISOLATED**: (default) The script runs in an isolated JavaScript environment, preventing direct access to the page's global variables or functions.

-   []{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB250 .pagebreak aria-label="250" role="doc-pagebreak" title="250"}**MAIN**: The script runs in the same execution context as the page itself, allowing access to global variables and functions.
:::
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par45 .Para}
The following [example]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30} runs a content script in each world and inspects the global object to test for the presence of the [d3]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property.

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Script World Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"https://d3js.org/\*\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"js\": \[\"isolated-content-script.js\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"world\": \"ISOLATED\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"run_at\": \"document_idle\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"https://d3js.org/\*\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"js\": \[\"main-content-script.js\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"world\": \"MAIN\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"run_at\": \"document_idle\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-3a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"MAIN:\", \`d3: \${ typeof d3 }\`);
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-3b]{.CaptionNumber}

[main-content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32}
:::
::::
:::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB251 .pagebreak aria-label="251" role="doc-pagebreak" title="251"}

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"ISOLATED:\", \`d3: \${ typeof d3 }\`);
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-3c]{.CaptionNumber}

[isolated-content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33}
:::
::::
:::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par46 .Para}
Below is the [console]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34} output on [d3js.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, where the host page declares the d3 global. The [MAIN]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} world [script]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36} can see this global, but the [ISOLATED]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} world cannot.

:::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
MAIN: d3:**object**
:::

::: FixedLine
ISOLATED: d3:**undefined**
:::
:::::
::::::
:::::::

**Caution** Bear in mind, enabling MAIN world access is bidirectional: your extension\'s content script can view and manipulate the host page\'s JavaScript, but the host page is also able to view and manipulate the extension\'s JavaScript. Variables, extension APIs, and sensitive information can be exposed if you are not careful about what you inject.

An [ISOLATED]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} world content [script]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38} executes in a separate runtime in parallel to the host page. Neither runtime can access the other's variables, and each has its own event loop, global scope, and task queue. However, both can access APIs that interact with the same objects: for example, the DOM, [localStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [sessionStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [IndexedDB]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [cookieStore]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, etc. Suppose the host page defines a variable in the global scope. The content script has no way of accessing its value. However, if the host page were to write that variable into [localStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the content script's [localStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API would have access to that value!

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par49 .Para}
Shared access to the [DOM]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40} is particularly interesting. One especially useful aspect is shared events: all events fired on the DOM will be handled by both the page event listeners *and* the content script listeners. This is incredibly important for page automation: knowledge of the host page's []{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB252 .pagebreak aria-label="252" role="doc-pagebreak" title="252"}event listeners (which can be reverse-engineered with tools like Chrome's devtools [getEventListeners()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method) means you can [fully]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41} emulate user behavior:

::: UnorderedList
-   Any [events]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42} ([click]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [keydown]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, etc.) fired by a content script on the DOM will trigger host page handlers.

-   Reciprocally, any events fired by a host script or user interactions can be handled by content script handlers.

-   Content scripts can manually manipulate the page and form inputs with methods like [focus()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [blur()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [scrollTo()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par53 .Para}
[ISOLATED]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} content [scripts]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45} are also able to add, modify, or remove DOM nodes from the host page. For example, if you wanted to wipe out all the CSS styling on all web pages, you could use the following example extension:

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"css\": \[\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-4a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB253 .pagebreak aria-label="253" role="doc-pagebreak" title="253"}

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
document
:::

::: FixedLine
  .querySelectorAll(\"style, link\[rel=\'stylesheet\'\], \[style\]\")
:::

::: FixedLine
  .forEach((el) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (el.tagName === \"STYLE\" \|\| el.tagName === \"LINK\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
      el.remove();
:::

::: FixedLine
    } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
      el.removeAttribute(\"style\");
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-4b]{.CaptionNumber}

content-script.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

Load this [extension]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47} and navigate to any web page. You will notice that all the CSS styling will be completely stripped out.

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This will not affect CSS styling that is added by JavaScript after the content script runs. One solution might be to wrap this script in a [MutationObserver]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, but then there may be performance considerations to take into account with all the extra [querySelectorAll]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} expressions being evaluated. This sort of page management is what makes content scripts so tricky to get right.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
### Matching Rules {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par56 .Para}
Some less frequently used [properties]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48} control where and how a content script runs within a browser extension. Content scripts are injected into web pages based on specified URL patterns, with additional properties allowing for fine-tuned behavior. These properties help define not only which pages the script runs on but also how it interacts with different frames and special cases.[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB254 .pagebreak aria-label="254" role="doc-pagebreak" title="254"}

::: UnorderedList
-   **match_origin_as_fallback:** (defaults to [false]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}) [Determines]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49} whether the script should match cross-origin iframes when the main page's origin does not match the matches pattern.

-   **match_about_blank:** (defaults to [false]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}) Allows the script to execute in [about:blank]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [about:srcdoc]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} frames, which are otherwise ignored.

-   **all_frames:** (defaults to [false]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}) Determines whether the script should run in all frames of a page, including iframes, instead of just the top-level frame.

-   **exclude_matches:** Defines URL [patterns]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50} where the script should *not* run.

-   **include_globs:** Specifies glob patterns that refine the set of URLs where the script should run, offering more flexibility than simple matches patterns. A URL must match [include_globs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} *and* the [matches]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} value to have content scripts injected.

-   **exclude_globs:** Similar to [exclude_matches]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, but operates at the glob level, preventing execution on pages matching specific patterns.
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par63 .Para}
The following [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [snippet]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51} demonstrates how these might be used:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC14 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"https://www.google.com/\*\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"exclude_matches\": \[\"https://www.google.com/maps/\*\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"include_globs\": \[\"\*://\*.google.com/\*search\*\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"exclude_globs\": \[\"\*://\*.google.com/\*/news\*\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"match_origin_as_fallback\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"match_about_blank\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"all_frames\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::

::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB255 .pagebreak aria-label="255" role="doc-pagebreak" title="255"}Runs the content script on [[[[www.google.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](http://www.google.com)]{.ExternalRef} pages ([[[[https://www.google.com/\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://www.google.com/*)]{.ExternalRef})

-   :::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par65 .Para}
    Excludes execution on [[[[https://www.google.com/maps/\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://www.google.com/maps/*)]{.ExternalRef} (Google Maps)

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   Includes pages where the URL contains "search" (but only within [[[[www.google.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](http://www.google.com)]{.ExternalRef})

    -   Excludes pages where the URL contains [\"/news]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}"
    :::
    ::::

-   Allows [execution]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52} in cross-origin iframes only if the top-level page is outside the [matches]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} scope

-   Enables execution in [about:blank]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [about:srcdoc]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} frames (if their parent frame matches the script's "matches" pattern)

-   Runs in all [frames]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53}, including iframes, instead of just the top-level frame
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
## Stale Content Scripts {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

When an [extension]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54} is updated, whether locally or through the Chrome Web Store, content scripts already injected into active pages *are not automatically replaced*. They remain in memory and continue running []{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB256 .pagebreak aria-label="256" role="doc-pagebreak" title="256"}until the page is reloaded or navigated. This can be surprising for new developers, especially since extension UIs like popups automatically close after an update.

If the updated background scripts or messaging logic relies on newer content script behavior, this mismatch can lead to unexpected issues. Developers should account for this when testing or deploying updates and consider adding version checks or prompting users to [refresh]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55} affected tabs.
:::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
## Spoofed Network Requests {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

An important [ability]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56} of browser extensions is to send authenticated requests to a server on behalf of the user. This functionality allows extensions to retrieve or manipulate data as if they were the user themselves. However, how these requests are made depends heavily on the server's security policies, including CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57} restrictions and cookie handling policies.

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par74 .Para}
If the server does not enforce origin restrictions, meaning it either has a permissive [CORS policy]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58} ([Access-Control-Allow-Origin: \*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}) or explicitly allows requests from the extension's [origin]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59}, then you can communicate directly with the server from a background script using [fetch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. However, if the server blocks cross-origin requests or has strict cookie policies, then making authenticated requests directly from the extension's background script becomes problematic. The following scenarios will prevent background script access:

::: UnorderedList
-   The server enforces strict CORS rules ([Access-Control-Allow-Origin]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} only allows requests from its own domain).

-   []{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB257 .pagebreak aria-label="257" role="doc-pagebreak" title="257"}If the server [enforces]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60} [SameSite=Strict]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [SameSite=Lax]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} cookies, they will not be sent with cross-origin requests made from a background script.

-   If the server requires session-based authentication (e.g., cookies set via [HttpOnly]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}).
:::
::::

A common [solution]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61} is to send requests from the page itself via a content script. Since content scripts operate within the context of the page, they inherit its authentication state, including session cookies and other credentials that a user's browser would normally send. Page cookies automatically attach to requests made from a content script, and the request appears to originate from the same domain as the page (bypassing CORS restrictions).

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Some web applications implement CSRF protection by requiring a CSRF token in requests. If the token is embedded in the page [(\<meta\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} tag, [window]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} object, hidden form field, etc.), the content script will need to extract it dynamically before making a request.
::::
:::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
## Logging and Errors {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par80 .Para}
