Console messages and [errors]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62} produced by a content script will show up in the host page's developer console (Figure [[8-2](#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Fig2)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB258 .pagebreak aria-label="258" role="doc-pagebreak" title="258"}

<figure id="Fig2" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_MO2" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_8_Chapter/534317_2_En_8_Fig2_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a web browser displaying the Wikipedia homepage. The left side shows the main page with the Wikipedia logo and language options. A speech bubble with &quot;Hello, world!&quot; is overlaid. The right side features the browser&#39;s developer tools, open to the Console tab, showing an error message: &quot;Uncaught Error: I&#39;m a content script error&quot; with a link to &quot;content-script.js:8:7&quot;." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 8-2</span>
<p>Console messages and errors from a content script</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par81 .Para}
Because uncaught [errors]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63} thrown in the content script are still part of the extension context, these error messages will also surface in the extension's error view (Figure [[8-3](#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Fig3)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB259 .pagebreak aria-label="259" role="doc-pagebreak" title="259"}

<figure id="Fig3" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_MO3" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_8_Chapter/534317_2_En_8_Fig3_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Google Chrome Extensions page displaying an error message. The page shows a section titled &quot;Errors&quot; with a message stating &quot;Uncaught Error: I&#39;m a content script error.&quot; Below, there is a &quot;Context&quot; section with a URL and a &quot;Stack Trace&quot; section showing a code snippet. The code includes a function with a line highlighted in yellow that throws the error. The interface includes buttons for &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update,&quot; with a search bar at the top." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 8-3</span>
<p>Content script errors visible in the extension error view</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
:::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
## Page Automation {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par82 .Para}
Although a content [script]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm64}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm65} cannot access event handlers, it can dispatch events on shared DOM nodes. Event handlers that the host page assigned in the host JavaScript context will be called by events dispatched from the content script JavaScript context. Since the DOM is shared irrespective of the script world, any content script configuration is capable of triggering event handlers. The following example demonstrates this by automating a search on W[ikipedia.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC15 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"MVX\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"https://\*.wikipedia.org/\*\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"css\": \[\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-5a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB260 .pagebreak aria-label="260" role="doc-pagebreak" title="260"}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC16 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Wait a few seconds so the user
:::

::: FixedLine
// can see the query being entered
:::

::: FixedLine
setTimeout(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  document.querySelector(\"#searchInput\").value = \"javascript\";
:::

::: FixedLine
}, 2000);
:::

::: FixedLine
setTimeout(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  document.querySelector(\'button\[type=\"submit\"\]\').click();
:::

::: FixedLine
}, 3000);
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-5b]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm66}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm67}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

After loading this [extension]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm68}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69}, visit [[[[https://wikipedia.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://wikipedia.org)]{.ExternalRef}. You will see the extension set a value in the input field and click the search button, and you will be taken to the page on JavaScript.

If you wanted to make the text entry more realistic, you could adjust the background [script]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm71} to the following:

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par85 .Para}
*content-script.js*

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC17 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const typedValue = \"javascript\";
:::

::: FixedLine
const input = document.querySelector(\"#searchInput\");
:::

::: FixedLine
const form = document.querySelector(\"#search-form\");
:::

::: FixedLine
function typeOrSubmit(idx = 0) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const char = typedValue\[idx\];
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (!char) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    setTimeout(() =\> form.submit(), 500);
:::

::: FixedLine
  } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
    input.value = input.value + char;
:::

::: FixedLine
    setTimeout(() =\> typeOrSubmit(++idx), 100);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::

::: FixedLine
if (input && form) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  setTimeout(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    input.focus();
:::

::: FixedLine
    typeOrSubmit();
:::

::: FixedLine
  }, 2000);
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB261 .pagebreak aria-label="261" role="doc-pagebreak" title="261"}Reload the [extension]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73} and the Wikipedia page. The content script will progressively type out the search term. Also note here that the content script is submitting the form directly, not just clicking the search button.

This touches upon an important concept to keep in mind when writing content scripts: you are at the mercy of the host [page]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75}. In the above example, we are using selectors defined by the host page to locate the elements we want to interact with. If the host page changes these selectors even a small amount, the content script will break. Furthermore, note that the content scripts are using built-in event dispatching methods like [click()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [focus()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [submit()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. These are handy because they are succinct and partially allow you the ability to avoid dispatching events manually.

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par88 .Para}
Sometimes, dispatching [events]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77} manually is required. A content script can't see which elements have event handlers attached, but the developer can find this information out ahead of time using the [getEventListeners()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method. For example, on [[[[https://wikipedia.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://wikipedia.org)]{.ExternalRef}, you can use this method to find all the event listeners on the "Read Wikipedia in your language" button (Figure [[8-4](#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Fig4)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB262 .pagebreak aria-label="262" role="doc-pagebreak" title="262"}

<figure id="Fig4" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_MO4" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_8_Chapter/534317_2_En_8_Fig4_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Wikipedia homepage alongside a browser&#39;s developer tools. The left side shows the Wikipedia logo, a globe with puzzle pieces, and links to various language versions, such as English, Deutsch, and Español, with article counts. The right side displays the &quot;Elements&quot; and &quot;Console&quot; tabs of the developer tools, highlighting a JavaScript event listener for a language list button." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 8-4</span>
<p>The result of getEventListeners()</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

We see here there is an event listener set by the host page. We can then [check]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm78}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm79} what this handler is doing by just dispatching a test click event in the developer console -- and you should see it toggle the language menu. To automate the opening of this menu, we could certainly call [click()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in the content script, but for the sake of this example, let's instead open the menu by manually [dispatching]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm80}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm81} a click event:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par90 .Para}
*content-script.js*

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC18 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
setTimeout(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const el = document.querySelector(\"#js-lang-list-button\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Scroll down to the button so we can see the click work
:::

::: FixedLine
  el.scrollIntoView();
:::

::: FixedLine
  **el.dispatchEvent(new Event(\"click\"));**
:::

::: FixedLine
}, 2000);
:::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB263 .pagebreak aria-label="263" role="doc-pagebreak" title="263"}Reload the [extension]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm82}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm83} and the Wikipedia page. You should see the content script scroll the page down and automatically open the language menu.
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
## CSS Isolation {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par92 .Para}
Injecting [CSS]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm84} into the page is useful when you intend to alter the appearance of existing page content, but less so when you are styling a widget that wants to be totally isolated from page CSS. To prevent CSS bleeding through, a common technique is to render your widget inside a shadow DOM, thereby totally isolating the widget HTML from the page CSS. One caveat of this is that the [CSS]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm85} cannot be injected as a content script, but rather loaded as a web accessible resource. The following example injects a content script widget that creates its own shadow DOM and renders itself inside the shadow DOM along with [[content-script.css]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm86}.

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC19 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Shadow DOM Widget Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"web_accessible_resources\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"resources\": \[\"content-script.css\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-6a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm87}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB264 .pagebreak aria-label="264" role="doc-pagebreak" title="264"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC20 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
(async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const container = document.createElement(\"div\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  container.style.cssText = \"position:fixed;top:0;left:0;z-index:10000;\";
:::

::: FixedLine
  document.body.appendChild(container);
:::
:::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const shadowRoot = container.attachShadow({ mode: \"open\" });
:::
::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const widget = document.createElement(\"div\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  widget.textContent = \"Hello from Shadow DOM!\";
:::

::: FixedLine
  widget.id = \"my-widget\";
:::
::::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **const cssText = await fetch(chrome.runtime.getURL(\"content-script.css\")).then(**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **(res) =\> res.text()**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **);**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **const styleSheet = new CSSStyleSheet();**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **styleSheet.replaceSync(cssText);**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **shadowRoot.adoptedStyleSheets = \[styleSheet\];**
:::
:::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  shadowRoot.append(widget);
:::

::: FixedLine
})();
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-6b]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm88}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm89}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB265 .pagebreak aria-label="265" role="doc-pagebreak" title="265"}

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC21 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**:host {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **all: initial;**
:::

::: FixedLine
**}**
:::
::::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
#my-widget {
:::

::: FixedLine
  width: 200px;
:::

::: FixedLine
  height: 200px;
:::

::: FixedLine
  background: white;
:::

::: FixedLine
  border: 2px dashed black;
:::

::: FixedLine
  display: grid;
:::

::: FixedLine
  place-items: center;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-6c]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.css]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm90}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm91}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par93 .Para}
Some things to note here:

::: UnorderedList
-   [[content-script.css]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm92}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm93} is not actually being injected into the page, but rather into the shadow DOM.

-   The CSS is using the :host { [all: initial; }]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} rule to prevent CSS rules applied to the host element from bleeding inside the shadow DOM.
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec16Heading"}
## Modules and Code Splitting {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16Heading .Heading}

As any experienced developer knows, modern JavaScript makes heavy use of JavaScript modules and the [import]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} keyword. Because the top-level content script is not a [module]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm94}, and there is currently no way to define it as a module, it cannot have static imports. Fortunately, there are a handful of straightforward solutions.

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec17Heading"}
### Bundling {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB266 .pagebreak aria-label="266" role="doc-pagebreak" title="266"}Most extension development is done using sophisticated build tools like Vite, Parcel, Webpack, or Plasmo, and these are usually configured to combine the entire content script [bundle]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm95} into a single file, thereby eliminating the need for using imports in the top-level content script. Traditional web applications have more of a need for lazy loading, since from a performance perspective, loading data from a remote server is expensive. Since browser extensions are exclusively served from the extension file server on the local device, there is a negligible penalty for bundling the entire content [script]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm96} into a gigantic monolithic script.
:::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec18Heading"}
### Dynamic Imports {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18Heading .Heading}

Content scripts may not be able to use [static imports]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm97}, but they are perfectly happy using [dynamic imports]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm98} and loading a secondary module that *can* use static imports. Doing so requires an additional network request to the extension file server, but this penalty is negligible and therefore acceptable in most cases.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par99 .Para}
Of course, any static or dynamic [import]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm99} in a content script will generate a network request for that module. Therefore, to allow these modules to be imported, they must be listed under [web_accessible_resources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in the manifest. This is demonstrated in the following example extension, which performs a dynamic import. Load the extension and inspect the console output:

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC22 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Dynamic Import Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"css\": \[\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"web_accessible_resources\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"resources\": \[\"\*.js\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-7a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB267 .pagebreak aria-label="267" role="doc-pagebreak" title="267"}

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC23 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const url = chrome.runtime.getURL(\"foo.js\");
:::

::: FixedLine
import(url).then((fooModule) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  fooModule.bar();
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-7b]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm100}
:::
::::
::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC24 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
import apiTest from \"./api-test.js\";
:::

::: FixedLine
export function bar() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Called bar!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::

::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Loaded foo module!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
apiTest();
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-7c]{.CaptionNumber}

[foo.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm101}[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm102}
:::
::::
::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB268 .pagebreak aria-label="268" role="doc-pagebreak" title="268"}

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC25 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
export default function () {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // You will only see this method present if
:::

::: FixedLine
  // the script has access to the WebExtensions API
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Can access API:\", !!chrome.runtime.getURL);
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-7d]{.CaptionNumber}

[api-test.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm103}
:::
::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par100 .Para}
The console [output]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm104} will be as follows:

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC26 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
Loaded foo module!
:::

::: FixedLine
Can access API: **true**
:::

::: FixedLine
**Called bar!**
:::
::::::
:::::::
::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par101 .Para}
There are a few [things]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm105} to note here:

::: UnorderedList
-   As expected, both the static and dynamic [import]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} incurred network requests for the module files.

-   The imported module retains access to the WebExtensions API and can import other modules.

-   Because it's a dynamic [import]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm106}, you can call methods on the imported module from the returned module object.
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec19Heading"}
### Dynamic Script Tags {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19Heading .Heading}

It is also [possible]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm107} to load modules by dynamically creating a [\<script type=\"module\"\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} element in the page. Change the previous example extension to match the following:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par106 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB269 .pagebreak aria-label="269" role="doc-pagebreak" title="269"}[*content-script.js*]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm108}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC27 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const url = chrome.runtime.getURL(\"foo.js\");
:::

::: FixedLine
const script = document.createElement(\"script\");
:::

::: FixedLine
script.setAttribute(\"type\", \"module\");
:::

::: FixedLine
script.setAttribute(\"src\", url);
:::

::: FixedLine
document.head.appendChild(script);
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par107 .Para}
Reload the [extension]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm109} and you will see the following console output:

:::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC28 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
Loaded foo module!
:::

::: FixedLine
Can access API: **false**
:::
:::::
::::::
:::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par108 .Para}
There are a few things to note here:

::: UnorderedList
-   Dynamically created script tags lose access to the WebExtensions API.

-   Because this isn't using the [import]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} keyword, we are unable to use specific exports from the module.
:::
::::

You may [encounter]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm110} some special situations where dynamic script tag creation is useful, but overall, it is less useful than using dynamic imports.
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec20Heading"}
## Programmatic Injection {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20Heading .Heading}

You can inject [content]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm111} scripts in ways other than just specifying them in the manifest. The [chrome.scripting]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm112} allows you to programmatically inject both JavaScript and CSS into a page. JavaScript can be injected using [[chrome.scripting.executeScript]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm113}, while CSS is added with [chrome.scripting.insertCSS]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Both methods are *overloaded*, meaning they support multiple ways to inject content scripts based on the desired implementation.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec21Heading"}
### Injecting Inline Functions or Styles {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB270 .pagebreak aria-label="270" role="doc-pagebreak" title="270"}The [chrome.scripting]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm114} can be used to inject a JavaScript function expression or a CSS style string into the page. The mechanism by which the function is passed into the page is somewhat unusual: it is effectively calling [func.toString()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and then evaluating that string in the context of the page.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par114 .Para}
Consider the following example that injects JS and CSS content scripts when the [toolbar]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm115} icon is clicked:

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC29 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Inline Injection Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"scripting\", \"activeTab\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {}
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-8a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm116}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC30 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener((tab) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const target = {
:::

::: FixedLine
    tabId: tab.id,
:::

::: FixedLine
  };
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.scripting.executeScript({**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **target,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **func: () =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **document.body.innerHTML = \`Hello, world!\`;**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.scripting.insertCSS({**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **target,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **css: \`body { background-color: red !important; }\`,**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-8b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm117}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB271 .pagebreak aria-label="271" role="doc-pagebreak" title="271"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

Note here that the content [script]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm118} function expression is defined in the background script but references a global [document]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} object when none exists. This works because the function is only evaluated once injected into the page.

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Caution
:::

When you inject an inline function, it is immediately executed in the page's context and then disappears. This means the function is not persistent, so event listeners like [chrome.runtime.onMessage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} do not stay registered.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec22Heading"}
### Passing Arguments {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par117 .Para}
The [chrome.scripting.executeScript]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [method]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm119} allows [passing arguments]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm120} to the injected function using the [args]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property. The following example demonstrates how this is accomplished by passing a string argument that is interpolated into the page HTML:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC31 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener((tab) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const target = { tabId: tab.id };
:::
:::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  chrome.scripting.executeScript({
:::

::: FixedLine
    target,
:::

::: FixedLine
    func: (**message**) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      document.body.innerHTML = **message**;
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    **args: \[\"Hello, world!\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB272 .pagebreak aria-label="272" role="doc-pagebreak" title="272"}This *only* works with inline function expressions (i.e., content scripts injected via the [func]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property).
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec23Heading"}
### Injecting Files {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23Heading .Heading}

Instead of [passing]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm121} in functions and strings, it is also possible to inject content scripts defined in files. The following example demonstrates how to inject JS and CSS:

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par120 .Para}
::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC32 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener((tab) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const target = {
:::

::: FixedLine
    tabId: tab.id,
:::

::: FixedLine
  };
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.scripting.executeScript({**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **target,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **files: \[\"content-script.js\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.scripting.insertCSS({**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **target,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **files: \[\"content-script.css\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-9a]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB273 .pagebreak aria-label="273" role="doc-pagebreak" title="273"}

::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC33 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
document.body.innerHTML = \`Hello, world!\`;
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-9b]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm122}
:::
::::
:::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC34 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
body {
:::

::: FixedLine
  background-color: red !important;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-9c]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.css]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm123}
:::
::::
:::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec24Heading"}
### Registration/Unregistration {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24Heading .Heading}

It is possible to register and [unregister]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm124} declarative content scripts on the fly. This is analogous to updating the manifest [content_scripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property. Because this is modifying the content script injection list, the scripts will not be injected until the next page load.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Par122 .Para}
The following example defines an extension that toggles the declarative injection when the toolbar icon is clicked.

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC35 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Registration/Unregistration Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"scripting\", \"activeTab\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {}
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-10a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB274 .pagebreak aria-label="274" role="doc-pagebreak" title="274"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PC36 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const contentScriptId = \"1\";
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener(async () =\> {
:::
:::::

:::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const activeScripts = await chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts();
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Toggle the content script
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (activeScripts.find((x) =\> x.id === contentScriptId )) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    chrome.scripting.unregisterContentScripts({
:::

::: FixedLine
      ids: \[id\],
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.log(\"Unregistered content script\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
    chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts(\[
:::

::: FixedLine
      {
:::

::: FixedLine
        id: contentScriptId,
:::

::: FixedLine
        matches: \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
        js: \[\"content-script.js\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
        css: \[\"content-script.css\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      },
:::

::: FixedLine
    \]);
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.log(\"Registered content script\")[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm125};
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 8-10b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm126}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_PB275 .pagebreak aria-label="275" role="doc-pagebreak" title="275"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec25Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you learned about how content scripts are a powerful feature in browser extensions that allow JavaScript and CSS to be injected into web pages, enabling modifications ranging from simple tweaks to fully interactive widgets. We explored the fundamentals of content scripts, including how they are injected declaratively via the manifest or programmatically using the [chrome.scripting API]{#534317_2_En_8_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm127}. Additionally, we explored methods for dynamically importing modules, handling network requests within content scripts, and automating user interactions.

In the next chapter, we will cover all the major WebExtensions APIs and how they can be used.
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml}

::::: ChapterContextInformation
:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_b979-8-8688-1594-2_9 .ContextInformation}
::: ChapterCopyright
© The Author(s), under exclusive license to APress Media, LLC, part of Springer Nature 2025
:::

[M. Frisbie]{.ContextInformationAuthorEditorNames}[[Building Browser Extensions]{.BookTitle}]{.ContextInformationBookTitles}[<https://doi.org/10.1007/979-8-8688-1594-2_9>]{.ChapterDOI}
::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 9. Extension and Browser APIs {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB277 .pagebreak aria-label="277" role="doc-pagebreak" title="277"}The WebExtensions API is the secret sauce of browser extensions. It allows them to reach into the web page and the browser to make changes, inspect and modify network traffic, and control aspects of the native browser's user interface.

::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Standards and the Role of W3C {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

The WebExtensions API is currently maintained by the World Wide Web Consortium ([W3C)]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2} through the [WebExtensions Community Group (WECG)]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3}. Unlike formal web standards like HTML, CSS, and the DOM API, WebExtensions is not yet an official standard. Instead, it is developed collaboratively by representatives from major browser vendors like Mozilla, Google, Apple, and Microsoft within the Community Group framework. The shared goal remains to create, maintain, and evolve the WebExtensions specification while reducing fragmentation and ensuring a consistent development experience across [browsers]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5}.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
## Global API Namespace {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB278 .pagebreak aria-label="278" role="doc-pagebreak" title="278"}All extensions access browser APIs through a [global namespace]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7}, either [chrome.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [browser.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Historically, Chromium browsers and Safari have favored the [chrome.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} namespace, while Firefox has long favored the [browser.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} namespace. At the time of the writing of this book, Chrome is introducing full support for the [browser.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} namespace. Although the [chrome.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} namespace remains widely supported, targeting [browser.\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is increasingly the best practice for forward [compatibility]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9} and cross-browser consistency.
:::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
## Promises vs. Callbacks {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par5 .Para}
The [WebExtensions API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10} was written before [async/await]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} was an industry standard. The initial API implementation used callbacks to support asynchronous code execution. For example, the following snippet executes a callback after a message response is received:

::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.sendMessage(\"msg\", **(response) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **console.log(\"Received a response!\", response);**
:::

::: FixedLine
**}**);
:::
::::::
:::::::
::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par6 .Para}
To align with modern coding conventions, most browsers have retrofitted their API methods to support both callbacks and promises. The above snippet can be refactored to use [async/await]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

:::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**const response = await** chrome.runtime.sendMessage(\"msg\");
:::

::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Received a response!\", response);
:::
:::::
::::::
:::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par7 .Para}
There are some things to know about making this change:

::: UnorderedList
-   Not all API methods have been adapted to support promises. Check your browser's documentation to see if it is supported. Callbacks will always work.

-   []{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB279 .pagebreak aria-label="279" role="doc-pagebreak" title="279"}For each method call, only use a callback or a promise, never both.

-   Mozilla's [webextension-polyfill]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} ([[[[https://github.com/mozilla/webextension-polyfill]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://github.com/mozilla/webextension-polyfill)]{.ExternalRef}) can be used to augment the WebExtensions API methods to be promise based irrespective of the host browser's implementation.
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
## Error Handling {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par11 .Para}
Errors thrown from WebExtensions API [methods]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12} must be handled differently based on your usage. If you use callbacks, when the method fails, the [chrome.runtime.lastError]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property will be defined only inside the callback handler:

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.tabs.executeScript(tabId, details, () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (**chrome.runtime.lastError**) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Error handling here
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
})
:::
::::::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par12 .Para}
If you use [async/await]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the error can be caught with a [.catch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [try/catch]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} block:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.tabs.executeScript(tabId, details).catch((e) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Error handling here
:::

::: FixedLine
})
:::

::: FixedLine
// or
:::

::: FixedLine
try {
:::

::: FixedLine
  await chrome.tabs.executeScript(tabId, details);
:::

::: FixedLine
} catch(e) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Error handling here
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
## Context-Restricted APIs {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par13 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB280 .pagebreak aria-label="280" role="doc-pagebreak" title="280"}Not all Chrome extension [APIs]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14} are available in every execution context. Access to certain APIs depends on where the code is running:

::: UnorderedList
-   APIs like [alarms]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [runtime.onInstalled]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} are only usable in background service workers, not in UI contexts.

-   The devtools.\* APIs can only be used in a DevTools page context, not in background or UI scripts.

-   Content scripts can't access most extension APIs directly. They must message the background script to use privileged [APIs]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16}.
:::
::::
:::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
## Events API {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

The "[Events API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17}" refers to the general pattern of adding event listeners inside an extension to respond to events fired by the browser or elsewhere in the extension. Most WebExtensions APIs utilize this format.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
### Format {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par18 .Para}
Each event [type]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18} has an interface to add, remove, and inspect listeners. For example, the [chrome.runtime.onMessage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} interface has the following methods:

::: UnorderedList
-   [chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.onMessage.dispatch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.onMessage.hasListener()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.onMessage.hasListeners()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [chrome.runtime.onMessage.removeListener()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Warning
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB281 .pagebreak aria-label="281" role="doc-pagebreak" title="281"}[dispatch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} can be used to forcibly dispatch an event, but it is not documented and therefore should not be relied upon.
::::

These methods are shared by all event interfaces. [addListener()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is used to attach a handler function to run when an event is fired, and [hasListener()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [hasListeners()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [removeListener()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} are used to view and remove that same function.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par26 .Para}
The following example uses several of these methods to handle toolbar icon button clicks:

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Toolbar Click Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 9-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
let count = 0;
:::

::: FixedLine
function handler() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Handler executed!\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (++count \> 4) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    chrome.action.onClicked.**removeListener**(handler);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::

::: FixedLine
// false
:::

::: FixedLine
console.log(chrome.action.onClicked.**hasListener**(handler));
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.**addListener**(handler);
:::

::: FixedLine
// true
:::

::: FixedLine
console.log(chrome.action.onClicked.**hasListener**(handler));
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 9-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB282 .pagebreak aria-label="282" role="doc-pagebreak" title="282"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

This example sets a listener for [onClicked]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} events, listens for five events, and then de-registers itself.
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
### Event Filtering {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par28 .Para}
It is possible to instruct the browser to declaratively filter events that only match certain URLs. The following code uses the native [event filtering]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19} behavior to only respond to events on [\*.wikipedia.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.webNavigation.onCommitted.addListener((event) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Handle filtered event
:::

::: FixedLine
}**, {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **url: \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **{ hostSuffix: \"wikipedia.org\" }**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
**}**);
:::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
## WebExtensions API Quick Reference {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par29 .Para}
This section is a comprehensive list of extension APIs. Each API includes a short snippet demonstrating common usage and links to the full documentation. The two primary references for the WebExtensions API are the Chrome Docs and MDN.[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB283 .pagebreak aria-label="283" role="doc-pagebreak" title="283"}

::: UnorderedList
-   [Chrome Docs]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20}: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/api]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/api)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [MDN]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21}: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

The companion extension to this book, *Example Chrome Extension*, has interactive demos and source code for many of these APIs. Get it here: [[[[https://buildingbrowserextensions.com/b2x]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://buildingbrowserextensions.com/b2x)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
### Permissions {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par33 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   [Permissions API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/permissions/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/permissions/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   Permissions API -- MDN[:]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Permissions_API]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Permissions_API)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par36 .Para}
The [chrome.permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API allows you to inspect current permissions and add or remove permissions. You can also add handlers that fire when permissions are added or removed.

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Can only request permissions after user action
:::

::: FixedLine
  const permissionsGranted =
:::

::: FixedLine
    await **chrome.permissions.request**({
:::

::: FixedLine
      permissions: \[\'tabs\'\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
const hasPermissions = await **chrome.permissions.contains**({
:::

::: FixedLine
  permissions: \[\'tabs\'\]
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
### Messaging {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par37 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB284 .pagebreak aria-label="284" role="doc-pagebreak" title="284"}[Chrome Messaging]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25} Overview -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/mv3/messaging/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/mv3/messaging/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [runtime]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/runtime/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/runtime/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [runtime]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27} -- MDN: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/runtime/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/runtime/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/tabs/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/tabs/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29} -- MDN: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/tabs/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/tabs/)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::

The extension messaging API is used to send messages between parts of the extension, or between the extension and an outside entity (such as another extension, the active web page, or an outside program).

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
#### One-off Messages {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par44 .Para}
The [chrome.runtime.sendMessage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [method]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31} can be used to send single messages between extension components. It can only be used to send messages *from* a content script. Listeners can send a message back inside the message handler.[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB285 .pagebreak aria-label="285" role="doc-pagebreak" title="285"}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Sender
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.sendMessage**(msg, (response) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Handle response
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Receiver
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onMessage**.addListener((msg, sender, sendResponse) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Handle initial message from sender
:::

::: FixedLine
  sendResponse(responseMsg);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par45 .Para}
To send a one-off message to a specific content script, you must instead use [chrome.tabs.sendMessage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and specify the [tabId]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Sender
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.tabs.sendMessage**(tabId, msg, (response) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Handle response
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Receiver (content script)
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onMessage**.addListener((msg, sender, sendResponse) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Handle initial message[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33} from sender
:::

::: FixedLine
  sendResponse(responseMsg);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
#### Opening Message Ports {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par46 .Para}
Persistent message [ports]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34} can be opened to connect two extension components that will be sending a large volume of message back and forth. It can only be used to connect a port *from* a content script.

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Endpoint 1
:::

::: FixedLine
const port1 = **chrome.runtime.connect**({name: \"portName\"});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Outgoing
:::

::: FixedLine
port1.postMessage(msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Incoming
:::

::: FixedLine
port1.onMessage.addListener((msg) =\> {});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Endpoint 2
:::

::: FixedLine
let port2 = null;
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onConnect**.addListener((p) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  port2 = p;
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Outgoing
:::

::: FixedLine
port2.postMessage(msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Incoming
:::

::: FixedLine
port2.onMessage.addListener((msg) =\> {});
:::
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par47 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB286 .pagebreak aria-label="286" role="doc-pagebreak" title="286"}To connect a port to specific content script, you must instead use [chrome.tabs.connect()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and specify the [tabId]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Endpoint 1
:::

::: FixedLine
const port1 = **chrome.tabs.connect**(tabId, {name: \"portName\"});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Outgoing
:::

::: FixedLine
port1.postMessage(msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Incoming
:::

::: FixedLine
port1.onMessage.addListener((msg) =\> {});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Endpoint 2 (content script)
:::

::: FixedLine
let port2 = null;
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onConnect**.addListener((p) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  port2 = p;
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Outgoing
:::

::: FixedLine
port2.postMessage(msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Incoming
:::

::: FixedLine
port2.onMessage.addListener((msg) =\> {});
:::
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
#### Messaging Native Applications {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par48 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB287 .pagebreak aria-label="287" role="doc-pagebreak" title="287"}Extensions can exchange [messages]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35} with native applications. If the name of the native application is known (e.g., [com.my_company.my_application]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}), the extension can target it in a similar way to content scripts:

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.sendNativeMessage**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  applicationName, msg, (response) =\> {});
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.tabs.connectNative**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  applicationName, {name: \"portName\"});
:::
:::::::
::::::::
:::::::::

An example of this is shown in Chapter [[[14]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_14_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}. Safari extensions use native messaging to connect the extension to the [native]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36} iOS or macOS app.
::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
#### Messaging Other Extensions {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par50 .Para}
To send a one-off message to another [extension]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}, you can pass the extension's ID to [chrome.runtime.sendMessage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC14 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Sender
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.sendMessage**(extensionId, msg, (response) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Handle response
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Receiver (foreign extension)
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onMessageExternal**.addListener((msg, sender, sendResponse) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Handle initial message from sender
:::

::: FixedLine
  sendResponse(responseMsg);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par51 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB288 .pagebreak aria-label="288" role="doc-pagebreak" title="288"}To connect a port to another extension, you can use [chrome.runtime.connect()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and specify the [extensionId]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC15 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Extension 1
:::

::: FixedLine
const port1 = **chrome.runtime.connect**(extensionId, {name: \"portName\"});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Outgoing
:::

::: FixedLine
port1.postMessage(msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Incoming
:::

::: FixedLine
port1.onMessage.addListener((msg) =\> {});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Extension2
:::

::: FixedLine
let port2 = null;
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onConnectExternal**.addListener((p) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  port2 = p;
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Outgoing
:::

::: FixedLine
port2.postMessage(msg);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Incoming
:::

::: FixedLine
port2.onMessage.addListener((msg) =\> {});
:::
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par52 .Para}
This method also allows for regular web pages (not content scripts) to send messages to your extension in the exact same way. The API is exposed in the web page's JavaScript runtime, and your extension can receive messages if the following conditions are true:

::: UnorderedList
-   The host page knows the ID of your extension and passes it to [runtime.connect()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [runtime.sendMessage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

-   Your extension whitelists the web page URL under the manifest's [extern]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[ally\_]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[connectable]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} field.
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec16Heading"}
### Storage {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par55 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   [storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/storage/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/storage/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API -- MDN: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/storage)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par58 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB289 .pagebreak aria-label="289" role="doc-pagebreak" title="289"}The extension storage API is a simple but powerful key/value storage. There are four main storage options you can use:

::: UnorderedList
-   [storage.local]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} stores values only on the local browser.

-   [storage.sync]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} stores values that are shared between authenticated browser sessions.

-   [storage.session]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} stores values in memory that will be discarded when the browser closes.

-   [storage.managed]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is a read-only store intended for enterprise users with a configured data backend during a policy-based installation
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par63 .Para}
[local]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [sync]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [session]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} all share the following:

::: UnorderedList
-   With only the [storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission, default maximum storage size for each is 10 MB. This limit can be removed with the additional [unlimitedStorage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission.

-   The maximum available storage size is the JSON serialized version of the data, not just the value of the data. Key names and JSON punctuation count toward the cap.

-   []{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB290 .pagebreak aria-label="290" role="doc-pagebreak" title="290"}Each features only a [get()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [set()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method. These methods are asynchronous, and both can read and write in parallel.

-   You can listen for changes to each by setting an [onChanged]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} handler.

-   Plain JavaScript objects can be stored and retrieved as is; there is no need to serialize/deserialize.
:::
::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par69 .Para}
The following example shows a parallelized [get/set]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [onChanged]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} handler:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC16 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.storage.onChanged.addListener**(console.log);
:::

::: FixedLine
await **chrome.storage.local.set**({ foo: \"tmp\" });
:::

::: FixedLine
// { foo: { newValue \"tmp\" } }
:::

::: FixedLine
await **chrome.storage.local.set**({ foo: \"bar\", baz: \"qux\" });
:::

::: FixedLine
// {
:::

::: FixedLine
//   foo: { oldValue: \"tmp\", newValue \"bar\" }
:::

::: FixedLine
//   baz: { newValue \"qux\" }
:::

::: FixedLine
// }
:::

::: FixedLine
console.log(await **chrome.storage.local.get**(\[\"foo\", \"baz\"\]));
:::

::: FixedLine
// { foo: \"bar\", baz: \"qux\" }
:::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec17Heading"}
### Authentication {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par70 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   [identity]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/identity/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/identity/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [identity]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API -- [MDN:]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/identity]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/identity)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par73 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB291 .pagebreak aria-label="291" role="doc-pagebreak" title="291"}The [chrome.identity]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API is used to manage authentication state and OAuth flows. Google Chrome supports extra methods that allow for native authentication inside the browser. All browsers support the use of [launchWebAuthFlow]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} for generic OAuth flows.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC17 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Native browser OAuth
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.identity.getAuthToken**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  { interactive: true },
:::

::: FixedLine
  async (token) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (token) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      const info = await **chrome.identity.getProfileUserInfo**(
:::

::: FixedLine
        { accountStatus: \"ANY\" }
:::

::: FixedLine
      );
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Supports generic OAuth
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.identity.launchWebAuthFlow**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  {
:::

::: FixedLine
    url: authUrl,
:::

::: FixedLine
    interactive: true,
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  (redirectUrl) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (redirectUrl) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This API is covered in depth in Chapter [[[11]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec18Heading"}
### Network Requests {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par75 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/declarativeNetRequest/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/declarativeNetRequest/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42} -- MDN:

    [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/declarativeNetRequest)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/webRequest/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/webRequest/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44} -- MDN: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/webRequest)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [webNavigation]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45} -- Chrome Docs: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/webNavigation/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/webNavigation/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [webNavigation]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46} -- MDN: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/webNavigation]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/webNavigation)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par83 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB292 .pagebreak aria-label="292" role="doc-pagebreak" title="292"}The [network request APIs]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47}[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48} include [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [webNavigation]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

::: UnorderedList
-   [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is used to declaratively instruct the browser to manage page traffic according to a set of rules defined by the extension.

-   [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is used to imperatively control page traffic with JavaScript handlers.

-   [webNavigation]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is used to inspect tab-level navigation events.
:::
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Par87 .Para}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PC18 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// Using DNR to block traffic to wikipedia.org
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.declarativeNetRequest.updateDynamicRules**({
:::

::: FixedLine
  addRules: \[{
:::

::: FixedLine
    id: 1,
:::

::: FixedLine
    priority: 1,
:::

::: FixedLine
    action: {
:::

::: FixedLine
      type: \"block\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    condition: {
:::

::: FixedLine
      urlFilter: \"https://www.wikipedia.org\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
  }\]
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::

::: FixedLine
// Using webRequest to block traffic to wikipedia.org
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.webRequest.onBeforeRequest.addListener**(
:::

::: FixedLine
  (details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return {
:::

::: FixedLine
      cancel: true
:::

::: FixedLine
    };
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  { urls: \[\"\*://www.wikipedia.org/\*\"\] },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \[**\"blocking\"**\]
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Log all tab traffic with webNavigation
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.webNavigation.onCompleted**.addListener((details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(details.url);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

These APIs are covered in depth in Chapter [[[11]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.

[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB294 .pagebreak aria-label="294" role="doc-pagebreak" title="294"}Chapter [[[12]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} includes examples of how to build an adblocker using [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec19Heading"}
