:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec59 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec59Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_Sec59Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml_PB326 .pagebreak aria-label="326" role="doc-pagebreak" title="326"}In this chapter, we covered all the various idiosyncrasies of the WebExtensions API, including promises and callbacks, error handling, and the events API. We went through each of the APIs, learning what they are for and seeing how they allow extensions to manage browser UI, network traffic, storage, authentication, messaging, system resources, privacy controls, AI models, and more.

In the next chapter, we will discuss the permissions that are required to enable these APIs as well as the best ways to manage permissions in an extension.
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml}

::: MainTitleSection
# 10. Permissions {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB327 .pagebreak aria-label="327" role="doc-pagebreak" title="327"}Browser extension [permissions]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1} are conceptually identical to mobile app permissions. Both software platforms have the ability to access very powerful APIs, but to protect the end user, permission to access these APIs must be explicitly requested on a piecewise basis. For both apps and extensions, permissions are declared by the developer in the code base -- for extensions, this is the extension manifest. In both cases, requesting access to trivial permissions will not require explicit user permission, but very powerful permissions will require the user to explicitly grant access. Furthermore, adding additional permissions in a subsequent update will typically require the user to explicitly accept this change in scope.

When building browser extensions, choosing permissions carefully is critically important. It has implications on how the marketplace listing will appear, the install and update flow for your extension, and how your extension will be reviewed by the marketplace.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Safari extension--supported permissions mostly overlap with Chromium and Firefox extensions, but there are many points of divergence. Read more about this here: [[[https://​developer.​apple.​com/​documentation/​safariservices/​assessing-your-safari-web-extension-s-browser-compatibility]{.RefSource}](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/safariservices/assessing-your-safari-web-extension-s-browser-compatibility)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Permissions Basics {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par5 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB328 .pagebreak aria-label="328" role="doc-pagebreak" title="328"}To understand the [basics]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2} of adding permissions, let's examine a very basic scenario that requires permissions. Begin with the following extension:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Missing Permissions Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"options_ui\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"open_in_tab\": true,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"page\": \"options.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"options.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

options.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.storage.sync.set({ foo: \"bar\" });
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-1c]{.CaptionNumber}

options.js
:::
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

This very simple extension consists of an options page that, when opened, attempts to write a dummy value to the browser storage.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB329 .pagebreak aria-label="329" role="doc-pagebreak" title="329"}This example uses an options page for two reasons: (1) because it is easier to access the console and reload the script and (2) because when an error is thrown in the background page in the first turn of the event loop, the browser will mark the service worker as permanently idle and refuse to open the background worker developer tools.
::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par8 .Para}
After loading this extension and opening the options page, you will see this error in the browser console:

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading \'sync\')
:::
::::
:::::
::::::

The reason for this error is that the [chrome.storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API requires the [storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission. Because the permission was not requested, the browser simply does not define the [storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property on the [chrome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} global object, and the attempted [sync()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} call throws the above error.

This might surprise you, as the entire extension was loaded without issue. It is important to understand that the browser makes no assumptions about what permissions might be needed when loading the extension, or if a requested permission will be needed in the code base at all. All permissions handling occurs at runtime.

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par11 .Para}
To fix this example, all you must do is add the [storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission as shown below:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Added Permissions Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"options_ui\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"open_in_tab\": true,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"page\": \"options.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"permissions\": \[\"storage\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-2]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB330 .pagebreak aria-label="330" role="doc-pagebreak" title="330"}
:::::::::::::::::

After reloading the extension, the options page will no longer throw the error. This is because you have successfully added the needed permissions to use the [chrome.s]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[torage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API.
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
## [Checking]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3} Permissions {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par13 .Para}
You can programmatically read all the permissions the current execution [context]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4} has access to via the [chrome.permissions.getAll()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method. Update the [options.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} script from above to log the extension's permissions:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.storage.sync.set({ foo: \"bar\" });
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.permissions.getAll()**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **.then(JSON.stringify)**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **.then(console.log);**
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-3]{.CaptionNumber}

options.js
:::
::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

Reload the extension, and reload the options page. The [console]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5} will display the following:

[{ \"origins]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}**\"**[:\[\],]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} **\"permissions\":\[\"storage\"\]** [}]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Once granted, optional permissions (covered in upcoming sections) are no different than required permissions, so [chrome.permissions.getAll()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} computes a merged [list]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6} of all the required *and* optional permissions.
::::
::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
## Using Optional Permissions {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par17 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB331 .pagebreak aria-label="331" role="doc-pagebreak" title="331"}As the name indicates, optional [permissions]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7} are permissions that can be optionally requested from the user. Optional permissions serve two primary [use cases]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8}:

::: UnorderedList
-   Permissions that will be requested right when the extension first loads, but that are not critical for the extension to operate properly. This is analogous to a mobile app requesting access to your location right when you open it for the first time.

-   Permissions that will be requested only after the user performs some action that triggers the request, such as clicking a "connect to foobar.com" button. This is analogous to a mobile app requesting access to your device's photos only after you click a "share image" button.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par20 .Para}
Some examples of situations where optional permissions are useful:

::: UnorderedList
-   You wish to allow users more control over the extension by allowing them to turn off some permissions, but still use some features of the extension.

-   You've already launched an extension but want to add more permissions without forcing the user to re-enable the extension.

-   You want to reduce the number of extension warnings that are displayed to the user after an install.
:::
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
### Granting Permissions Declaratively vs. Imperatively {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

:::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par24 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB332 .pagebreak aria-label="332" role="doc-pagebreak" title="332"}Whereas regular web browser permissions can be acquired *declaratively*, optional extension permissions must be acquired *imperatively*[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9}. Consider the following example of regular JavaScript running in a web page:

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition(() =\> {})
:::
::::
:::::
::::::

The web browser geolocation API is not accessible by default; the user must grant access. However, the API is structured that simply calling this method will prompt the user with a dialog to grant permission; if the user grants permission, the script can proceed with execution as if the permission was granted all along.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par26 .Para}
Contrast this with extension example from the beginning of the chapter: calling a method without permissions will simply throw an error with no recourse. When using API methods requiring optional permissions, you must use the permissions API to check if the extension has permissions, request permissions if they are not granted, and only then can you proceed with the permissioned API call. This is demonstrated in the following example:

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Requesting Permissions Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"options_ui\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"open_in_tab\": true,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"page\": \"options.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"optional_permissions\": \[\"storage\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-4a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB333 .pagebreak aria-label="333" role="doc-pagebreak" title="333"}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<button id=\"save\"\>Save\</button\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"options.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-4b]{.CaptionNumber}

options.html
:::
::::
:::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const permissions = **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **permissions: \[\"storage\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
**};**
:::

::: FixedLine
document.querySelector(\"#save\").addEventListener(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"click\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **if (!(await chrome.permissions.contains(permissions))) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **await chrome.permissions.request(permissions);**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
    chrome.storage.sync.set({ foo: \"bar\" });
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 10-4c]{.CaptionNumber}

options.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
### Permission Request Idempotence {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par27 .Para}
The previous example can be simplified even further. Requesting [permissions]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10} is idempotent, so you can simply call [request()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} each time with no penalty:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
document.querySelector(\"#save\").addEventListener(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"click\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **await chrome.permissions.request(permissions);**
:::

::: FixedLine
    chrome.storage.sync.set({ foo: \"bar\" });
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::
::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
### Shared Permission Grants {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB334 .pagebreak aria-label="334" role="doc-pagebreak" title="334"}Certain permissions [granted]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11} through the browser's native interface, such as microphone or camera access, are not available to nonstandard extension surfaces like popups, offscreen documents, or the side panel. However, these permission grants are shared across the same origin, such as [chrome-extension://foobar]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This means if a permission is needed in one of these restricted contexts, the extension can first request it from a full-page tab where the browser allows permission prompts. Once granted, that permission can then be used in other parts of the [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12}.
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
## Host Permissions {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

Beginning in Manifest V3, [permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13} were split out into separate fields, with a mirrored split between [optional_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [optional_host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. Conceptually, the difference is straightforward: [permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} are the APIs you need access to, and [host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} are the origins you need to use the APIs in. As discussed in Chapter [[[5]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}, the host permissions list effectively creates a whitelist of domains with extra privileges.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par30 .Para}
An origin matching the whitelist [grants access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14} to the following:

::: UnorderedList
-   [XMLHttpRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [fetch]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} access to those origins without cross-origin restrictions (even for requests made from content scripts).

-   The ability to read tab-specific metadata without the [tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission, such as the [url]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [title]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and [favIconUrl]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} properties of [Tab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} objects.

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB335 .pagebreak aria-label="335" role="doc-pagebreak" title="335"}The ability to inject scripts programmatically using [tabs.executeScript()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} into pages served from those origins.

-   The ability to access cookies for that host using the [cookies]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API, as long as the [cookies]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API permission is also included.

-   The ability to bypass tracking protection for extension pages where a host is specified as a full domain or with wildcards. Content scripts, however, can only bypass tracking protection for hosts specified with a full domain.
:::
::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par36 .Para}
The only difference between [checking and requesting]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15} [optional_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} vs. [optional_host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is the use of the [origins]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property instead of [permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property. For example, checking for [google.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} origins would be done in the following way:

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.permissions.contains({ **origins: \[\"\*://\*.google.com\"\]** })
:::
::::
:::::
::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
### Permissions Lifetime {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

Once a [permission]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16} is granted, the extension has that permission for the entire lifetime of the extension. However, it is possible that the user can explicitly revoke that permission via the browser's extension management page. If the extension is uninstalled and then reinstalled, all the optional permissions that were formerly [granted]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17} are lost.
:::
::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
## Permissions Warnings {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par38 .Para}
For [permissions]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18} such as [alarms]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [storage]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19}, the APIs they enable are of little consequence to the user, and therefore, they can be accessed silently. Conversely, some permissions are sufficiently powerful and therefore []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB336 .pagebreak aria-label="336" role="doc-pagebreak" title="336"}require the user to explicitly grant access to the extension. This takes the form of a dialog box with a description of what the permissions entail. For example, the following is what displays in Google Chrome when requesting the [tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20} (Figure [[10-1](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_10_Chapter/534317_2_En_10_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser window displays a pop-up from a Chrome extension titled &quot;Requesting Permissions Example.&quot; The pop-up requests additional permissions to read browsing history. It includes options to &quot;Deny&quot; or &quot;Allow&quot; the request. The URL in the address bar is &quot;chrome-extension:/keofdfdpcipaimjlfcjolpjgbfgcpjld/options.html.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 10-1</span>
<p>Prompt displayed after <span id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21">requesting tabs permission</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

For the [permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} lists, the warning dialog will be displayed immediately after installing and before the extension has an opportunity to execute any code. If the permission is denied, the extension will not be installed.

For [[optional_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22} and [[optional_host_permissions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23}, the warning will only display when the extension makes the request for those permissions. If the permission is denied, the extension will continue to operate as it did before without the additional permissions.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB337 .pagebreak aria-label="337" role="doc-pagebreak" title="337"}Unlike HTML5 permissions, the extension will be able to make another request for the optional permission if it is initially denied.
::::

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
### Testing Permissions Warnings {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

When developing your [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24}, you will notice that loading an unpacked will not show the permission warning dialog when initially installing the extension. (Optional permissions will still show the warning dialog.) Of course, this is to avoid annoying the developer when they repeatedly reinstall their unfinished extension. Consider the following example extension, and suppose we need to evaluate what the [install-time]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25} permissions might be:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par43 .Para}
*manfest.json - Trivial one-file browser extension used to generate permission warning dialog*

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Testing Permission Warnings Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"tabs\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
#### Manual CRX Generation {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par44 .Para}
To see the install-time [permission]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27} warning dialog, you will need to pack the extension into a [.crx]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file and then load that into your browser.

::: UnorderedList
-   Click "[Pack extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28}" from the extension management page (Figure [[10-2](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Fig2)]{.InternalRef}).

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB338 .pagebreak aria-label="338" role="doc-pagebreak" title="338"}Select the extension folder (the same folder you would select when loading unpacked) (Figure [[10-3](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Fig3)]{.InternalRef}).

-   Leave the [.pem]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} field blank.

-   The browser will generate two files: a [.crx]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and a [.pem]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

-   Drag and drop the [.crx]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file into the extension management page to install it. If you did this correctly, the browser will prompt you with the permission warning dialog (Figures [[10-4](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Fig4)]{.InternalRef} and [[10-5](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Fig5)]{.InternalRef}).

-   Delete the two created files; they have no further use.
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB339 .pagebreak aria-label="339" role="doc-pagebreak" title="339"}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB340 .pagebreak aria-label="340" role="doc-pagebreak" title="340"}

<figure id="Fig2" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_MO2" class="MediaObject">

The URL bar displays &quot;chrome:/extensions&quot;. The page features a search bar labeled &quot;Search extensions&quot; and buttons for &quot;Load unpacked&quot;, &quot;Pack extension&quot;, and &quot;Update&quot;. A sidebar shows &quot;My extensions&quot; and &quot;Keyboard shortcuts&quot;. A link at the bottom reads &quot;Discover more extensions and themes on the Chrome Web Store&quot;." />
</div>

<p>The “<span id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29">Pack extension</span>” button on the extension management page</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig3" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_MO3" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_10_Chapter/534317_2_En_10_Fig3_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Chrome browser window displaying the &quot;Extensions&quot; page. A dialog box titled &quot;Pack extension&quot; is open, prompting the user to select the root directory of the extension and an optional private key file. Options to &quot;Cancel&quot; or &quot;Pack extension&quot; are at the bottom of the dialog. The browser&#39;s address bar shows &quot;chrome:/extensions&quot; and the page includes buttons for &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update.&quot;" />
</div>

</div></figcaption>
</figure>

A pop-up dialog asks to add &quot;Testing Permission Warnings Example&quot; with a note that it can read browsing history. Options to &quot;Cancel&quot; or &quot;Add extension&quot; are visible. The browser interface includes buttons for &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update,&quot; with a toggle for Developer mode." />
</div>

<p>The install-time permissions warning dialog</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

It used to be possible to install and use an extension with a local [.crx]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file -- this is no longer the case. It can still be used to test warnings as shown here, but as you will see, the extension will be permanently disabled by Chrome. Read more here: [[[https://​developer.​chrome.​com/​docs/​extensions/​how-to/​distribute/​install-extensions]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/how-to/distribute/install-extensions)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

::: Para
<figure id="Fig5" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_MO5" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_10_Chapter/534317_2_En_10_Fig5_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome Extensions page showing a warning about an extension. The page includes options to load, pack, and update extensions. A safety check section advises reviewing an extension labeled &quot;Testing Permission Warnings Example,&quot; which Chrome cannot verify. The extension is not listed in the Chrome Web Store and may have been added without the user&#39;s knowledge. Options to view details or remove the extension are available. Developer mode is toggled on." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 10-5</span>
<p>The disabled .crx extension</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
#### Automated Warning Testing {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

The Chrome team also [maintains]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32} an open source testing tool. The Extension Update Testing Tool is a local extension update server that can be used for testing updates to Chrome Extensions during local []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB341 .pagebreak aria-label="341" role="doc-pagebreak" title="341"}development, including permission grants. The tool can be found on GitHub here: [[[https://​github.​com/​GoogleChromeLabs​/​extension-update-testing-tool]{.RefSource}](https://github.com/GoogleChromeLabs/extension-update-testing-tool)]{.ExternalRef}.
:::
:::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
## Considerations for Published Extensions {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

When publishing your [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34} to a marketplace, the permissions you select can have implications outside of your code base.

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
### Triggering the Slow Review Queue {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

When building an extension, you will probably not have any discretion for selecting which permissions are needed. After all, the needs of the extension are absolute, and the permissions needed to make it function will flow from there. However, if the speed of review for your extension is important, then it is important to understand that requiring some permissions such as [\<all_urls\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} will cause your extension to be placed in a slower review queue. Also, adding new permissions in an update will trigger the slow review [queue]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36}.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

This queue can increase your extension review time from less than 24 hours to a few days.
::::
:::::
::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
## Auto-disable Updates {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par56 .Para}
One critically important [consideration]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38} is what happens when required permissions that will show warnings are added to an extension in an update. The browser silently updates extensions in the background, so it won't show the warning dialog as soon as the update is installed. Instead, []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB342 .pagebreak aria-label="342" role="doc-pagebreak" title="342"}it will silently disable the extension and add a small notification to the browser toolbar, indicating action is required. To re-enable the extension, the user must open this notification and accept the new permissions (Figures [[10-6](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Fig6)]{.InternalRef} and [[10-7](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Fig7)]{.InternalRef}). This flow is shown below.

<figure id="Fig6" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_MO6" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_10_Chapter/534317_2_En_10_Fig6_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a web browser menu showing options like &quot;New Tab,&quot; &quot;New Window,&quot; &quot;New Incognito Window,&quot; &quot;History,&quot; &quot;Downloads,&quot; &quot;Bookmarks,&quot; and &quot;Zoom.&quot; A cursor hovers over an &quot;Error&quot; button in the top right corner. The browser&#39;s address bar displays &quot;Google or type a URL.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 10-6</span>
<p>The disabled extension notification</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig7" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_MO7" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_10_Chapter/534317_2_En_10_Fig7_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A browser screenshot shows a notification from Chrome indicating that the &quot;Tab Manager for Chrome Dev Docs&quot; is disabled. The message suggests re-enabling it by accepting new permissions, which include reading and changing bookmarks. Two buttons are visible: &quot;Remove&quot; and &quot;Accept permissions.&quot; The Google homepage is partially visible in the background." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 10-7</span>
<p>The permissions dialog to re-enable the extension</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec16Heading"}
## Permissions List {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16Heading .Heading}

The following is a list of all permissions, what they enable, and the [warning message]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39} they will incur (if any).

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar7 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB343 .pagebreak aria-label="343" role="doc-pagebreak" title="343"}This permissions list includes values from different browser vendors and different manifest versions. Some permissions may be deprecated.

For consistency, the APIs are displayed inside the [chrome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} namespace. This namespace is valid in all Chromium and Firefox browsers.
::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec17Heading"}
### accessibilityFeatures.modify {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par60 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   [Grants]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40} your extension write access to the [chrome.accessibilityFeatures]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB344 .pagebreak aria-label="344" role="doc-pagebreak" title="344"}Chrome warning message: "Change your accessibility settings"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec18Heading"}
### accessibilityFeatures.read {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par64 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41} read access to the [chrome.accessibilityFeatures]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers

-   Chrome warning message: "Read your accessibility settings"
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec19Heading"}
### aiLanguageModelOriginTrial {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par68 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants [access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42} to the Prompt API, allowing an extension to send natural language requests to Gemini Nano in the browser

-   Available for Google Chrome
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar9 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB345 .pagebreak aria-label="345" role="doc-pagebreak" title="345"}At the time of the writing of this book, Chrome was trialing access to an in-browser [large language model (LLM)]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43}. It\'s included in this book because it\'s likely the trial will closely resemble how extensions will use AI models in future releases. Refer to Chapter [[[9]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_9_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for more details on the Prompt API.
::::
:::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec20Heading"}
### activeTab {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par72 .Para}
The [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45} is commonly misunderstood -- largely in part to its name. Extension developers were experiencing the following issues:

::: UnorderedList
-   Extensions only needed access to the "active tab," meaning the tab that was currently focused by the browser.

-   Extensions needed access to the active tab irrespective of its domain but did not want to incur the off-putting "Read and change all your data on the websites you visit" warning message.

-   Extensions only needed temporary access to the active tab.
:::
::::

From this, the [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission was born. This permission does not allow access to anything new: requesting the [\<all_urls\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission would make the [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission pointless. However, by requesting [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} instead of [\<all_urls\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, the extension is granted extra permissions only after a limited set of user interactions without showing any warning to the user.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par77 .Para}
The allowed user interactions are

::: UnorderedList
-   Executing an action

-   Executing a context menu item

-   Executing a keyboard shortcut command

-   Accepting a suggestion from the Omnibox API
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par82 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB346 .pagebreak aria-label="346" role="doc-pagebreak" title="346"}Once one of these user interactions occurs, the extension is temporarily granted the following extra permissions for that tab:

::: UnorderedList
-   Call [scripting.executeScript()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [scripting.insertCSS()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} on the active tab.

-   Get the URL, title, and favicon for that tab via an API that returns a [tabs.Tab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} object (essentially the same as the [tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission).

-   Intercept network requests in the tab to the tab's main frame origin using the [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API. The extension temporarily gets host permissions for the tab's main frame origin.
:::
::::

These extra permissions only last until the tab is navigated or is closed. This permission is available in Chromium browsers and Firefox.
:::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec21Heading"}
### alarms {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par87 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   [Grants]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46} your extension access to the [chrome.alarms]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec22Heading"}
### background {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par90 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Makes Chrome start up early and shut down late so that extensions can have a longer life

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox
:::
::::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB347 .pagebreak aria-label="347" role="doc-pagebreak" title="347"}When any installed extension has [background]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47} permission, Chrome runs as a background process as soon as the user logs into their computer and before the user launches Chrome. The background permission also makes Chrome continue running after its last window is closed until the user explicitly quits Chrome.

The intended use of the [background]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission is to enable background scripts to perform useful work without being bound to the lifetime of the browser window.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par95 .Para}
*bookmarks*

::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.bookmarks]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Read and change your bookmarks"

-   Firefox warning message: "Read and modify bookmarks"
:::
::::
:::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec23Heading"}
### browserSettings {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par100 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Enables an [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48} to modify certain global browser settings. Each property of this API is a [BrowserSetting]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} object, providing the ability to modify a particular setting.

-   Available for Firefox.

-   Firefox warning message: "Read and modify browser settings".
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec24Heading"}
### browsingData {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par104 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49} access to the [chrome.browsingData]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB348 .pagebreak aria-label="348" role="doc-pagebreak" title="348"}Firefox warning message: "Clear recent browsing history, cookies, and related data"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec25Heading"}
### captivePortal {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par108 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Determines the captive [portal]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51} state of the user's connection. A captive portal is a web page displayed when a user first connects to a Wi-Fi network.

-   Available for Firefox.
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec26Heading"}
### clipboardRead {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par111 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Required if the [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52} uses [document.execCommand(\'paste\')]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Read data you copy and paste"

-   Firefox warning message: "Get data from the clipboard"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec27Heading"}
### clipboardWrite {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par116 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Indicates the [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53} uses [document.execCommand(\'copy\')]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [document.execCommand(\'cut\')]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB349 .pagebreak aria-label="349" role="doc-pagebreak" title="349"}Chrome warning message: "Modify data you copy and paste"

-   Firefox warning message: "Input data to the clipboard"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec28Heading"}
### contentSettings {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par121 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension [access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54} to the [chrome.contentSettings]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Change your settings that control websites' access to features such as cookies, JavaScript, plugins, geolocation, microphone, camera etc."
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec29Heading"}
### contextMenus {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par125 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55} access to the [chrome.contextMenus]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec30Heading"}
### contextualIdentities {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par128 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB350 .pagebreak aria-label="350" role="doc-pagebreak" title="350"}Grants your extension permission to list, [create]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56}, more commonly known as "containers"

-   Available for Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec31Heading"}
### cookies {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par131 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[cookies]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec32Heading"}
### debugger {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par134 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension [access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58} to the [chrome.debugger]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   :::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par137 .Para}
    Multiple Chrome warning messages:

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   "Access the page debugger backend"

    -   "Read and change all your data on the websites you visit"
    :::
    ::::
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec33Heading"}
### declarativeContent {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par140 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension [access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59} to the [chrome.declarativeContent]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec34 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec34Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par143 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension [access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60} to the [chrome.declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers

-   Chrome warning message: "Block content on any page"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec35 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec35Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par147 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB351 .pagebreak aria-label="351" role="doc-pagebreak" title="351"}Grants the extension access to events and [methods]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61} within the [chrome.declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API that return information on declarative rules matched

-   Available for Chromium browsers

-   Chrome warning message: "Read your browsing history"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec36 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec36Heading"}

-   Available for Chromium browsers.

-   No warnings are generated if the extension already has host access.
:::
::::
:::::

-   Grants your extension [access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63} to the [chrome.declarativeWebRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

:::

:::::

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec41 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec41Heading"}

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Manage your downloads"

-   Firefox warning message: "Download files and read and modify the browser's download history"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec42 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec42Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par173 .Para}

-   Chrome warning message: "Open downloaded files"

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB353 .pagebreak aria-label="353" role="doc-pagebreak" title="353"}Firefox warning message: "Open files downloaded to your computer"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec43 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec43Heading"}

::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension [access]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69} to the [chrome.downloads.setUiOptions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers
:::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec44 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec44Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par180 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[favicon]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70} API

-   Chrome warning message: "Read the icons of the websites you visit."

-   Available for Chromium browsers

::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec45 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec45Heading"}

-   Available for Firefox

-   Firefox warning message: "Read the text of all open tabs"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec46 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec46Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par188 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB354 .pagebreak aria-label="354" role="doc-pagebreak" title="354"}Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[fontSettings]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec47 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec47Heading"}
### gcm {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec47Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par191 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73} access to the [chrome.gcm]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers

-   Deprecated in favor of Web Push
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec48 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec48Heading"}
### geolocation {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec48Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par195 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Allows the extension to use the HTML5 [geolocation]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74} API without prompting the user for permission

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Detect your physical location"

-   Firefox warning message: "Access your [location]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75}"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec49 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec49Heading"}
### history {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec49Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par200 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[history]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Read and change your browsing history"

-   Firefox warning message: "Access browsing [history]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77}"
:::
::::
:::::

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec50 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec50Heading"}
### Host Permissions {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec50Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB355 .pagebreak aria-label="355" role="doc-pagebreak" title="355"}[Host permissions]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm78} can either be requested globally or for only a subset of hosts. These permissions can be requested on all browsers, and the warning message displayed may appear in several different ways.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar10 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

It may be possible to avoid declaring any host permissions by using the [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm79}.
::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec51 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec51Heading"}
#### Global Host Permission {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec51Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par207 .Para}
Global host [permission]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm80}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm81} means the extension has access to every host with a permitted protocol. This is appropriate for extensions that need to manage all the browser's pages and traffic all the time. There are several different ways to request this, but they all have the same effect:

::: UnorderedList
-   [http://\*/\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [https://\*/\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [\*://\*/\*]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [\<all_urls\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par212 .Para}
This will show the following warning messages:

::: UnorderedList
-   Chrome warning message: "Read and change all your data on the websites you visit"

-   Firefox warning message: "Access your data for all [websites]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm82}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm83}"
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar11 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB356 .pagebreak aria-label="356" role="doc-pagebreak" title="356"}If your extension uses this permission, your extension will be placed in the manual review queue when submitting to extension marketplaces. As a result, it will take significantly [longer]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm84}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm85} to be approved.
::::
:::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec52 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec52Heading"}
#### Partial Host Permission {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec52Heading .Heading}

Partial host [permission]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm86}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm87} means the extension has access to only hosts that match one or more matchers. This is appropriate for extensions that know ahead of time the set of hosts that it will need to manage or communicate with.

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar12 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Refer to Chapter [[[5]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_5_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for coverage on how matchers work.
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par218 .Para}
The warning [displayed]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm88}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm89} will vary based on how many hosts are covered. A simple single host matcher for [foobar.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} will display the following warnings:

::: UnorderedList
-   Chrome warning message: "Read and change your data on [foobar.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}"

-   Firefox warning message: "Grants the extension access to [[[https://​foobar.​com]{.RefSource}](https://foobar.com)]{.ExternalRef}"
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par221 .Para}
If more than one host is covered, the browser will adjust the warning message based on how many hosts are covered. For example, Firefox also will show the following messages:

::: UnorderedList
-   Access your data for sites in the \[named\] domain

-   Access your data in \# other domains

-   Access your data for \[named site\]

-   Access your [data]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm90}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm91} on \# other sites
:::
::::
:::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec53 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec53Heading"}
### identity {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec53Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par226 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB357 .pagebreak aria-label="357" role="doc-pagebreak" title="357"}Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[identity]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm92} API

-   Warning message: "Know your email address"

-   Available in Chromium browsers and Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec54 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec54Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par230 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your [extension]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm93} access to *only* the user's email address via the [chrome.identity]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API

-   Chrome warning message: "Know your email address"

-   Available in Chromium browsers
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec55 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec55Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par234 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[idle]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm94} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec56 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec56Heading"}
### management {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec56Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par237 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[management]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm95} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Manage your apps, extensions, and themes"

-   Firefox warning message: "Monitor extension usage and manage themes"
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec57 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec57Heading"}
### menus {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec57Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par242 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB358 .pagebreak aria-label="358" role="doc-pagebreak" title="358"}Grants your extension permission to add items to the browser's menu system with the [[menus]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm96} API

-   Available for Firefox
:::
::::

:::: {.FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This API is modeled on Chrome's [contextMenus]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API, which enables Chrome extensions to add items to the browser's context menu. The [menus]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API adds a few features to Chrome's API.
::::
:::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec58 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec58Heading"}
### menus.overrideContext {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec58Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par246 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension permission to hide all default Firefox [menu]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm97} items in favor of providing a custom context menu UI

-   Available for Firefox
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec59 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec59Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par249 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the native messaging [API]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm98}

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Communicate with cooperating native applications"

-   Firefox warning message: "Exchange messages with programs other than [Firefox]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm99}"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec60 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec60Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par254 .Para}

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Display notifications"

-   Firefox warning message: "Display notifications to you"

::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec61 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec61Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par259 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB359 .pagebreak aria-label="359" role="doc-pagebreak" title="359"}Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[offscreen]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm101} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers

::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec62 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec62Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par262 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[pageCapture]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm102} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Read and change all your data on the websites you [visit]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm103}"
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec63 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec63Heading"}
### pkcs11 {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec63Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par266 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   The [pkcs11]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm104} API enables an extension to enumerate PKCS #11 security modules and to make them accessible to the browser as sources of keys and certificates.

-   Available for Firefox.

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB360 .pagebreak aria-label="360" role="doc-pagebreak" title="360"}Firefox warning message: "Provide cryptographic authentication services".
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par270 .Para}
Per Wikipedia:

::: UnorderedList
-   *Most commercial certificate authority (CA) software uses PKCS #11 to access the CA signing key or to enroll user certificates. Cross-platform software that needs to use smart cards uses PKCS #11, such as Mozilla Firefox and OpenSSL*[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm105} *(using an extension).*
:::
::::
:::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec64 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec64Heading"}
### power {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec64Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par272 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[power]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm106} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers
:::
::::
:::::

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   Chrome warning message: "Change your privacy-related settings"

-   Firefox warning message: "Read and modify privacy [settings]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm108}"

::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec66 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec66Heading"}

-   Available for Chromium browsers *only* in the dev channel
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec67 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec67Heading"}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par283 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Grants your extension access to the [chrome.]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[[proxy]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm110}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm111} API

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox

-   []{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB361 .pagebreak aria-label="361" role="doc-pagebreak" title="361"}Chrome warning message: "Read and change all your data on the websites you visit"

-   Firefox warning message: "Control browser [proxy]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm112}[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm113} settings"
:::
::::
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec68 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec68Heading"}
### readingList {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec68Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par288 .Para}
::: UnorderedList

### unlimitedStorage {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec87Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Par359 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   Provides an [unlimited]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm135} quota for storing client-side data, such as databases and local storage files. Without this permission, the extension is limited to 5 MB of local storage.

-   Available for Chromium browsers and Firefox.

-   Firefox warning message: "Store unlimited amount of client-side [data]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm136}".
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_FPar14 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This permission applies to IndexedDB, [chrome.storage.local]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, OPFS, and application cache. It doesn\'t currently work with wildcard subdomains such as http://\*.example.com.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec96 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec96Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_Sec96Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you learned about what permissions are, what they do, and how to declare them. The chapter also covered the difference between required, optional, and host permissions. It also explained all the tricky implications with selecting and changing permissions.

[]{#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml_PB369 .pagebreak aria-label="369" role="doc-pagebreak" title="369"}In the next chapter, we will cover all the different aspects of how extensions can use and modify the browser's network capabilities, strategies for authentication, as well as how extensions can creatively use content scripts to act as an agent of the authenticated user. We will also cover the important differences between [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml}

::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 11. Networking and Authentication {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB371 .pagebreak aria-label="371" role="doc-pagebreak" title="371"}As you wade deeper into browser extension development, you will quickly realize that there are some interesting idiosyncrasies when it comes to making network requests. Although browser extensions and web pages use the same APIs and network layers to send requests, there are fundamental differences that you will need to surmount when building a well-formed browser extension. This chapter will cover some core areas of extension networking: dispatching network requests, authentication, and browser extension networking APIs.

::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Comparing Websites and Extensions {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par3 .Para}
To begin this chapter, let's set the stage by comparing a traditional [website]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1} and a browser extension.

::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Taba .Table}
  Category                                                            Websites                                                                                                                               Browser Extensions (MV3)
  ------------------------------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Origin**                                                          A website has a predictable origin and a user-provided domain.                                                                         Browser extensions are automatically assigned an origin via the extension ID.
  **APIs**[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2}                    Websites can use [XMLHttpRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [fetch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} anywhere.   Background scripts can only use [fetch()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
  **Remote assets**                                                   Remote assets can be served from the same origin without configuring a cross-origin policy.                                            Remote assets must be loaded with a cross-origin policy correctly configured. Scripts cannot be executed from remote origins.
  **Page types**                                                      All web pages on a website can send requests and authenticate in consistent fashion.                                                   Extension UIs, content scripts, and background scripts all have different restrictions for sending requests, for example, content scripts are subject to the host page's cross-origin restrictions, but popups are not.
  **Server requests**                                                 The website can send same-origin requests to the backend.                                                                              If a browser extension uses a backend server, the requests will *always* be cross-origin.
  **Authentication**                                                  No restrictions: the website can use cookie auth, JWT auth, or OAuth anywhere.                                                         Not all forms of authentication work everywhere, for example, service workers can't use cookie authentication in every situation.
  **Long-running requests**[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3}   Any long-running requests will stay alive while a tab is open.                                                                         Long-running requests in background service workers, popups, and content scripts may all be unexpectedly terminated, for example, a popup is closed or a service worker is terminated.
  **Cross-browser**                                                   The website origin remains consistent across browsers.                                                                                 The browser extension origin is different across browsers: [chrome-extension://extensionID]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} vs. [moz-extension://uuid]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::
:::::

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
## Networking Architecture {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB373 .pagebreak aria-label="373" role="doc-pagebreak" title="373"}When it comes to sending network requests, different components of browser extensions are better than others for certain tasks. How you dispatch your network requests will depend on the nature of your browser extension. This section will cover some things to consider when designing your extension.

::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
### Extension UIs {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}
