    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"**webNavigation**\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-4a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm115}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC20 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.webNavigation.onCompleted.addListener**((details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(details);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-4b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm116}
:::
::::
:::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par133 .Para}
*Example console output:*

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC21 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"documentId\": \"7D0F7EBBCB0020C74DA89F2D36C461B4\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"documentLifecycle\": \"active\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"frameId\": 0,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"frameType\": \"outermost_frame\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"parentFrameId\": -1,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"processId\": 1040,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"tabId\": 173953269,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"timeStamp\": 1661549345594.7412,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"url\": \"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page\"
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm117}
:::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec28Heading"}
### The webRequest API {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par134 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB413 .pagebreak aria-label="413" role="doc-pagebreak" title="413"}The [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API allows browser extensions to inspect and modify network requests events from a web page. The API allows an extension to add event handler for the following request [lifecycle events]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm118}:

::: UnorderedList
-   [onActionIgnored]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onAuthRequired]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onBeforeRedirect]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onBeforeRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onBeforeSendHeaders]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onCompleted]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onErrorOccurred]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onHeadersReceived]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onResponseStarted]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}

-   [onSendHeaders]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par145 .Para}
Figure [[11-12](#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Fig12)]{.InternalRef} shows the flow of [events]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm119}.

<figure id="Fig12" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_MO12" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_11_Chapter/534317_2_En_11_Fig12_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Flow chart illustrating a sequence of events in a network request process. The chart begins with &quot;onBeforeRequest,&quot; followed by &quot;onBeforeSendHeaders,&quot; &quot;onSendHeaders,&quot; and &quot;onHeadersReceived.&quot; It then branches to &quot;onResponseStarted&quot; and &quot;onErrorOccurred.&quot; From &quot;onResponseStarted,&quot; it proceeds to &quot;onCompleted.&quot; &quot;onErrorOccurred&quot; connects to &quot;onBeforeRedirect&quot; and &quot;onAuthRequired,&quot; which loops back to earlier stages. Arrows indicate the flow and decision points in the process." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 11-12</span>
<p>The webRequest <span id="534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm120">event flow</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_FPar14 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB414 .pagebreak aria-label="414" role="doc-pagebreak" title="414"}Details of each of these events can be found in the MDN documentation: [[[[https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/API/webRequest)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec29Heading"}
#### Modifying Requests {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par147 .Para}
If you merely wish to inspect the [request]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm121} lifecycle event data, only the [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission is needed. If, however, you wish to potentially modify these requests, you will need an additional [webRequestBlocking]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission. This grants you the ability to perform the following tasks:

::: UnorderedList
-   The extension can **cancel a request** inside the [onBeforeRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [onBeforeSendHeaders]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, or [onAuthRequired]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} blocking handler.

-   []{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB415 .pagebreak aria-label="415" role="doc-pagebreak" title="415"}The extension can **redirect the request** inside the [onBeforeRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [onHeadersReceived]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} blocking handler.

-   The extension can **modify the request headers** inside the [onBeforeSendHeaders]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} blocking [handler]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm122}.

-   The extension can **modify the response headers** in the [onHeadersReceived]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} blocking handler.

-   The extension can **modify the request's authentication credentials** in the [onAuthRequired]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} blocking handler.
:::
::::

The following simple example will block all image requests on Wikipedia. Keep in mind that you must also request URL permissions if you want to inspect or modify requests on that origin.

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_FPar15 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API and Manifest V3 lack support in Google Chrome, so this example is written in Manifest V2. I recommend testing it out in Firefox.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par155 .Para}
::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC22 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Blocking webRequest Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 2,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"scripts\": \[\"background.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"webRequest\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"webRequestBlocking\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"\<all_urls\>\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm123}
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-5a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB416 .pagebreak aria-label="416" role="doc-pagebreak" title="416"}

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC23 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.webRequest.onBeforeRequest**.addListener(
:::

::: FixedLine
  () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **return {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **cancel: true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**;
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  {
:::

::: FixedLine
    urls: \[
:::

::: FixedLine
     \"\*://www.wikipedia.org/portal/wikipedia.org/assets/img/\*\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\[\"blocking\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
);
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-5b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec30Heading"}
#### Fragmentation {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30Heading .Heading}

[Chromium browsers]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm124}[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm125} have ended support for blocking [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in Manifest V3, but Firefox continues to support the API in Manifest V3.
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec31Heading"}
### The declarativeNetRequest API {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB417 .pagebreak aria-label="417" role="doc-pagebreak" title="417"}The [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API [(DNR)]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm126} is billed as the successor to the [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API. The motivation for this shift was to eliminate the blocking JavaScript handlers in favor of a more efficient declarative model that is implemented by the browser. Now, instead of performing modifications inside a JavaScript function, you will supply the "instructions" for each matching request as a JSON config object. For nearly all extensions, DNR is an effective replacement for [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}/[webRequestBlocking]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec32Heading"}
#### Permissions {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par158 .Para}
The [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API can be used with three different [permissions]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm127}:

::: UnorderedList
-   The [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission allows your extension to define DNR rules, but you will still need to request host permissions to apply rules on specific origins.

-   The [declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission can be used instead of the [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission. It allows you to block or upgrade requests on any origin without requesting host permissions. Redirects or header modification will still require specific host permissions.

-   The [declarativeNetRequestFeedback]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm128} can be added as a supplement to the previous two. With it, your extension can access functions and events that return information on rules matched.
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec33Heading"}
#### Rule and Ruleset Structure {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par162 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB418 .pagebreak aria-label="418" role="doc-pagebreak" title="418"}All DNR rules take the form of a JSON object. Only one rule will ever be applied per request, per extension. Each [rule]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm129} object is structured as follows:

::: UnorderedList
-   The [id]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} must be a unique positive integer.

-   The [priority]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is an optional integer that can be used to control which rule is applied in scenarios where multiple rules match a request.

-   The [condition]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is an object that describes when this rule should be applied. With it, you can include or exclude domains, domain types, tab IDs, and resource types.

-   The [action]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is an object that describes what this rule does. It includes a [type]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, which can be [block]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [redirect]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [allow]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [upgradeScheme]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [modifyHeaders]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, or [allowAllRequests]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, and various properties that configure how the rule should behave.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par167 .Para}
DNR rules are defined in one of two ways:

::: UnorderedList
-   **Static rulesets** are arrays of rule objects in JSON files. Ruleset files can be provided in the manifest or added programmatically. They can also be enabled or disabled on demand. Static [rulesets]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm130} can only ever exist as immutable files packaged with the extension. The total number of static rules is limited by the browser.

-   **Dynamic** [**rules**]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm131} are rule objects that are exclusively programmatically created. They can be added, removed, modified, and enabled or disabled. A subset of dynamic rules are **session rules**, which are dynamic rules that are not persisted across browsers. The total number of dynamic rules is limited by the browser.
:::
::::

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB419 .pagebreak aria-label="419" role="doc-pagebreak" title="419"}Whether static or dynamic, a DNR rule is treated the same by the browser.
:::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec34 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec34Heading"}
#### Safe/Unsafe Rules {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec34Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par171 .Para}
DNR rules are split into "Safe" and "Unsafe" [rules]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm132}.

::: UnorderedList
-   **Safe rules** are rules with an action of [block]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [allow]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, [allowAllRequests,]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [upgradeScheme]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.

-   **Unsafe rules** are rules with an action of [redirect]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [modifyHeaders]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par174 .Para}
Safe rules offer important advantages:

::: UnorderedList
-   Safe rules are subject to a much higher dynamic rules quota. (At the time of the writing of this edition, the quota for safe [rules]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm133} was 30,000, compared to 5,000 for unsafe rules.)

-   Extension updates that *only* change safe rules do not require review and can be published *instantly*.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_FPar16 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

The [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API has quite a bit of depth to it. Refer to the Google Chrome [documentation]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm134} for details on all its features: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/reference/api/declarativeNetRequest)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec35 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec35Heading"}
#### Static Rulesets {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec35Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par178 .Para}
Let's examine [static rulesets]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm135} by examining the following simple extension that can block images on Wikipedia:[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB420 .pagebreak aria-label="420" role="doc-pagebreak" title="420"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC24 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Static DNR Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"declarative_net_request\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"rule_resources\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"id\": \"ruleset_1\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"enabled\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"path\": \"rules_1.json\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\]**[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm136}
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": **\[\"declarativeNetRequest\"**\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"host_permissions\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"\*://\*.wikipedia.org/\*\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"\*://\*.wikimedia.org/\*\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-6a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm137}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC25 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const RULESET_ID = \"ruleset_1\";
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener(async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const enabled_rulesets =
:::

::: FixedLine
    await **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.getEnabledRulesets();**
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (enabled_rulesets.includes(RULESET_ID)) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.updateEnabledRulesets({**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **disableRulesetIds: \[RULESET_ID\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
  } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.updateEnabledRulesets({**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **enableRulesetIds: \[RULESET_ID\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm138}
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Toggled ruleset\");
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-6b]{.CaptionNumber}

[background.js]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm139}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB421 .pagebreak aria-label="421" role="doc-pagebreak" title="421"}

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC26 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\[
:::

::: FixedLine
  {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"id\": 1,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"priority\": 1,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"type\": \"block\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"condition\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"domains\": \[\"wikipedia.org\", \"wikimedia.org\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"resourceTypes\": \[\"image\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
\]
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-6c]{.CaptionNumber}

[rules_1.json]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm140}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

Load this extension and navigate to [wikipedia.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. The initial extension state will enable the ruleset. You will notice that images do not load. Click the [extension's toolbar button]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm141} to toggle the ruleset. When you reload Wikipedia again, images should load normally.

::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par180 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB422 .pagebreak aria-label="422" role="doc-pagebreak" title="422"}You may notice that a new [\_metadata]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} directory has appeared inside your local development folder (Figure [[11-13](#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Fig13)]{.InternalRef}). As the name suggests, the [binary file]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm142} inside the directory is how the browser keeps track of active rulesets. Feel free to ignore it but ensure you do not commit it to version control or add it to a production build.

<figure id="Fig13" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_MO13" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_11_Chapter/534317_2_En_11_Fig13_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle9" alt="File directory screenshot showing folders and files. The folders are &quot;_metadata&quot; and &quot;generated_indexed_rulesets.&quot; The files listed are &quot;_ruleset1,&quot; &quot;background.js,&quot; &quot;manifest.json,&quot; and &quot;rules_1.json.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 11-13</span>
<p>Automatically generated indexed ruleset <span id="534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm143">binary</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec36 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec36Heading"}
#### Dynamic Rules {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec36Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Par181 .Para}
[Dynamic rules]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm144} are very similar to static rulesets. In similar fashion, they can be queried, added, and removed on demand. In this example, let's instead write a dynamic redirect rule:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC27 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Dynamic DNR Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"**declarativeNetRequest**\"\],
:::
:::::::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\"host_permissions\": \[\"\*://\*.wikipedia.org/\*\", \"\*://\*.wikimedia.org/\*\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-7a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB423 .pagebreak aria-label="423" role="doc-pagebreak" title="423"}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PC28 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**const RULE_ID = 1;**
:::

::: FixedLine
**const RULE_1 = {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **id: RULE_ID,**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **priority: 1,**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **action: {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **type: \"redirect\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **redirect: {**
:::
::::::::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**url: \"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Hot_dog_with_mustard.png/1920px-Hot_dog_with_mustard.png\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **condition: {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **domains: \[\"wikipedia.org\", \"wikimedia.org\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **resourceTypes: \[\"image\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
**};**[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm145}
:::
:::::::::::

::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.action.onClicked.addListener(async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **const dynamic_rules = await**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.getDynamicRules();**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **if (dynamic_rules.find((rule) =\> rule.id === 1)) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.updateDynamicRules({**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **removeRuleIds: RULE_ID,**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **} else {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.updateDynamicRules({**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **addRules: \[RULE_1\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
  console.log(\"Toggled rule\");
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 11-7b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_PB424 .pagebreak aria-label="424" role="doc-pagebreak" title="424"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

Load this [extension]{#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm146} and navigate to [wikipedia.org]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. You will notice that images load normally. Click the extension's toolbar button and reload Wikipedia again. All images on Wikipedia should now be a hot dog.
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec37 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec37Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_11_Chapter.xhtml_Sec37Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, we explored a variety of modalities in which browser extensions can send network requests. We reviewed all the different components and how they can and should send network requests. Next, we discussed authentication strategies, including in-depth coverage on how to implement OAuth in browser extensions. Finally, we discussed how to use the major WebExtensions APIs that can inspect and manipulate traffic in the browser.

In the next chapter, we will walk through a series of tutorials on how to build different classes of browser extensions.
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml}

:::::

::: MainTitleSection

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB425 .pagebreak aria-label="425" role="doc-pagebreak" title="425"}In this chapter, we'll walk through building a variety of browser extensions, each chosen to highlight a distinct class of functionality that's either commonly used or especially powerful. The goal is to showcase practical, real-world, and *simple* patterns that reflect how modern extensions are written.

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

In addition to the [tutorials]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1} in this chapter, this book includes an open source companion extension called **Example Chrome Extension**, available on the Chrome Web Store. It contains working examples of many commonly used extension APIs (source code: [[[[https://github.com/classvsoftware/example-chrome-extension]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional};]{.RefSource}](https://github.com/classvsoftware/example-chrome-extension%3B)]{.ExternalRef} install link: [[[[https://buildingbrowserextensions.com/b2x]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://buildingbrowserextensions.com/b2x)]{.ExternalRef}).
::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Introduction {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par4 .Para}
To avoid over-complicating each topic and overwhelming the reader, this chapter's [tutorials]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2} will cleave to a few key principles:

::: UnorderedList
-   Tutorials will use Manifest V3 and be designed for use in a Chromium browser.

-   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB426 .pagebreak aria-label="426" role="doc-pagebreak" title="426"}Tutorials will not use compilers, TypeScript, SCSS, or JS frameworks, only raw HTML, JS, and CSS that can be directly loaded into the browser.

-   Each tutorial will focus on only a handful of extension APIs.
:::
::::

In each tutorial, we'll discuss stylistic choices for architecture, important concepts to understand, and notable code snippets. To improve readability and avoid including large sections of boilerplate code, the full source code for each tutorial will be provided in the book's companion code repository.

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Some [tutorials]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3} require signing up for third-party services, using external APIs, or enabling experimental features in Chrome. These are clearly noted where applicable. However, every example in this chapter is fully self-contained and can be built entirely inside a browser extension project.
::::
:::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
## Notepad {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par10 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   Popups

-   Background scripts

-   Storage API

-   Context menu API
:::
::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par15 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB427 .pagebreak aria-label="427" role="doc-pagebreak" title="427"}This tutorial covers how to build a simple [notepad]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4} extension using Chrome's extension APIs. The extension allows users to

::: UnorderedList
-   Write and edit text notes in a popup

-   Store notes persistently using the Chrome Storage API

-   Right-click any web page to quickly save selected text or the page URL as a note
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par19 .Para}
This notepad extension uses two primary [components]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5}: the popup interface and the background script.

::: UnorderedList
-   The popup allows users to type, view, and delete notes. New notes are saved through a button click, and all saved notes are displayed in a scrollable list.

-   The background script manages the context menus. When the extension is installed, it adds context menu options to save selected text or the current page URL.
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
#### Storage {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

Notes are [stored]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6} using [chrome.storage.local]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, which persists note content across same-browser sessions and has generous storage limits, but will *not* sync between browsers. The popup listens to [chrome.storage.onChanged]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} events and re-renders the stored notes list whenever stored data changes.

Chrome storage is a simple asynchronous key-value store. In this project, all notes are stored as an array of strings under a single key. Whenever a user adds or deletes a note, the entire array is read, updated in memory, and written back to storage. This approach simplifies the data model and allows the extension to listen for changes on just one key, []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB428 .pagebreak aria-label="428" role="doc-pagebreak" title="428"}simplifying synchronization between components like the popup and background script. While it rewrites the whole list with each update, this tradeoff is acceptable given the small size and low frequency of changes in a typical notepad use case.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
#### Context Menus {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

The [context menu]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7} functionality is implemented using the [chrome.contextMenus]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API, which lets extensions add custom right-click actions based on the user's current interaction. This extension defines two menu items: one with the [\"selection\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} context to capture highlighted text and another with the [\"page\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} context to save the current page URL.

When a user selects one of these options, the background script receives a click event containing contextual details such as selected text or the page URL and title. The listener uses this information to create and [store]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8} new notes.
:::
:::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par26 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares a background script, a popup, and permissions for context menus and storage. The manifest sets "type": "[module\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} to treat the service worker as an ES module, allowing for the use of [import]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} statements to load other modules. The manifest is as follows:

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"name\": \"Notepad\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"permissions\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"storage\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"contextMenus\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"service_worker\": **\"background.js\"**,
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"type\": \"module\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"default_popup\": **\"popup.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::
:::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB429 .pagebreak aria-label="429" role="doc-pagebreak" title="429"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par27 .Para}
We're going to be using the same storage key across the application, so let's define it inside a consts file that can be used in both the popup and background:

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
export const STORAGE_KEY_NOTES = \"notes\";
:::

::: FixedLine
export const MENU_ID_SELECTION = \"saveSelection\";
:::

::: FixedLine
export const MENU_ID_URL = \"savePageUrl\";
:::

::: FixedLine
export const MENU_ID_LINK = \"saveLinkUrl\";
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

[consts.js]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10}
:::
::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par28 .Para}
Our [popup]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11} will be tasked with adding and removing notes, so we'll define some utils to separate the storage API calls from the rest of the code:

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const addNote = (text) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.storage.local.get(\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\], (res) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    const notes = res\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\] \|\| \[\];
:::

::: FixedLine
    notes.push(text);
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.storage.local.set({ \[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\]: notes });**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12}
:::

::: FixedLine
};
:::
::::::::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const deleteNote = (index) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.storage.local.get(\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\], (res) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    const notes = res\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\] \|\| \[\];
:::

::: FixedLine
    notes.splice(index, 1);
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.storage.local.set({ \[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\]: notes });**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
};
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-1c]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB430 .pagebreak aria-label="430" role="doc-pagebreak" title="430"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par29 .Para}
Next, we'll need to render the current [notes]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13} state on initial render and whenever we detect a change in the storage value:

::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function renderNotes(notes) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Renders the popup HTML text and buttons
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14}
:::
::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.storage.onChanged.addListener((changes)** =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (**changes\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\]**) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    renderNotes(changes\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\].newValue \|\| \[\]);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.storage.local.get(\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\]**, (res) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  renderNotes(res\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\] \|\| \[\]);
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-1d]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par30 .Para}
In the background [script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}, we'll configure the extension to register the context menu options. Each option defines the [contexts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} that decide when each option will appear. In this case, we want to show either when text is selected, when the page is right-clicked, or when a link is right-clicked. We'll use the [onInstalled]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} event to ensure this only occurs a single time:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB431 .pagebreak aria-label="431" role="doc-pagebreak" title="431"}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener**(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.contextMenus.create**({
:::

::: FixedLine
    id: MENU_ID_SELECTION,
:::

::: FixedLine
    title: \"Save selected text to Notepad\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    contexts: **\[\"selection\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
:::::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **chrome.contextMenus.create**({
:::

::: FixedLine
    id: MENU_ID_URL,
:::

::: FixedLine
    title: \"Save page URL to Notepad\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    contexts: **\[\"page\", \"selection\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
::::::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **chrome.contextMenus.create**({
:::

::: FixedLine
    id: MENU_ID_LINK,
:::

::: FixedLine
    title: \"Save link URL to Notepad\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    contexts: **\[\"link\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16}
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-1e]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par31 .Para}
This adds the options, but we need to handle events fired whenever a user clicks them. Using the [id]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} values assigned previously, we can extract the desired textual values from the event and append it to our stored notes list:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB432 .pagebreak aria-label="432" role="doc-pagebreak" title="432"}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.contextMenus.onClicked.addListener**((info, tab) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  let noteText = \"\";
:::
:::::

:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  switch (info.menuItemId) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **case MENU_ID_SELECTION:**
:::

::: FixedLine
      noteText = \`\[\${**tab.title**}\] \${**info.selectionText**}\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
    **case MENU_ID_URL:**
:::

::: FixedLine
      noteText = **info.pageUrl**;
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
    case **MENU_ID_LINK**:
:::

::: FixedLine
      noteText = **info.linkUrl**;
:::

::: FixedLine
      break;
:::

::: FixedLine
    default:
:::

::: FixedLine
      return;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17}
:::
::::::::::::::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  chrome.storage.local.get(\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\], (res) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    const notes = res\[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\] \|\| \[\];
:::

::: FixedLine
    notes.push(noteText);
:::

::: FixedLine
    chrome.storage.local.set({ \[STORAGE_KEY_NOTES\]: notes });
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-1f]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB433 .pagebreak aria-label="433" role="doc-pagebreak" title="433"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par32 .Para}
After installing the extension, you can verify that all [features]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18} are working correctly by interacting with the popup and context menu options. The extension should allow you to create, view, and delete notes through the popup, as well as save content directly from any web page using the right-click menu. Use the following steps to test its functionality:

::: UnorderedList
-   Open the extension popup and type a sample note, then click the save button to add it to the list.

-   Close and reopen the popup to confirm the note persists across sessions.

-   Highlight some text on a web page, right-click it, and select "Save selected text to Notepad" to add it as a note.

-   Right-click anywhere on a page (without highlighting text) and choose "Save page URL to Notepad" to store the current URL.

-   Right-click on any hyperlink and select "Save link URL to Notepad" to save the link's destination.

-   Reopen the popup to confirm each of these [actions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19} successfully added new notes to the list.

-   Click the delete icon next to a note to ensure removal works correctly.
:::
::::
:::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
## Tab Manager {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par40 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   Side panels

-   Tabs API
:::
::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par43 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB434 .pagebreak aria-label="434" role="doc-pagebreak" title="434"}This tutorial demonstrates how to build a real-time [tab manager]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20}. The extension displays a searchable list of all open tabs in a side panel. The extension requires no background script and runs entirely in the context of the side [panel]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21}. The extension allows users to

::: UnorderedList
-   Search by tab title or URL

-   Click a tab to bring it into focus

-   Close tabs directly from the panel

-   See the tab list update live as tabs open, update, or close
:::
::::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par48 .Para}
This tab manager extension is built around a single [component]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22}: a global side panel interface.

::: UnorderedList
-   The side panel displays a list of all open browser tabs, including each tab's title and URL. Users can search the list using a text input that filters results.

-   Each tab entry includes a clickable title that activates the tab and a close button that removes it. The tab list updates automatically in response to tab creation, updates, and removals.
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
#### Side Panel {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB435 .pagebreak aria-label="435" role="doc-pagebreak" title="435"}The extension's [side panel]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23} is opened when the user clicks the extension icon in the Chrome toolbar. This triggers the side panel to display the panel in the current window. Unlike per-tab panels, the global panel maintains a consistent view across tabs and doesn't require additional configuration or state management.

Because the panel runs in the context of an extension page, it has full access to privileged extension [APIs]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24} like [chrome.tabs]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This enables the panel to read, filter, and control tabs directly, without the need for a background script or message passing.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
#### Tabs {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

The [tab]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25} list is kept in sync with the browser's actual state by listening to Chrome's tabs API events. When a tab is opened, updated (e.g., its title or URL changes), or closed, the extension refreshes its internal tab list and re-renders the panel to reflect the current state. This ensures the interface stays accurate without requiring user refresh. The filtering logic is applied dynamically based on the current input in the search field. Any change to the tab data or the filter criteria will re-run the filtering process and update the visible list.
:::
:::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par54 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares a side panel and permissions for tabs and side panel:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB436 .pagebreak aria-label="436" role="doc-pagebreak" title="436"}

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Tab Manager\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": **\[\"tabs\", \"sidePanel\"\]**,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"side_panel\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_path\": \"panel.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {}
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26}
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-2a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par55 .Para}
Our background [script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28} doesn't need to do much, we just need it to kick open the side panel when the extension icon is clicked:

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.sidePanel
:::

::: FixedLine
  .setPanelBehavior({ openPanelOnActionClick: true })
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-2b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
::::::::
:::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par56 .Para}
Inside the [side panel]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29}, we'll set up a global tab list that will be used to store a copy of whatever tabs were most recently returned and a global filter string that records whatever the most recent query was. We'll set up a render and filter function to add the tabs to the page. This way, the tabs and filter independently updating will always display fresh data in the side panel.[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB437 .pagebreak aria-label="437" role="doc-pagebreak" title="437"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
let allTabs = \[\];
:::

::: FixedLine
let currentFilter = \"\";
:::
:::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function renderTabs(tabs) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Renders the tab list inside the side panel
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::

:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function filterTabs() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const q = currentFilter.toLowerCase();
:::

::: FixedLine
  const filtered = allTabs.filter(
:::

::: FixedLine
    (tab) =\>
:::

::: FixedLine
      (tab.title && tab.title.toLowerCase().includes(q)) \|\|
:::

::: FixedLine
      (tab.url && tab.url.toLowerCase().includes(q))
:::

::: FixedLine
  );
:::

::: FixedLine
  **renderTabs(filtered);**
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30}
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-2c]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par57 .Para}
To update the tab list and search filter, we'll define an update tabs function to retrieve a list of all active [tabs]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31} and separately listen for input events:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function updateTabList() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.tabs.query({}, (tabs) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **allTabs = tabs;**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **filterTabs();**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
searchInput.addEventListener(\"input\", () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  currentFilter = searchInput.value;
:::

::: FixedLine
  **filterTabs();**
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-2d]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB438 .pagebreak aria-label="438" role="doc-pagebreak" title="438"}
::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par58 .Para}
To keep the tabs list fresh, we'll call the [update function]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32} when the side panel opens and on every relevant tab event:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
updateTabList();[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33}
:::
::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.tabs.**onCreated**.addListener(updateTabList);
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.tabs.**onRemoved**.addListener(updateTabList);
:::

::: FixedLine
chrome.tabs.**onUpdated**.addListener(updateTabList);
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-2e]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par59 .Para}
After installing the extension, you can confirm it works by opening the side panel and interacting with the tab list. The panel should display an up-to-date view of all open tabs and allow you to search, activate, and close them in real time. Use the following actions to verify that everything [functions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34} as expected:

::: UnorderedList
-   Click the extension icon in the toolbar to open the side panel.

-   Type in the search input to filter the list by matching tab titles or URLs.

-   Click a tab title in the list to bring that tab into focus.

-   Click the close button next to a tab to immediately remove it from both the panel and the browser.

-   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB439 .pagebreak aria-label="439" role="doc-pagebreak" title="439"}Open new tabs or close existing ones in the browser and verify that the list updates automatically.

-   [Navigate]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35} to a different page in one of the open tabs and confirm that the title and URL in the panel update accordingly.
:::
::::
:::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec16Heading"}
## Screenshot Capture {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par66 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   Screenshot capture API

-   Active tab permission

-   Keyboard shortcuts

-   Extension pages
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec17Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17Heading .Heading}

This extension allows users to take a screenshot of the current visible tab using a keyboard shortcut. When the shortcut is triggered, the extension [captures]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36} the tab as an image, stores it temporarily, and opens a new tab that displays the screenshot along with a download button. The screenshot viewer is implemented as a simple extension page that displays the image and enables downloading it to the local filesystem.
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec18Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18Heading .Heading}

This extension consists of three main [components]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}: a background service worker, a keyboard shortcut handler, and a static HTML page that acts as the screenshot viewer.

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec19Heading"}
#### Keyboard Shortcut {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB440 .pagebreak aria-label="440" role="doc-pagebreak" title="440"}The [keyboard shortcut]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38} is defined using the [commands]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} key in the extension manifest. When the user presses the assigned key combination (e.g., [Ctrl+Shift+S]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}), the extension receives a [chrome.commands.onCommand]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} event. This event is captured by the background script, which then performs the screenshot logic.
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec20Heading"}
### Screenshot Capture {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20Heading .Heading}

When the shortcut is triggered, the extension captures an [image]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39} of the current tab and records it as a data URL. This image data is then stored in extension local storage so that it can be retrieved by the screenshot viewer page. This API uses the [\"activeTab\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [permission]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40}, which grants temporary access to the active tab in response to a user gesture. This conveniently allows us to avoid requesting the much more sensitive [\"tabs\"]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permissions, since we only care about the currently visible tab anyway.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec21Heading"}
### Screenshot Viewer {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21Heading .Heading}

After capturing the image, the background script opens the internal extension page [screenshot.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in a new [tab]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41}. This page loads the stored screenshot and displays it to the user with the option to download the file.
:::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec22Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par76 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares a background script, a screenshot command, and [permissions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42} for active tab and storage:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB441 .pagebreak aria-label="441" role="doc-pagebreak" title="441"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Screenshot Capture\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": **\[\"activeTab\", \"storage\"\]**,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"commands\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"take-screenshot\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"suggested_key\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"default\": \"Ctrl+Shift+S\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"description\": \"Capture the current tab\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-3a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par77 .Para}
Next, we'll configure the background [script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44} so the screenshot command event is triggered; the extension will capture the visible tab and save it in the extension's storage:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.commands.onCommand.addListener**(async (command) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (command === \"take-screenshot\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
    try {
:::

::: FixedLine
      **const imgData = await chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab();**
:::
:::::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
      await chrome.storage.local.set({ screenshot: **imgData** });
:::

::: FixedLine
      const url = chrome.runtime.getURL(\"screenshot.html\");
:::

::: FixedLine
      await chrome.tabs.create({ url });
:::

::: FixedLine
    } catch (e) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      console.error(\"Failed to capture screenshot:\", e);
:::

::: FixedLine
    }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45}
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-3b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB442 .pagebreak aria-label="442" role="doc-pagebreak" title="442"}
::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par78 .Para}
Finally, when the screenshot [page]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46} loads, we'll retrieve the image data from storage and display it alongside a download button:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC14 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.storage.local.get(\[\"screenshot\"\]**, (res) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const dataUrl = res.screenshot;
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (dataUrl) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **img.src = dataUrl;**
:::
:::::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    button.onclick = () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      const a = document.createElement(\"a\");
:::

::: FixedLine
      a.href = dataUrl;
:::

::: FixedLine
      a.download = \`screenshot-\${Date.now()}.png\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
      a.click();
:::

::: FixedLine
    };
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47}
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-3c]{.CaptionNumber}

screenshot.js
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec23Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par79 .Para}
After installing the extension, you can verify that the screenshot functionality works correctly by using the keyboard shortcut and confirming the captured image is displayed and downloadable. The extension captures the visible portion of the current tab and opens a new page to preview and download the screenshot. Try the following steps to [test]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48} it:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB443 .pagebreak aria-label="443" role="doc-pagebreak" title="443"}

::: UnorderedList
-   Press [Ctrl+Shift+S]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} (or your system's equivalent shortcut) while viewing any web page to capture a screenshot.

-   Click the download button to save the image and confirm that the file downloads as a [.png]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} with a timestamped file name.
:::
::::
:::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec24Heading"}
## Password Manager {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par82 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   Content scripts

-   Messaging API

-   Sniffing network traffic
:::
::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec25Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25Heading .Heading}

This tutorial covers how to build a minimal [password manager]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49}. It automatically detects when users submit login forms, captures the username and password fields, and stores the credentials along with the hostname where they were used. When the user revisits the same site, the extension popup shows any saved credentials for that hostname. The user can click a button to autofill them into the login form. All credential data is stored locally in the extension using the Chrome Storage API.

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

To preserve simplicity, this example stores credentials in plaintext. An extension's storage is not visible from web pages or other extensions, but it is visible to the host system. In a real password manager extension, you would need to add additional encryption layers to protect the credentials.
::::
:::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec26Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB444 .pagebreak aria-label="444" role="doc-pagebreak" title="444"}This password manager consists of three [components]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50}: a background script that monitors outgoing login data, a popup UI that shows saved credentials for the current site, and a content script that performs input field autofill when the user clicks the "Fill" button.

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec27Heading"}
#### Background Script {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27Heading .Heading}

The [background]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51} service worker listens for outgoing form submissions. For the sake of simplifying this tutorial, we won't exhaustively search outgoing network traffic for credentials; we'll just look for the common username/password field keys in outgoing payloads.

When both a username and password are found, the extension stores them together under a key tied to the hostname of the originating site. This is an important security measure that password managers provide: to prevent phishing, they will only offer to fill passwords on the same hostname they were recorded from.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec28Heading"}
#### Popup {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28Heading .Heading}

The popup [page]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52} displays a list of stored credentials when visiting a site that matches a stored hostname. It queries the current active tab's hostname and then checks the stored credential list for any credentials tied to that key. Each credential entry is shown with a "Fill" button that sends the credentials to the active tab via a runtime message.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec29Heading"}
#### Content Script {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29Heading .Heading}

The [content script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53} listens for messages from the popup. When it receives a [fillCredentials]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} message, it searches for the relevant [username]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [password]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} input fields in the page. If it finds them, it fills in the values and dispatches input and change events to mimic real user input.
:::
::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec30Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par93 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB445 .pagebreak aria-label="445" role="doc-pagebreak" title="445"}We'll begin with a manifest that declares a background script, a popup, a universally injected content script, and [permissions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54} for all URLs, storage, and [webRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC15 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Password Manager\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": **\[\"storage\", \"webRequest\"\]**,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"host_permissions\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"default_popup\": \"popup.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\"js\": \[\"content.js\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-4a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par94 .Para}
In the background [script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56}, we'll listen to all outgoing requests and throw out anything that doesn't look like a form submission. If a form body is submitted and contains credentials, we'll store it.[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB446 .pagebreak aria-label="446" role="doc-pagebreak" title="446"}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC16 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.webRequest.onBeforeRequest**.addListener(
:::

::: FixedLine
  async (details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (!details.requestBody \|\| !details.initiator) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      return;
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::
::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    const formData = details.requestBody.formData;
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (!formData) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      return;
:::

::: FixedLine
    }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57}
:::
:::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    const encoded = new URLSearchParams();
:::

::: FixedLine
    for (const \[key, values\] of Object.entries(formData)) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      encoded.append(key, values\[0\]);
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::
:::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    const credentials = extractCredentials(encoded.toString());
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (!credentials) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      return;
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::
:::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    **const url = new URL(details.initiator);**
:::
::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    storeCredentials(url, credentials);
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **urls: \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **types: \[\"xmlhttprequest\", \"main_frame\", \"sub_frame\"\],**
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \[\"**requestBody**\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
);[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58}
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-4b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB447 .pagebreak aria-label="447" role="doc-pagebreak" title="447"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par95 .Para}
Next, we'll configure the content [script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59} that is injected in every page. It will listen for an event sent from the extension that provides credentials sent from extension storage. The credentials are then programmatically injected into a login form if it exists.

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC17 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener**((msg, sender, \_) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (msg.type !== \"fillCredentials\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::
:::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const { usernameInput, passwordInput } =
:::

::: FixedLine
      findLoginInputs(document);
:::
:::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  if (usernameInput) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    fillInput(usernameInput, msg.username);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (passwordInput) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    fillInput(passwordInput, msg.password);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60}
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-4c]{.CaptionNumber}

content.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par96 .Para}
In the [popup]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61}, we'll load the list of stored credentials that match the current hostname. When the user clicks "Fill" for a specific credential set, the popup will send a message to the content script and pass the selected credential set.[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB448 .pagebreak aria-label="448" role="doc-pagebreak" title="448"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC18 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
// \...
:::
::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function showLogins() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const hostname = await getCurrentHostname();
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (!hostname) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::
::::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const key = \`credentials\_\${hostname}\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
  const result = await chrome.storage.local.get(key);
:::

::: FixedLine
  const list = result\[key\] \|\| \[\];
:::
::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  list.forEach(({ username, password }) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // \...
:::
:::::

:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    fillBtn.onclick = () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      **chrome.tabs.query({**
:::

::: FixedLine
          **active: true, currentWindow: true**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **}, (\[tab\]) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
          **chrome.tabs.sendMessage(tab.id, {**
:::

::: FixedLine
            **type: \"fillCredentials\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
            **username,**
:::

::: FixedLine
            **password,**
:::

::: FixedLine
          **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
    };
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62}
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
showLogins();
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-4d]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB449 .pagebreak aria-label="449" role="doc-pagebreak" title="449"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec31Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par97 .Para}
After installing the extension, you can [test]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63} its functionality by submitting login forms, revisiting the same sites, and using the popup to autofill stored credentials. These actions will trigger the core features implemented in the background, content, and popup scripts:

::: UnorderedList
-   Visit a login page (suggestion: [reddit.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} was used in my testing) and submit a form that includes a username and password field.

-   Open the extension popup on the same site after submitting the form to view saved credentials tied to that hostname.

-   Click the "Fill" button next to a saved credential in the popup to autofill the login form fields on the page. Verify that the fields are populated programmatically, complete with input and change events.

-   Repeat the process on a different site and confirm that only the credentials [associated]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm64} with that specific hostname are shown in the popup.
:::
::::
:::::
