::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec32Heading"}
## Ad Blocker {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par102 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   declarativeNetworkRequest API

-   Toolbar icon API
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec33Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB450 .pagebreak aria-label="450" role="doc-pagebreak" title="450"}This tutorial shows how to build a simple [ad blocker]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm65}. It detects and blocks requests to a list of well-known ad network domains. The extension adds a toolbar icon that allows users to toggle ad blocking on or off. A badge label reflects the current state, showing ON in green when active and OFF in red when disabled.
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec34 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec34Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec34Heading .Heading}

This ad blocker consists of two [parts]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm66}: a static ruleset to block known ad URLs and a background service worker that manages the toggle state and updates the badge.

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec35 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec35Heading"}
#### DNR Rules {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec35Heading .Heading}

The block list includes domains commonly used by ad networks. These domains are included in the extension's [declarative_net_request]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} rule resources, using a simple list of URL patterns that target ad delivery and tracking services. The [rules]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm67} are bundled in a [static rules.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} file and declared in the manifest.
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec36 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec36Heading"}
### Toggling Rules {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec36Heading .Heading}

Since we're just using a single ruleset, we can [toggle]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm68} that ruleset on and off and reflect that state in the rest of the extension. The toolbar icon badge can be updated each time the ruleset is toggled.
:::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec37 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec37Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec37Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par109 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares a background script, DNR and host access DNR permissions, and an initially enabled ruleset:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB451 .pagebreak aria-label="451" role="doc-pagebreak" title="451"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC19 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Ad Blocker\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"declarativeNetRequest\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"host_permissions\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {},
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"declarative_net_request\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"rule_resources\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **{**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"id\": \"adblock_rules\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"enabled\": true,**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **\"path\": \"rules.json\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69}
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-5a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par110 .Para}
The ruleset will be a long list of DNR [rules]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm71} structured to block ad and tracking requests. This is accomplished as follows:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB452 .pagebreak aria-label="452" role="doc-pagebreak" title="452"}

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC20 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\[
:::

::: FixedLine
  {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"id\": 1,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"priority\": 1,
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"action\": { \"type\": \"block\" },**
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"condition\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"urlFilter\": **\"doubleclick.net\"**,
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"resourceTypes\": **\[\"script\", \"image\", \"xmlhttprequest\"\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
    }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72}
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::

::: FixedLine
\]
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-5b]{.CaptionNumber}

rules.json (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par111 .Para}
In the [background script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73}, we'll define four [functions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74}:

::: UnorderedList
-   [setBadge()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75} to use the action button badge API to configure its appearance

-   [isRulesetEnabled()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76} to check the enabled [rulesets]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77}

-   [toggleAdblock()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm78} to update

-   [syncBadge()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm79} as an entrypoint to perform the badge update
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par116 .Para}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC21 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const RULESET_ID = \"adblock_rules\";
:::
::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function setBadge(enabled) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.action.setBadgeText**(
:::

::: FixedLine
      { text: enabled ? \"ON\" : \"OFF\" });
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.action.setBadgeBackgroundColor**(
:::

::: FixedLine
      { color: enabled ? \"#4CAF50\" : \"#F44336\" });
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function isRulesetEnabled() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const enabledRules =
:::

::: FixedLine
      await **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.getEnabledRulesets**();
:::

::: FixedLine
  return enabledRules.includes(RULESET_ID);
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm80}
:::
::::::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function toggleAdblock() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const enabled = await isRulesetEnabled();
:::

::: FixedLine
  await **chrome.declarativeNetRequest.updateEnabledRulesets**({
:::

::: FixedLine
    enableRulesetIds: enabled ? \[\] : \[RULESET_ID\],
:::

::: FixedLine
    disableRulesetIds: enabled ? \[RULESET_ID\] : \[\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
  setBadge(!enabled);
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function syncBadge() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const enabled = await isRulesetEnabled();
:::

::: FixedLine
  setBadge(enabled);
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm81}
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-5c]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB453 .pagebreak aria-label="453" role="doc-pagebreak" title="453"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par117 .Para}
Finally, we'll attach these methods to extension startup, installed, and clicked events so that the ad blocking begins as soon as the extension is installed and so that the badge state always matches the ruleset state:

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC22 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onStartup.addListener**(syncBadge);
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener**(syncBadge);
:::

::: FixedLine
**chrome.action.onClicked.addListener**(toggleAdblock);
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-5d]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB454 .pagebreak aria-label="454" role="doc-pagebreak" title="454"}
::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec38 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec38Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec38Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par118 .Para}
After installing the extension, you can test its ad blocking functionality and toggle behavior by visiting ad-heavy websites and using the toolbar icon to switch the blocker on or off. Try the following [steps]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm82} to verify the extension is working as intended:

::: UnorderedList
-   Visit a website like [cnn.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, which loads content from several major ad networks that this extension will block.

-   Check that ads and sponsored content elements are blocked when the extension is enabled.

-   Click the toolbar icon to toggle ad blocking off, then reload the page to observe the ads appearing again.

-   Click the icon once more to re-enable ad [blocking]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm83} and reload the page to confirm the ads are blocked again.

-   Observe the toolbar badge switching between "ON" (green) and "OFF" (red) as you toggle the extension.
:::
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec39 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec39Heading"}
## LLM Chatbot {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec39Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par124 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   Side panel

-   Storage

-   Options page

-   Including third-party libraries

-   LLM API integration
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec40 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec40Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec40Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB455 .pagebreak aria-label="455" role="doc-pagebreak" title="455"}This [tutorial]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm84} walks through building an extension that embeds a Gemini-powered chatbot in the browser's side panel. The chatbot provides conversational AI features using the Gemini API, with conversation history stored locally between sessions. To support API access, the extension includes an options page where users paste their own Gemini API key. API keys and chat history are stored in extension storage.

[LLMs]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm85} are a particularly compelling tool in extension development because of their ability to efficiently process and understand large volumes of textual data, such as the contents of a web page. An extension can programmatically extract raw HTML, pass it to an LLM, and receive structured insights, summaries, or contextual analysis in response. This allows developers to build advanced features like content classification, data extraction, and intelligent summarization with minimal custom logic.
:::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec41 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec41Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec41Heading .Heading}

This chatbot extension consists of three key [parts]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm86}: the side panel interface, a simple options page for the API key, and storage-backed chat logic that maintains context and communicates with the Gemini API.

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec42 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec42Heading"}
#### LLM APIs {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec42Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par133 .Para}
At the time of the writing of this book, the pace of LLM development progresses at a breakneck pace. Any company, model, and [API]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm87} that I might select for this tutorial will surely be replaced by a newer version, a newer model, etc. Despite this, there are some aspects of LLMs that remain constant over time that will make this example stand up as time passes:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB456 .pagebreak aria-label="456" role="doc-pagebreak" title="456"}

::: UnorderedList
-   **LLMs operate entirely on text, both for input and output**. While some APIs wrap text in structured fields like messages or contents, all of them fundamentally accept strings as prompts and return strings as responses.

-   **APIs can stream responses or send entire responses all at once**. Most providers offer two response modes: a standard blocking request that returns the full output at once and a streaming [mode]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm88} that delivers the response incrementally as it's generated. Streaming can improve responsiveness and user experience, especially for longer replies.

-   **APIs return markdown**. Responses are typically formatted in Markdown, which is easily converted into HTML by client-side libraries.

-   **APIs can hot swap models**. Modern LLM APIs are model-agnostic by design. Switching between different model versions or families is usually a matter of updating a string in the request (such as [model=gpt-4]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [model=gemini-2.5-pro]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}), without needing to rewrite your application logic.

-   **APIs use key authentication**. LLM APIs almost always require an API key or bearer token, typically sent via an [Authorization]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} header or URL query parameter. In client-side extensions like this one, the key must be supplied by the user and stored securely.

-   [**APIs**]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm89} **are constrained by token limits**. Each request is subject to a maximum token count. If a conversation grows too long, older messages may need to be truncated, summarized, or dropped to stay within the model's context window.
:::
::::
:::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec43 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec43Heading"}
#### Options Page {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec43Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB457 .pagebreak aria-label="457" role="doc-pagebreak" title="457"}To avoid the need to host our own [server]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm90}, this extension will have a "bring-your-own-API-key" style of interface. The user will be responsible for entering their API key, and the extension will use that key to talk directly to the LLM API.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec44 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec44Heading"}
#### Conversation and Key Storage {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec44Heading .Heading}

All messages are [stored]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm91} in chrome storage. This allows chat data to persist between panel sessions. Whenever a message is added, it's pushed to the history array and saved back to storage. Similarly, the API key is stored in the extension storage.
:::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec45 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec45Heading"}
#### Google Gemini API {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec45Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par142 .Para}
For this example, I've selected the Google [Gemini]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm92} 2.0 Flash API for two reasons:

::: UnorderedList
-   It's simple to get started with and use.

-   It has a generous free tier.
:::
::::

One important thing to note about the Gemini API is that, unlike some other LLM providers, there is no concept of a persisted conversation on the server. To preserve conversation context, each API request must include the full chat history up to that point. In this light, our extension will save the full history in extension storage and send the full chat log with each request.
:::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec46 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec46Heading"}
#### Side Panel {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec46Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par146 .Para}
The [panel]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm93} listens to storage change events to reactively re-render the chat log whenever updates occur:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB458 .pagebreak aria-label="458" role="doc-pagebreak" title="458"}

::: OrderedList
1.  ::: ItemNumber
    1\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    User enters a prompt.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

2.  ::: ItemNumber
    2\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Extension appends the prompt text to storage. Side panel reactively re-renders with new user prompt.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

3.  ::: ItemNumber
    3\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Extension sends entire prompt history to Gemini API.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::

4.  ::: ItemNumber
    4\.
    :::

    ::: ItemContent
    Gemini API responds, and response is appended to chrome storage. Side panel reactively re-renders with new API response.
    :::

    ::: ClearBoth
     
    :::
:::
::::
:::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec47 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec47Heading"}
#### Markdown {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec47Heading .Heading}

The Gemini API responses are formatted in [Markdown]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm94}. This is readable enough rendered as plaintext, but it's trivial to just bundle a third-party library that specializes in converting Markdown syntax to HTML. We'll include [marked.min.js]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} in the extension bundle and use it to render the API responses.
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec48 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec48Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec48Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par152 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares an options page, a side panel, and [permissions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm95} for storage and side panels:

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC23 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"AI Chatbot\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": **\[\"storage\", \"sidePanel\"\]**,
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"side_panel\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"default_path\": \"panel.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **},**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"options_ui\": {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"page\": \"options.html\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"open_in_tab\": true**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm96}
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-6a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB459 .pagebreak aria-label="459" role="doc-pagebreak" title="459"}
::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par153 .Para}
We'll start with the options [page]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm97}, which is just a simple input for the user's API key:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC24 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\...
:::

::: FixedLine
\<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<h1\>Gemini Chat Settings\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<label for=\"key\"\>Enter your Gemini API key:\</label\>\<br /\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<input type=\"text\" id=\"key\" placeholder=\"AIza\...\" /\>[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm98}
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<br /\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<button id=\"saveBtn\"\>Save\</button\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<script type=\"module\" src=\"options.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</body\>
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-6b]{.CaptionNumber}

options.html (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC25 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**chrome.storage.local.get(STORAGE_KEY_API_KEY, (res) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **if (res\[STORAGE_KEY_API_KEY\]) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **input.value = res\[STORAGE_KEY_API_KEY\];**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
**});**
:::
::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
button.addEventListener(\"click\", () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const key = input.value.trim();
:::

::: FixedLine
  **chrome.storage.local.set({\[STORAGE_KEY_API_KEY\]: key });**
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-6c]{.CaptionNumber}

options.js [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm99}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB460 .pagebreak aria-label="460" role="doc-pagebreak" title="460"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par154 .Para}
Next, we'll configure the HTML for our chat side [panel]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm100}. We'll need a section for the chat to render, a button for clearing the chat, and a form for submitting new prompts.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC26 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"panel.css\" /\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<script src=\"marked.min.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<body\>[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm101}
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<div class=\"container\"\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\<div id=\"chatLog\" class=\"chat-log\"\>\</div\>**
:::
::::::::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    **\<button id=\"clearChatBtn\"\>Clear Chat\</button\>**
:::
::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    **\<form id=\"chatForm\"\>**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\<input type=\"text\" id=\"chatInput\" placeholder=\"Ask me anything\...\" required /\>**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\<button type=\"submit\"\>Send\</button\>**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\</form\>**
:::
:::::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    \<div id=\"missingKey\" class=\"warning\" hidden\>
:::

::: FixedLine
      Please enter your Gemini API key in the Options page.
:::

::: FixedLine
    \</div\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</div\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<script type=\"module\" src=\"panel.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</body\>[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm102}
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-6d]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB461 .pagebreak aria-label="461" role="doc-pagebreak" title="461"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par155 .Para}
Finally, we'll tie the whole thing together with [JavaScript]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm103}. Our chat will be stored in the same format that is transmitted to the Gemini API:

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC27 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  role: \"user\" \| \"model\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  parts: \[{ text: \"\...\" }\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::
::::::::
:::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par156 .Para}
We'll define a [renderChat function]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm104} that unpacks the chat objects and renders them using the [marked]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} library:

:::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC28 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\...
:::

::: FixedLine
function renderChat(messages) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    chatLog.innerHTML = \"\";
:::
::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    for (const message of messages) {
:::

::: FixedLine
        const role = message.role \|\| \"model\";
:::

::: FixedLine
        const text = message.parts?.\[0\]?.text \|\| \"\";
:::
::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
        const div = document.createElement(\"div\");
:::

::: FixedLine
        div.className = \`chat-entry \${role}\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
        **div.innerHTML = marked.parse(text);**
:::

::: FixedLine
        chatLog.appendChild(div);
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::
::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    chatLog.scrollTop = chatLog.scrollHeight;
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm105}
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-6e]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par157 .Para}
Next, we'll define the initialization logic for the panel. We'll set storage listeners to re-render the chat, handle clear chat clicks, and retrieve the API key on first load:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB462 .pagebreak aria-label="462" role="doc-pagebreak" title="462"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC29 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
clearChatBtn.addEventListener(\"click\", () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  chrome.storage.local.set({ \[STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY\]: \[\] });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.storage.onChanged.addListener((changes) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (changes\[STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY\]) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    const messages = changes\[STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY\].newValue \|\| \[\];
:::

::: FixedLine
    renderChat(messages);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.storage.local.get(STORAGE_KEY_API_KEY, (res) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  apiKey = res\[STORAGE_KEY_API_KEY\];
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (!apiKey) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    missingKey.hidden = false;
:::

::: FixedLine
    chatForm.hidden = true;
:::

::: FixedLine
    clearChatBtn.hidden = true;
:::

::: FixedLine
    return;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm106}
:::
:::::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  chrome.storage.local.get(STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY, (res) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    renderChat(res\[STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY\] \|\| \[\]);
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-6f]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm107}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par158 .Para}
Finally, we'll write the logic for submitting [prompts]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm108}. Each time the user submits a chat, we'll append it to the stored chat and send off the entire chat to the API. When the response arrives, we'll append that to the stored chat as well. Each storage write will automatically re-render the UI.[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB463 .pagebreak aria-label="463" role="doc-pagebreak" title="463"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC30 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\...
:::

::: FixedLine
async function handlePrompt(prompt) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  chrome.storage.local.get(STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY, async (res) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    const history = res\[STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY\] \|\| \[\];
:::
:::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    const userMessage = {
:::

::: FixedLine
      role: \"user\",
:::

::: FixedLine
      parts: \[{ text: prompt }\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    };
:::

::: FixedLine
    const updatedHistory = \[\...history, userMessage\];
:::
::::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    **chrome.storage.local.set({ \[STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY\]: updatedHistory });**
:::
::::

::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    const response = await **fetch(\`\${API_ENDPOINT}?key=\${apiKey}\`, {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **method: \"POST\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **body: JSON.stringify({**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **contents: updatedHistory,**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **generationConfig: { temperature: 0.7 }**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **})**
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::
:::::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    let modelReply = {
:::

::: FixedLine
      role: \"model\",
:::

::: FixedLine
      parts: \[{ text: \"No response.\" }\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    };[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm109}
:::
:::::::

::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    try {
:::

::: FixedLine
      const data = await response.json();
:::

::: FixedLine
      **const replyText = data?.candidates?.\[0\]?.content?.parts?.\[0\]?.text;**
:::

::: FixedLine
      if (replyText) {
:::

::: FixedLine
        modelReply.parts\[0\].text = replyText;
:::

::: FixedLine
      } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
        modelReply.parts\[0\].text = \"No valid response from Gemini.\";
:::

::: FixedLine
      }
:::

::: FixedLine
    } catch (e) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      console.error(\"Error parsing Gemini response:\", e);
:::

::: FixedLine
      modelReply.parts\[0\].text = \"Error parsing Gemini response.\";
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::
:::::::::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    const finalHistory = \[\...updatedHistory, modelReply\];
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.storage.local.set({ \[STORAGE_KEY_CHAT_HISTORY\]: finalHistory });**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chatForm.addEventListener(\"submit\", (e) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  e.preventDefault();
:::

::: FixedLine
  const prompt = chatInput.value.trim();
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (!prompt \|\| !apiKey) return;
:::

::: FixedLine
  chatInput.value = \"\";
:::

::: FixedLine
  **handlePrompt(prompt);**
:::

::: FixedLine
});[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm110}
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-6g]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB464 .pagebreak aria-label="464" role="doc-pagebreak" title="464"}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB465 .pagebreak aria-label="465" role="doc-pagebreak" title="465"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec49 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec49Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec49Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par159 .Para}
After installing the extension and entering your Gemini [API]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm111} key on the options page, you can test the chatbot by opening the side panel and starting a conversation. Messages and responses should be stored and rendered automatically, with history retained across sessions. Try the following steps to confirm everything works as expected:

::: UnorderedList
-   Open the extension's Options page and paste your Gemini API key, then click *Save*.

-   Open the side panel by clicking the extension icon in the toolbar.

-   Enter a prompt like *"What is the capital of France?"* and wait for the model to reply.

-   Follow up with *"What river runs through there?"* to test whether the chatbot remembers the previous message.

-   Close and reopen the side panel to verify that the full conversation is still displayed.

-   Click the Clear [Chat]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm112} button to reset the conversation and clear stored history.

-   Submit a new prompt to start a fresh conversation and ensure the chat resets correctly.
:::
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec50 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec50Heading"}
## On-device AI Summarizer {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec50Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par167 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   Popups

-   On-device AI APIs
:::
::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec51 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec51Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec51Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB466 .pagebreak aria-label="466" role="doc-pagebreak" title="466"}This tutorial demonstrates how to build a Chrome extension that uses the new [on-device Summarizer API]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm113}, powered by Gemini Nano. This API enables Chrome extensions to generate summaries of text content directly within the browser using a built-in model that runs entirely on the user's device. This local-first architecture prioritizes privacy, speed, and offline functionality, making it a forward-looking foundation for AI-powered extensions.

The extension features a simple popup interface: users visit any page, open the extension, and click a button to generate a summary of the visible page content. Summarization is performed using the downloaded Gemini Nano [model]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm114}, which runs directly in Chrome.

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This API is currently available via origin trial and requires Developer Mode during the experimental period. For more details, see [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/ai/built-in-apis]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/ai/built-in-apis)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec52 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec52Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec52Heading .Heading}

Running a [large language model (LLM)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm115} inside a web browser is still in its infancy, but it offers a fascinating preview of what's to come. While cloud-based LLMs benefit from enormous compute power, browsers operate under significant resource constraints: less CPU, less RAM, and tighter storage limits.

The [Gemini Nano model]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm116}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm117} is a lightweight, distilled version of Google's flagship LLMs. While it lacks the power and versatility of its cloud-hosted counterparts, it's optimized for local inference and designed to work within Chrome's sandboxed environment. It runs slower and may generate more limited outputs, particularly on low-end devices.

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec53 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec53Heading"}
#### Managing the Model Lifecycle {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec53Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB467 .pagebreak aria-label="467" role="doc-pagebreak" title="467"}Using the on-device AI APIs involves managing the lifecycle of the model itself. Chrome does not ship with Gemini Nano pre-installed. Instead, the browser exposes APIs for requesting and initializing the model at runtime.

Before your extension can generate summaries, it must first request the model download. This is typically handled with a one-time initialization step during the extension's first run or when the user triggers an AI feature for the first time. Downloading the model is a blocking prerequisite: nothing can happen until this is complete. The [Gemini Nano model]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm118}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm119} is several gigabytes in size and can take up to 10--15 minutes to download, depending on connection speed. Once downloaded, Chrome caches the model, so future interactions are instant.

The Chrome AI APIs provide lifecycle events and callbacks for monitoring the download progress, completion, and failure scenarios. Your extension should provide user feedback during this process, such as progress bars or messages explaining the delay.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec54 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec54Heading"}
#### Initializing the Model {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec54Heading .Heading}

After download, the model must be [initialized]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm120}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm121} before it can accept prompts. Initialization ensures the model is properly loaded into memory and ready for inference. Once initialization is complete, the model can be used with any of the supported APIs: text summarizer, prompt, language detection, etc., all of which share the same underlying Gemini Nano runtime.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec55 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec55Heading"}
#### Using the AI APIs {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec55Heading .Heading}

With the model fully initialized, your extension can now begin sending prompts to the on-device [API]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm122}. For summarization tasks, the [summarize()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} method is used to generate a condensed version of user-visible text, like the contents of the current web page. Each request runs entirely locally, []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB468 .pagebreak aria-label="468" role="doc-pagebreak" title="468"}and no API keys or server credentials are required. The APIs are promise based, so they are easy to add to any JavaScript project, but expect them to be *very* slow.
:::
::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec56 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec56Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec56Heading .Heading}

We'll begin with a manifest that declares a popup, active tab permissions, and the [key]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [trial_tokens]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} needed to access the APIs.

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

You will need to provide your own values here to enable the APIs.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par182 .Para}
::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC31 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"On-device AI Summarizer\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"default_popup\": \"popup.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"activeTab\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"key\": \" MIIBIjANBgkqhkiG9w0\...\",**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"trial_tokens\": \[**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **\"Al4sMoKS5j3IH\...\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\]**
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm123}
:::
::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-7a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm124}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par183 .Para}
The popup page doesn't include anything special, just a button and a container for messages and the output:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB469 .pagebreak aria-label="469" role="doc-pagebreak" title="469"}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC32 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<button id=\"summarizeBtn\"\>Summarize Page\</button\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<div id=\"status\"\>\</div\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<div id=\"summary\"\>\</div\>
:::
:::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    \<script type=\"module\" src=\"popup.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-7b]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.html [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm125}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par184 .Para}
The popup [JS]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm126} is where all the action happens. Since the model acquisition is handled by the browser and is idempotent, the API checks, model download, initialization, page text acquisition, and summarization can all execute sequentially inside the button click listener.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC33 .ProgramCode}
:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\...
:::

::: FixedLine
summarizeBtn.addEventListener(\"click\", async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  clearUI();
:::
::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  if (!**self.ai?.summarizer**) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return updateStatus(\"summarizer API not supported.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::
::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const capabilities = **await self.ai.summarizer.capabilities();**
:::
::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  if (capabilities.available === \"no\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return updateStatus(\"summarizer model unavailable.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm127}
:::
::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const summarizer = await **createSummarizer**(capabilities);
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (!summarizer) return;
:::
:::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  updateStatus(\"Fetching page content\...\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  const textContent = await **getPageText**();
:::
:::::

::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  updateStatus(\"Summarizing\...\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  try {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **const summary = await summarizer.summarize(textContent, {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **context: \"This is for a general audience.\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
    summaryEl.textContent = summary;
:::

::: FixedLine
    updateStatus(\"Done.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  } catch (e) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    updateStatus(\`Error generating summary: \${e}\`);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }});[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm128}
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-7c]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB470 .pagebreak aria-label="470" role="doc-pagebreak" title="470"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par185 .Para}
The [getPageText()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [method]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm129} uses the [activeTab]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permissions to send a content script to the page, retrieve the text contents, and return it:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC34 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function getPageText() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const \[tab\] = await chrome.tabs.query({ active: true, currentWindow: true });
:::

::: FixedLine
  const \[{ result }\] = await chrome.scripting.executeScript({
:::

::: FixedLine
    target: { tabId: tab.id },
:::

::: FixedLine
    **func: () =\> document.body.innerText**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
  return result;
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm130}
:::
:::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-7d]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::

Finally, we'll write the [createSummarizer()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [method]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm131}. This instantiates a [Summarizer]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} instance that wraps the Gemini Nano model. Of course, when this code runs after a click, the model download may have not yet begun, it may be in progress, or it may be finished. The [Summarizer]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} constructor exposes a [monitor]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} function to allow us to monitor the download progress and a [ready]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} promise that lets us know when we can start sending summary prompts.

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB471 .pagebreak aria-label="471" role="doc-pagebreak" title="471"}The first time we call [summarizer.create()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, it will trigger the browser to begin the download. In practice, this download takes an extremely long time. Once the download finishes, the [ready]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} promise will resolve, this function will return the [Summarizer]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} instance, and calling [summarizer.summarize()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} can start prompting for AI-generated page summaries.

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Warning
:::

These client [AI APIs]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm132} are far from finalized. At the time of the writing of this book, the provided example functioned in Chrome 134, but as this feature becomes more mature, there will likely be large shifts in the API. You can read the Summarizer API docs here: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/ai/summarizer-api]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/ai/summarizer-api)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec57 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec57Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec57Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par189 .Para}
After installing the extension, you can test the on-device summarization workflow by opening any web page and using the popup to generate a summary. The Summarizer API is still experimental and can be slow or unreliable, especially on large pages or low-end devices. Expect that it may sometimes fail to return a summary or take a long time to generate results. To begin [testing]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm133}, follow these steps:

::: UnorderedList
-   Visit any article page such as a news story or blog post. Click the extension icon to open the popup, then click *Summarize Page*.

-   Watch for status messages like "Fetching page content\...", "Summarizing\...", and "Done." that indicate progress.

-   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB472 .pagebreak aria-label="472" role="doc-pagebreak" title="472"}Wait for the summary to appear in the popup. This may take several seconds or minutes on first use while the Gemini Nano model is downloading.

-   If an error occurs, such as "summarizer model unavailable" or "Error generating summary", try again later or test with simpler pages.
:::
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec58 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec58Heading"}
## User Scripts {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec58Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par194 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   Options page

-   Host permissions

-   [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec59 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec59Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec59Heading .Heading}

This chapter explores how to build a minimal [user script]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm134} manager using the [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API. The extension allows users to type or paste JavaScript into a popup editor and inject that script into any website using a secure, isolated execution context. Unlike traditional content scripts, user scripts are ideal for code written or supplied at runtime.
:::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec60 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec60Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec60Heading .Heading}

We'll design this extension to just inject a single user script on all URLs. The [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [API]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm135} allows us to check if a script is registered and read the content of registered scripts, so that will serve as our storage mechanism and state management.

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec61 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec61Heading"}
#### Execution Context {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec61Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB473 .pagebreak aria-label="473" role="doc-pagebreak" title="473"}This [extension]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm136} uses the [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API, which enables runtime registration of user scripts in a safe, isolated world. Chrome guarantees that user scripts run in their [own]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm137} isolated JavaScript environment ([USER_SCRIPT]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} world), exempt from the host page's Content Security Policy restrictions.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec62 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec62Heading"}
#### Popup UI {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec62Heading .Heading}

The [popup]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm138} is a simple form with a [\<textarea\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} for editing the user script JavaScript and two buttons for registering and clearing the user script. Like [declarativeNetRequest]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, we can test the registered state of our user script and use that to inform the visible state of the popup.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec63 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec63Heading"}
#### Registration Behavior {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec63Heading .Heading}

Whenever the user clicks "Inject Script," the extension checks if a script is already registered. If so, it is first unregistered to avoid [duplication]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm139}. The new user script is then registered and takes effect on all URLs ([\<all_urls\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}). The extension tracks the currently active script using a fixed ID ([dynamic-user-script]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}) so that it can be replaced or removed as needed.
:::
::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec64 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec64Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec64Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par203 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares a popup and requests the [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} permission and all URLs for host permissions:

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC35 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"User Script Injector\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[**\"userScripts\"**\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"host_permissions\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"default_popup\": \"popup.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-8a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm140}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB474 .pagebreak aria-label="474" role="doc-pagebreak" title="474"}
::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par204 .Para}
For this example, the markup is trivial, just a textarea with some buttons:

::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC36 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<textarea id=\"scriptArea\"\>
:::

::: FixedLine
      document.body.style.background = \'purple\';
:::

::: FixedLine
    \</textarea
:::

::: FixedLine
    \>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<button id=\"register\"\>Register Script\</button\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<button id=\"clear\"\>Clear Scripts\</button\>
:::
:::::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    \<script type=\"module\" src=\"popup.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-8b]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.html [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm141}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par205 .Para}
We'll define some [helper functions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm142} for setting and checking the registered user script state:

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC37 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const SCRIPT_ID = \"dynamic-user-script\";
:::

::: FixedLine
const MATCH_URLS = \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\];
:::
:::::

:::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function registerUserScript(code) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  return **chrome.userScripts.register**(\[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      id: SCRIPT_ID,
:::

::: FixedLine
      matches: MATCH_URLS,
:::

::: FixedLine
      js: \[{ code }\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      runAt: \"document_idle\",
:::

::: FixedLine
      world: \"USER_SCRIPT\"
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]);
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm143}
:::
::::::::::::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function unregisterUserScriptIfExists() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const existing = await getExistingScript();
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (existing) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return **chrome.userScripts.unregister**({ ids: \[SCRIPT_ID\] });
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
async function getExistingScript() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const existing = await **chrome.userScripts.getScripts**({ ids: \[SCRIPT_ID\] });
:::

::: FixedLine
  return existing.length \> 0 ? existing\[0\] : null;
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-8c]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB475 .pagebreak aria-label="475" role="doc-pagebreak" title="475"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par206 .Para}
Next, we'll attach handlers to the two buttons. Script [registration]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm144} will be idempotent (i.e., we'll unregister and re-register each time).

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC38 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
injectBtn.addEventListener(\"click\", async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const code = scriptArea.value;
:::
:::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  try {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **await chrome.userScripts.configureWorld({**
:::

::: FixedLine
      csp: \"script-src \'self\'\",
:::

::: FixedLine
      messaging: false
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::
::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    await **unregisterUserScriptIfExists**();
:::

::: FixedLine
    await **registerUserScript**(code);
:::
:::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    alert(\"Script registered! Reload the target page to see it in action.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
    updateButtonStates();
:::

::: FixedLine
  } catch (err) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.error(\"Failed to register script\", err);
:::

::: FixedLine
    alert(\"Failed to register userscript: \" + err.message);
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm145}
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::

::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
clearBtn.addEventListener(\"click\", async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  try {
:::

::: FixedLine
    await **unregisterUserScriptIfExists**();
:::

::: FixedLine
    alert(\"Script unregistered.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
    updateButtonStates();
:::

::: FixedLine
  } catch (err) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    console.error(\"Failed to unregister\", err);
:::

::: FixedLine
    alert(\"Failed to unregister script.\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-8d]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB476 .pagebreak aria-label="476" role="doc-pagebreak" title="476"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec65 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec65Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec65Heading .Heading}

After installing the extension, you can test the user script [injection]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm146} flow by registering a script from the popup and then visiting any site to observe its effect. Since the [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API runs in an isolated environment, the injected code won't be affected by page security restrictions, but the changes will require a page reload to take effect. Try the following steps to verify the extension works:

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par208 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB477 .pagebreak aria-label="477" role="doc-pagebreak" title="477"}Open the popup and edit the default script in the textarea to something like [document.body.style.background = \'pink\';]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and click the "Register Script" button to inject the script.

-   Navigate to any website (e.g., [[[[https://example.com]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://example.com)]{.ExternalRef}) and reload the page to see the background color change.

-   Return to the popup and click "Clear Scripts", then reload the same site to [confirm]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm147} the changes are no longer applied.

-   Edit the script again with a new effect (e.g., changing font size) and register it to confirm that updates replace the previous script.
:::
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec66 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec66Heading"}
## DevTools {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec66Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par213 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   DevTools panels

-   DevTools API
:::
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec67 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec67Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec67Heading .Heading}

This [example]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm148} builds a simple DOM tree explorer inside the browser DevTools. We'll explore how to configure an extension to render a DevTools interface and utilize its powerful APIs. When opened in DevTools, this tree [explorer]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm149} will highlight a DOM node in a web page when its corresponding tree entry is hovered in developer tools.
:::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec68 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec68Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec68Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB478 .pagebreak aria-label="478" role="doc-pagebreak" title="478"}Part of the purpose of this tutorial is to [demonstrate]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm150} how much can be accomplished from a DevTools page with no additional permissions.

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec69 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec69Heading"}
#### DevTools Panel {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec69Heading .Heading}

Our DevTools [panel]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm151} will display a mirrored DOM tree of node element names. We'll configure the panel so that whenever a particular node is highlighted, the panel script will inject JavaScript into the web page that will render a box around the corresponding DOM node.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec70 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec70Heading"}
#### Injected Highlighter {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec70Heading .Heading}

The developer DevTools API is capable of [injecting]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm152} scripts into the page, quite like the extension scripting API. In our case, we'll inject a script that will target a specific DOM node and render a translucent box over it, including its dimensions.
:::
:::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec71 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec71Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec71Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par220 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares a DevTools page:

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC39 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"DOM Tree Explorer\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\"devtools_page\": \"devtools.html\"**
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-9a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm153}
:::
::::
::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB479 .pagebreak aria-label="479" role="doc-pagebreak" title="479"}
:::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par221 .Para}
Note that *no additional permissions are needed*; the out-of-the-box DevTools permissions can meet all our needs. Next, we'll configure our DevTools page, which is little more than a thin page container:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC40 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"devtools.js\" type=\"module\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</head\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>\</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-9b]{.CaptionNumber}

[devtools.html]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm154}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC41 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.devtools.panels.create(
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"DOM Explorer\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"panel.html\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  () =\> { }
:::

::: FixedLine
);[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm155}
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-9c]{.CaptionNumber}

[devtools.js]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm156}
:::
::::
::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par222 .Para}
Next, our panel [HTML]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm157}:

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC42 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<div id=\"dom-tree\" class=\"tree\"\>\</div\>
:::
:::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    \<script type=\"module\" src=\"panel.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-9d]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.html
:::
::::
::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB480 .pagebreak aria-label="480" role="doc-pagebreak" title="480"}
:::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par223 .Para}
The panel script is somewhat involved, but at its core, it only really needs two DevTools [API methods]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm158} to operate:

::: UnorderedList
-   [chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, for injecting JavaScript and returning data

-   [chrome.devtools.network.onNavigated]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, for detecting when the user changes pages with DevTools still open
:::
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par226 .Para}
First, we'll define an [injectHighlighter()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} [method]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm159} that will be injected into the web page via [inspectedWindow.eval()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This function constructs a singleton object on the host page using a global [\_\_DOM_HIGHLIGHTER\_\_]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} property, and it will use a shadow DOM to isolate any rendered content from the host page styles.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC43 .ProgramCode}
::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function injectHighlighter() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (globalThis.\_\_DOM_HIGHLIGHTER\_\_) return;
:::
:::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const host = document.createElement(\"div\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  host.style.position = \"fixed\";
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::
::::::

:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **const shadow = host.attachShadow({ mode: \"open\" });**[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm160}
:::
::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const style = document.createElement(\"style\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  style.textContent = \`
:::

::: FixedLine
    \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  \`;
:::
:::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const overlay = document.createElement(\"div\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  \...
:::
:::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **globalThis.\_\_DOM_HIGHLIGHTER\_\_** = {
:::

::: FixedLine
    highlightBySelector: (selector) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      const el = document.querySelector(selector);
:::

::: FixedLine
      if (!el) {
:::

::: FixedLine
        overlay.style.display = \"none\";
:::

::: FixedLine
        return;
:::

::: FixedLine
      }
:::
::::::::::

:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
      **const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();**
:::

::: FixedLine
      overlay.style.top = \`\${Math.round(rect.top)}px\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
      \...
:::

::: FixedLine
    },
:::

::: FixedLine
    clear: () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      overlay.style.display = \"none\";
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  };
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-9e]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB481 .pagebreak aria-label="481" role="doc-pagebreak" title="481"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par227 .Para}
Now we'll define the two powerhouse methods of the highlighting:

::: UnorderedList
-   **createTreeNode()**[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm161}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm162}**:** This function recursively builds a visual representation of the DOM tree using [\<div\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} and [\<span\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} elements. Each node in the tree corresponds to an HTML element and includes hover-based interactivity: when the user hovers over a node label, the extension uses **chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval()**[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm163}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm164} to call a highlightBySelector method in the inspected page, visually highlighting the corresponding element. When the mouse leaves, the highlight is cleared. The method uses [node.selector]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} to uniquely identify and track the element in the actual DOM.

-   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB482 .pagebreak aria-label="482" role="doc-pagebreak" title="482"}**loadDomTree()**[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm165}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm166}**:** This function initializes the DOM tree view in the DevTools panel. It first waits until the inspected page has fully loaded ([document.readyState === \'complete\']{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}). Once ready, it injects a DOM highlighter script into the page and then serializes the [document.body]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} into a simplified object structure using a helper function ([serializeDom]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}). This serialized tree is passed back to the panel and rendered into a navigable UI using [createTreeNode]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar7 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

DevTools scripts are given special permission to use [eval()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}; it is disallowed in other MV3 contexts.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par231 .Para}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC44 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function createTreeNode(node) {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const div = document.createElement(\"div\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  div.className = \"node\";
:::

::: FixedLine
  div.dataset.selector = node.selector;
:::
:::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  const label = document.createElement(\"span\");
:::

::: FixedLine
  label.className = \"node-label\";
:::

::: FixedLine
  label.textContent = \`\<\${node.tag}\>\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
  div.appendChild(label);
:::
:::::::

:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  if (node.children && node.children.length \> 0) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    node.children.forEach((child) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      div.appendChild(createTreeNode(child));
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::
::::::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  label.addEventListener(\"mouseenter\", (e) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    const selector = e.currentTarget.parentElement.dataset.selector;
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval(**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **\`\_\_DOM_HIGHLIGHTER\_\_.highlightBySelector(\${JSON.stringify(selector)})\`**
:::

::: FixedLine
    );
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
:::::::::

:::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  **label.addEventListener(\"mouseleave\", () =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval(\`\_\_DOM_HIGHLIGHTER\_\_.clear()\`);**
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  return div;
:::

::: FixedLine
}[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm167}
:::
:::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
function loadDomTree() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  treeContainer.textContent = \"Loading DOM\...\";
:::
:::::

::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  function waitForReadyState() {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval**(
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"document.readyState\",
:::

::: FixedLine
      (state, error) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
        if (error \|\| state !== \"complete\") {
:::

::: FixedLine
          setTimeout(waitForReadyState, 100);
:::

::: FixedLine
          return;
:::

::: FixedLine
        }
:::

::: FixedLine
        **chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval(**
:::

::: FixedLine
            **\`(\${injectHighlighter.toString()})()\`);**
:::
:::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
        **chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval**(
:::

::: FixedLine
          **\`(function() {**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **const body = document.body;**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **if (!body) return null;**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **return (\${serializeDom.toString()})(body);**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **})()\`,**
:::

::: FixedLine
          (result, error) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
            if (error \|\| !result) {
:::

::: FixedLine
              treeContainer.textContent = \"Failed to load DOM.\";
:::

::: FixedLine
              console.error(\"DOM load error:\", error);
:::

::: FixedLine
              return;
:::

::: FixedLine
            }
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
            treeContainer.innerHTML = \"\";
:::

::: FixedLine
            treeContainer.appendChild(createTreeNode(result));
:::

::: FixedLine
          }
:::

::: FixedLine
        );
:::

::: FixedLine
      }
:::

::: FixedLine
    );
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm168}
:::
::::::::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  waitForReadyState();
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-9f]{.CaptionNumber}

panel.js [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm169}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB484 .pagebreak aria-label="484" role="doc-pagebreak" title="484"}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec72 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec72Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec72Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par232 .Para}
After installing the extension, you can test the DOM Tree Explorer by opening Chrome DevTools and navigating through the mirrored DOM tree. The extension will inject a highlighter into the inspected [page]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm170}, allowing you to visually locate and trace elements in real time. Follow these steps to verify the core functionality:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB485 .pagebreak aria-label="485" role="doc-pagebreak" title="485"}

::: UnorderedList
-   Open a web page in Chrome and launch DevTools (F12 or Ctrl+Shift+I).

-   Click the "DOM Explorer" tab that appears alongside other DevTools panels. Wait for the "Loading DOM\..." message to disappear and the mirrored DOM tree to render.

-   Hover over any tag name in the tree to highlight the corresponding element in the live page with an outline box. Move your mouse away to clear the highlight.

-   [Navigate]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm171} to another web page with DevTools still open, and confirm that the DOM tree automatically reloads for the new page.
:::
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec73 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec73Heading"}
## Monetized Extension {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec73Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par237 .Para}
Key topics covered:

::: UnorderedList
-   ExtensionPay integration

-   Paywalling

-   External libraries
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar8 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[ExtensionPay]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm172} is a third-party payment platform that can be added to an extension. Read on to learn more about how it works.
::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec74 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec74Heading"}
### Overview {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec74Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par242 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB486 .pagebreak aria-label="486" role="doc-pagebreak" title="486"}Monetizing [extensions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm173} is a particularly tricky subject, for several reasons:

::: UnorderedList
-   **Users expect extensions to be free.** The vast majority of extensions listed on Chrome, Firefox, and Edge marketplaces are free. Users have grown accustomed to getting utility without cost, and introducing a paywall can cause friction or confusion. Unlike app stores, browser extension platforms provide no built-in way to charge for usage, meaning there's no standard payment experience for users.

-   **There's no native support for payments.** Extension marketplaces don't support in-app purchases, subscriptions, or license management. If you want to accept payments, you must integrate a third-party provider like Stripe, Gumroad, or Paddle. This usually means building and maintaining a separate website or service just to process and validate payments, infrastructure that sits entirely outside the extension itself.

-   **Monetization requires a reliable identity system.** If you're accepting payments, you need to track which users have paid, enable or disable features accordingly, and prevent abuse like double-charging or license sharing. This means implementing some form of [authentication]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm174} and syncing user state between the extension and your server. Without it, there's no way to know whether a user should have access to premium features.
