-   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB487 .pagebreak aria-label="487" role="doc-pagebreak" title="487"}**Extension code is inherently exposed.** Like frontend JavaScript, all extension logic runs in the user's browser and can be inspected, decompiled, or tampered with. Users can unpack your .crx, read your scripts, and modify runtime values in DevTools. If you're gating features with simple flags in JavaScript, a determined user can bypass them with minimal effort. Obfuscation tools help deter casual reverse engineering, but they cannot fully secure your logic.
:::
::::

In this tutorial, we'll explore how to use an out-of-the-box extension payment platform. The extension will only have dummy functionality, but we'll demonstrate how to get started paywalling extension content and directing users to payment flows.

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_FPar9 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

Many developers think adding a donation link to their extension will get them a bit of revenue. My recommendation: don't bother. If you want to bring in money, add a paywall; users ignore donation buttons.
::::
:::::::

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec75 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec75Heading"}
### Design {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec75Heading .Heading}

We're going to use ExtensionPay ([[[[https://extensionpay.com/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://extensionpay.com/)]{.ExternalRef}) in this example. When [building]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm175} a paywalled extension with ExtensionPay, it's important to understand the different pieces that the platform provides.

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec76 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec76Heading"}
#### Payment Processing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec76Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par250 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB488 .pagebreak aria-label="488" role="doc-pagebreak" title="488"}ExtensionPay is a developer-facing platform that simplifies extension monetization, but it is not a payment [processor]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm176} itself. Instead, it acts as a bridge between your browser extension and Stripe, the underlying payment platform that processes transactions.

::: UnorderedList
-   **Stripe handles all the actual money movement.** When you use ExtensionPay, you still need to create a Stripe account and connect it to ExtensionPay. Stripe is responsible for securely handling credit card payments, issuing payouts to your bank account, verifying customer identities (AML/KYC), managing currency conversion, and handling disputes or refunds. Stripe also hosts the checkout experience via its embedded or hosted checkout flows. Extensions should *not* talk directly to the [Stripe]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm177} API or embed Stripe API keys in their code, as this risks accidental disclosure or misuse.

-   :::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par252 .Para}
    **ExtensionPay simplifies Stripe integration for your extension.** The Stripe API is powerful but complex. ExtensionPay provides a lightweight JavaScript library and a backend API that handle authentication, purchase tracking, and license validation. You don't need to build your own backend or work directly with Stripe's full API. ExtensionPay consists of two key components:

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   **The ExtPay JavaScript library** is included in your extension to communicate securely with ExtensionPay's backend.

    -   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB489 .pagebreak aria-label="489" role="doc-pagebreak" title="489"}**The ExtensionPay servers** act as an intermediary between your extension and Stripe, managing user status, payment validation, and access control.
    :::
    ::::

-   **No server required.** One of ExtensionPay's major benefits is that it's designed to be used without a custom backend. You can handle license validation, feature gating, and user access entirely inside your extension, using ExtensionPay's client-side tools. Stripe [webhooks]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm178} are still handled by ExtensionPay's infrastructure, so you don't need to spin up a server to handle payment events like cancellations or renewals.

-   **You own the customer relationship.** Even though ExtensionPay simplifies the integration, you still operate through your own Stripe account. That means all customer data, subscriptions, and payment history are visible in your Stripe dashboard. You can issue refunds, change pricing, or manage customer subscriptions directly. ExtensionPay doesn't sit between you and your users; it just facilitates communication.
:::
::::
:::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec77 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec77Heading"}
#### Identity {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec77Heading .Heading}

Who is this user? Did a user pay yet? You'll need to track these answers, and ExtPay is a simple JavaScript API for accessing the user's [identity]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm179}. Extensions are installed anonymously, and by default, the browser gives you no information about the person using your product. If you want to monetize, you'll need a way to associate usage with identity. This typically involves some form of authentication, account management, and user tracking.

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB490 .pagebreak aria-label="490" role="doc-pagebreak" title="490"}ExtPay simplifies this by giving your extension a lightweight JavaScript API that uniquely identifies users and links them to their payment history. When a user purchases your extension, ExtPay automatically associates that payment with a persistent user ID, caches that user's license locally so it survives browser restarts, and offers a programmatic way to check payment status, plan tier, and other metadata.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec78 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec78Heading"}
#### Paywalling {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec78Heading .Heading}

Using ExtPay, you will need to structure your code in a way that separates paid vs. free logic. You can safely [gate]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm180} specific UI components, functions, or routes based on the user's subscription status. Since the payment status is being checked client-side, you won't be able to stop the most determined users from teasing out your payment logic. In general, if you can connect with a userbase that is happy to pay for your software, reverse engineering concerns will become vanishingly small.
:::
::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec79 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec79Heading"}
### Implementation {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec79Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par260 .Para}
We'll begin with a manifest that declares a background script, a popup page, and storage [permissions]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm181}:

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC45 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Paywalled Extension\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"storage\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\"
:::

::: FixedLine
   },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"default_popup\": \"popup.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-10a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB491 .pagebreak aria-label="491" role="doc-pagebreak" title="491"}
:::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par261 .Para}
In the background, you'll initialize the ExtPay library (which you'll need to include with the extension bundle).

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC46 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
importScripts(\"ExtPay.js\")
:::
::::

::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const extpay = ExtPay(\"\<YOUR_REGISTERED_ID\>\");
:::

::: FixedLine
extpay.startBackground();
:::
:::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-10b]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm182}
:::
::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par262 .Para}
With this in place, you can test if a user has paid, and if not, you can kick them over to the payment page. This payment page is hosted on the ExtensionPay website, but it is linked to your registered extension and therefore will be branded as such.

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC47 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
**extpay.getUser()**.then(user =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (user.paid) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // \...
:::

::: FixedLine
  } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
    **extpay.openPaymentPage()**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm183}
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-10c]{.CaptionNumber}

background.js (excerpt)
:::
::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par263 .Para}
In the popup, we can paywall content in a similar fashion, by checking the user status and acting accordingly:[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB492 .pagebreak aria-label="492" role="doc-pagebreak" title="492"}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC48 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
        \<script src=\"ExtPay.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
        \<script src=\"popup.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-10d]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.html [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm184}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PC49 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const extpay = ExtPay(\"\<YOUR_REGISTERED_ID\>\")
:::
::::

::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
extpay.getUser().then(user =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  **if (user.paid) {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Render extension normally
:::

::: FixedLine
  } else {
:::

::: FixedLine
    // Render payment prompt, and direct them to
:::

::: FixedLine
    // payment page with **extpay.openPaymentPage()**
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}).catch(err =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Error!
:::

::: FixedLine
});[]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm185}
:::
:::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 12-10e]{.CaptionNumber}

popup.js [(excerpt)]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm186}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec80 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec80Heading"}
### Testing {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec80Heading .Heading}

After installing the extension, you can test the [paywall]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm187} and payment integration by simulating the user experience for both paid and unpaid states. Before testing, you must first sign up for a Stripe account, register your extension on ExtensionPay, and replace [\<YOUR_REGISTERED_ID\>]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} with your actual registered ExtensionPay ID in the code. The following steps walk through how to verify that monetization and gating logic is working correctly:

:::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Par265 .Para}
::: UnorderedList
-   []{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_PB493 .pagebreak aria-label="493" role="doc-pagebreak" title="493"}Open the extension popup to trigger the [extpay.getUser()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} call. If the user has not paid, the extension should display a payment prompt and open the hosted Stripe payment page when [extpay.openPaymentPage()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} is called.

-   Complete the Stripe checkout flow using test [credentials]{#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm188} provided by Stripe if you're in test mode. After a successful purchase, return to the popup or reload it. The extension should now recognize the user as paid and render the gated content or functionality.
:::
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec81 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec81Heading"}
## Summary {#534317_2_En_12_Chapter.xhtml_Sec81Heading .Heading}

In this chapter, you explored how extensions can go far beyond basic UI tweaks, tapping into advanced browser APIs to modify pages, inspect elements, and inject logic in creative ways. We built a variety of hands-on tools, including a live DOM tree explorer using the DevTools API, a dynamic user script injector powered by the [userScripts]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} API, a side panel[--]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}based chatbot using Gemini, and several feature-rich tools like a password manager, tab manager, and ad blocker. Along the way, we covered how extensions hook into developer tools, how they register runtime scripts securely, how to gate premium features, and how to handle messaging, storage, and execution contexts across extension components.

In the next chapter, we will cover how to develop, deploy, and update extensions in marketplaces such as the Chrome Web Store.
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml}

::::
:::::

`<!--Begin Abstract-->`{=html}

::: MainTitleSection
# 13. Developing, Publishing, and Managing Extensions {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_chaptertitle .ChapterTitle lang="en"}
:::

:::::::: AuthorGroup
::: AuthorNames
[[Matt Frisbie]{.AuthorName}^[1](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2) [ ]{.ContactIcon}^]{.Author}
:::

:::::: Affiliations
:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Aff2 .Affiliation}
[(1)]{.AffiliationNumber}

::: AffiliationText
Glen Ellyn, IL, USA
:::
::::

::: ClearBoth
 
:::
::::::
::::::::

`<!--End Abstract-->`{=html}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: Fulltext
[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB495 .pagebreak aria-label="495" role="doc-pagebreak" title="495"}The process of developing, testing, and publishing a browser extension is significantly different from developing a traditional website. The steps involved more closely resemble those of a mobile app than those of a website. Gaining a better understanding of how to effectively develop, publish, and distribute your extension is critical to mastery of the subject.

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar1 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This chapter will be Google Chrome/Chrome Web Store-centric, but all major browsers (with the exception of Safari) offer nearly identical facilities when developing locally and when publishing to a marketplace.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec1Heading"}
## Local Development {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec1Heading .Heading}

You'll spend quite a lot of time developing your extension locally, so it's worth getting familiar with how all the parts fit together. In this section, we'll explore how a locally loaded extension works and all the different ways you can look under the hood.

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar2 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB496 .pagebreak aria-label="496" role="doc-pagebreak" title="496"}For developers new to extension development, Chapter [[[3]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_3_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} demonstrates the basic process of loading a local extension into your browser for development purposes.
::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec2Heading"}
### Inspecting Your Extension {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec2Heading .Heading}

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par6 .Para}
Begin by installing this dummy extension:

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC1 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Demo Extension\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"background\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"type\": \"module\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"options_ui\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"open_in_tab\": true,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"page\": \"options.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"content_scripts\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    {
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"matches\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"css\": \[\],
:::

::: FixedLine
      \"js\": \[\"content-script.js\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
  \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"scripting\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"declarativeNetRequest\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"tabs\"
:::

::: FixedLine
   \],
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"host_permissions\": \[\"\<all_urls\>\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-1a]{.CaptionNumber}

[manifest.json]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm1}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB497 .pagebreak aria-label="497" role="doc-pagebreak" title="497"}

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC2 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<h1\>Options\</h1\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script src=\"options.js\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-1b]{.CaptionNumber}

[options.html]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm2}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC3 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Initialized options!\");
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-1c]{.CaptionNumber}

[options.js]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm3}
:::
::::
:::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC4 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Initialized background!\");
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-1]{.CaptionNumber}

 [background.js]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm4}
:::
::::
:::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC5 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
console.log(\"Content script initialized!\");
:::
::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-1d]{.CaptionNumber}

[content-script.js]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm5}
:::
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par7 .Para}
Once this extension is loaded locally, your browser will add a card inside the browser extension management view. In Google Chrome, the URL for this page is [chrome://extensions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. An example card is shown in Figure [[13-1](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig1)]{.InternalRef}.[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB498 .pagebreak aria-label="498" role="doc-pagebreak" title="498"}

<figure id="Fig1" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO1" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig1_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome extensions page showing a demo extension. The page includes options to load, pack, and update extensions, with a search bar at the top. The demo extension is listed with version 0.0.1, an ID, and options to view details, remove, or toggle activation. Developer mode is visible in the top right corner." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-1</span>
<p>An example <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm6">extension card in the extension management page view</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

From this card, you're able to view the details page, uninstall or disable the extension, view the extension's errors page, and reload the extension.

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar3 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Details about reloading an extension are covered later in this chapter.
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par10 .Para}
Clicking to open the details page will reveal something like Figure [[13-2](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig2)]{.InternalRef}.

<figure id="Fig2" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO2" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig2_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Chrome browser window displaying the &quot;Extensions&quot; page. The page shows details for a &quot;Demo Extension&quot; with options to load, pack, or update extensions. The extension is turned on, with no description provided. Version is 0.0.1, size is less than 1 MB, and ID is a long alphanumeric string. Permissions include reading browsing history and blocking content. Site access is set to &quot;On all sites.&quot; Options for incognito mode, file URL access, and error collection are visible. Developer mode is enabled." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-2</span>
<p>An example <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm7">extension detail view</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par11 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB500 .pagebreak aria-label="500" role="doc-pagebreak" title="500"}There are a few things to note in this view:

::: UnorderedList
-   **This view is a snapshot.** Values listed inside are not real time and will not update unless the page reloads.

-   **Inspect views**[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm8} contains a list of links paired to each extension view that is currently active. Each link will open a developer tools window targeted at that particular extension view. (The "views" name can be confusing, as this tool also allows you to inspect the background service worker, which has no user interface.)

-   [**Permissions**]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm9} displays the list of permissions prompts that the extension would have to explicitly request if it were loaded in production. Because this extension is loaded in development mode, the permissions dialog is silenced.

-   [**Source**]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm10} indicates where in the current OS's filesystem it is loading the extension files from.
:::
::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec3Heading"}
#### Inspecting an Extension View {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec3Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par16 .Para}
Clicking the "options" link under *Inspect Views* will open the corresponding developer [tools]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm11} interfaces (Figure [[13-3](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig3)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB501 .pagebreak aria-label="501" role="doc-pagebreak" title="501"}

<figure id="Fig3" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO3" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig3_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser window displaying a Chrome extension options page. The URL bar shows &quot;chrome-extension:/geehgldgoddaeggfdoodhbkofpagmpmpm/options.html&quot;. The page title is &quot;Options&quot; on the left. On the right, the browser&#39;s developer console is open, showing the message &quot;Initialized options!&quot; with a link to &quot;options.js:1&quot;. The console tab is active, with no issues reported." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-3</span>
<p>The options page inspect <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm12">view</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par17 .Para}
Note here that the console is showing log output for both the current tab console output and the background service [worker]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm13}. If you wish to split out console outputs, the developer console allows you to select specific sources (Figure [[13-4](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig4)]{.InternalRef}).[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB502 .pagebreak aria-label="502" role="doc-pagebreak" title="502"}

<figure id="Fig4" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO4" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig4_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser window displaying the Chrome Developer Tools Console tab. The URL bar shows a Chrome extension link. The console lists &quot;DevTools Performance Metrics&quot; and &quot;background.js #40 (activated)&quot; under the &quot;top&quot; dropdown. The interface includes options for Elements, Console, Sources, and Network tabs." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-4</span>
<p>Selecting a logging source in developer <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm14">tools</span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
:::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec4Heading"}
#### Inspecting a Background Service Worker {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec4Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par18 .Para}
It's also possible to directly inspect a [background service worker]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm15}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm16}. Clicking the *Inspect view* link will reveal something like Figure [[13-5](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig5)]{.InternalRef}.

<figure id="Fig5" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO5" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig5_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a browser&#39;s DevTools console tab displaying the message &quot;Initialized background!&quot; with a link to &quot;background.js:1&quot; on the right. The interface includes tabs for Console, Sources, Network, Performance, Memory, and Application, along with filter and settings options." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-5</span>
<p>Inspecting the <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm17">background service worker</span><span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm18"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar4 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Warning
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB503 .pagebreak aria-label="503" role="doc-pagebreak" title="503"}One *extremely* important thing to keep in mind is that this background service worker will interfere with the lifecycle of the service worker. It will prevent the service worker from going idle. It will also prolong the lifespan of a service worker past reloads, causing bizarre behavior when debugging an extension. **When you are done with a service worker developer tools window, close it immediately.** Leaving it open in the background will change the behavior of your local extension in unpredictable ways and make it much harder to debug.
::::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par20 .Para}
Chrome offers a real-time view of your service worker [status]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm19}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm20} and log output at [chrome://serviceworker-internals/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} (Figure [[13-6](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig6)]{.InternalRef}). This page will not interfere with how your service workers behave, allowing them to go idle.

<figure id="Fig6" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO6" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig6_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of the Chrome Service Worker internals page. It displays details about a service worker registration, including the scope, storage key, origin, and status. The status is &quot;ACTIVATED&quot; and &quot;RUNNING.&quot; Additional information includes script URL, fetch handler existence, and IDs for registration, renderer, and DevTools agent. Options to stop, inspect, or unregister the service worker are available." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-6</span>
<p>Google Chrome’s service worker internals <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm21">page</span><span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm22"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
:::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec5Heading"}
#### Inspecting a Content Script {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec5Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par21 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB504 .pagebreak aria-label="504" role="doc-pagebreak" title="504"}Inspecting the console for web pages with [content scripts]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm23}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm24} will mix the console output for the web page and the content script together (Figure [[13-7](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig7)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig7" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO7" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig7_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a web browser displaying the Wikipedia homepage and the browser&#39;s console. The Wikipedia page shows language options with article counts, including English, Japanese, Russian, and others. The console on the right lists options like &quot;Demo Extension&quot; and &quot;DevTools Performance Metrics,&quot; with checkboxes for settings such as &quot;Preserve log&quot; and &quot;Show CORS errors in console.&quot; The console displays the message &quot;Content script initialized!&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-7</span>
<p>Example console output for Wikipedia.org</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

As with background service workers and options pages, it is possible to filter for only the content script console [output]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm25}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm26}.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec6Heading"}
### File Changes {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec6Heading .Heading}

In the [extension]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm27} detail view shown above, it indicates where in the filesystem the extension was loaded from. This is because the extension is serving files directly from that directory! Any changes you make to the []{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB505 .pagebreak aria-label="505" role="doc-pagebreak" title="505"}source files will be immediately reflected the next time the extension loads them via network request. For example, a modification to [popup.html]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} will show the very next time the popup is opened. This does *not* apply to files that the extension uses only on install such as [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm28}. Updates to these files will only be reflected upon a formal extension reload.
:::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec7Heading"}
### Error Monitoring {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec7Heading .Heading}

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par24 .Para}
Monitoring [errors]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm29}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm30} during development can be tricky. For views that have HTML interfaces, errors thrown in the page can be monitored via the normal developer tools console. All errors thrown in an extension, no matter where they are thrown, will appear in the extension error view (Figures [[13-8](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig8)]{.InternalRef}, [[13-9](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig9)]{.InternalRef}, and [[13-10](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig10)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig8" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO8" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig8_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="A screenshot of the Chrome Extensions page in a web browser. The page displays options to &quot;Load unpacked,&quot; &quot;Pack extension,&quot; and &quot;Update.&quot; Developer mode is toggled on. Under &quot;All Extensions,&quot; a card for &quot;Demo Extension 0.0.1&quot; is shown with options for &quot;Details,&quot; &quot;Remove,&quot; and &quot;Errors.&quot; The extension ID is visible, and there is a link to inspect views for the service worker, which is inactive." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-8</span>
<p>The <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm31">Errors</span><span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm32"></span> button will only appear when the extension throws its first error. This button will open the extensions error view</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig9" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO9" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig9_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Chrome browser window displaying the Extensions page in Developer mode. The page shows three uncaught errors: &quot;Content script error,&quot; &quot;Background error,&quot; and &quot;Options error.&quot; The &quot;Options error&quot; is expanded, revealing details in the context of &quot;options.html&quot; and a stack trace in &quot;options.js&quot; with a highlighted line: `throw new Error(&quot;Options error!&quot;);`. The URL bar shows a Chrome extension error link. Tabs for Wikipedia and another page are visible." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-9</span>
<p>The extension error view showing errors from multiple <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm33">sources</span><span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm34"></span></p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>

<figure id="Fig10" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO10" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig10_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a Chrome browser window displaying the Extensions page in Developer mode. The page shows an error message: &quot;Service worker registration failed. Status code: 15&quot; related to a demo extension. The JSON code snippet highlights &quot;background.js&quot; as the service worker. Below, an uncaught error message reads: &quot;Uncaught Error: Background error!&quot; The interface includes options to load, pack, and update extensions, with a sidebar for keyboard shortcuts and a link to the Chrome Web Store." />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-10</span>
<p>A service worker fails to register after an <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm35">error</span><span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm36"></span> thrown during initialization</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar5 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Warning
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB507 .pagebreak aria-label="507" role="doc-pagebreak" title="507"}If a service worker throws an error in the first turn of the event loop, it will fail to register. This is important, as any event handlers that it sets will never execute.
::::
::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec8Heading"}
### Extension Reloads {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec8Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par26 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB508 .pagebreak aria-label="508" role="doc-pagebreak" title="508"}There are several different points at which portions of the [extension]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm37}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm38} are reloaded:

::: UnorderedList
-   An **extension reload** obtains a new copy of [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}, updates the background service worker, and closes any stale popups and options pages that are open. This is required when the [manifest.json]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} changes.

-   An **extension page reload** is a page refresh of any page that uses the extension protocol [chrome-extension://]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}. This is required to reflect changes in HTML, JS, CSS, and images in popup and options pages.

-   A **web page reload** is a page refresh of any web page that has content scripts injected. This is required to reflect changes in the injected content scripts.

-   A **devtools reload** is closing and opening the browser's developer tools interface. This is required to reflect changes in devtools pages.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par31 .Para}
There are three ways to force an extension reload:

::: UnorderedList
-   Uninstall and reinstall the extension.

-   Click the reload icon ↺ in the corresponding card on the Chrome Extensions page.

-   Programmatically reload the [extension]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm39}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm40} using [chrome.runtime.reload()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} or [chrome.management.setEnabled()]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}.
:::
::::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec9Heading"}
## Automated Extension Tests {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec9Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB509 .pagebreak aria-label="509" role="doc-pagebreak" title="509"}Because the components of browser extensions are built from web technologies, testing them is not too different from testing web pages. Popular tools such as [Vitest]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm41}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm42} and [Puppeteer]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm43}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm44} can be configured to power automated tests for your extension. Some idiomatic aspects of browser extensions such as popups and content scripts can't be directly tested, but with some clever workarounds, you will still be able to compose a robust and effective test suite.

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar6 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

This section assumes you are familiar with JavaScript automated testing. If not, the Vitest documentation has a good tutorial geared for beginners: [[[[https://vitest.dev/guide/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://vitest.dev/guide/)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec10Heading"}
### Unit Tests {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec10Heading .Heading}

Because [unit tests]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm45}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm46} don't rely on extension components rendering inside their native browser containers, these tests can be written in a nearly identical fashion to regular web page unit tests. A popular unit [test]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm47}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm48} setup for extensions is to use Vite as the main build tool, React as the framework, and Vitest as the test runner. The following example sets up a very simple extension with a popup that reads from the Chrome storage API. The example includes some [unit tests]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm49}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm50} that make sure the popup renders as expected:

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par38 .Para}
::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC6 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"name\": \"Unit Test Example\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"manifest_version\": 3,
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"action\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"default_popup\": \"index.html\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"permissions\": \[\"storage\"\]
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-2a]{.CaptionNumber}

manifest.json
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB510 .pagebreak aria-label="510" role="doc-pagebreak" title="510"}

:::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC7 .ProgramCode}
::::::::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
{
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"type\": \"module\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"scripts\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"dev\": \"vite\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"build\": \"vite build\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"test\": \"vitest \--watch\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"dependencies\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"react\": \"\^18.2.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"react-dom\": \"\^18.2.0\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
  \"devDependencies\": {
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"@testing-library/dom\": \"\^10.4.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"@testing-library/jest-dom\": \"\^6.6.3\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"@testing-library/react\": \"\^16.2.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"jsdom\": \"\^26.0.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"vite\": \"\^5.0.0\",
:::

::: FixedLine
    \"vitest\": \"\^1.6.1\"
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-2b]{.CaptionNumber}

package.json
:::
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB511 .pagebreak aria-label="511" role="doc-pagebreak" title="511"}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC8 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
import { defineConfig } from \"vitest/config\";
:::
::::

::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
export default defineConfig({
:::

::: FixedLine
  test: {
:::

::: FixedLine
    globals: true,
:::

::: FixedLine
    environment: \"jsdom\",
:::

::: FixedLine
  },
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-2c]{.CaptionNumber}

[vitest.config.ts]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm51}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm52}
:::
::::
::::::::::::::

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC9 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
\<!DOCTYPE html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\<html\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \<body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<div id=\"app\"\>\</div\>
:::

::: FixedLine
    \<script type=\"module\" src=\"index.tsx\"\>\</script\>
:::

::: FixedLine
  \</body\>
:::

::: FixedLine
\</html\>
:::
::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-2d]{.CaptionNumber}

index.html
:::
::::
:::::::::::::

:::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC10 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
import React from \"react\";
:::

::: FixedLine
import { createRoot } from \"react-dom/client\";
:::

::: FixedLine
import { Popup } from \"./Popup\";
:::

::: FixedLine
const rootElement = document.getElementById(\"app\");
:::

::: FixedLine
const root = createRoot(rootElement);
:::

::: FixedLine
root.render(\<Popup /\>);
:::
:::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-2e]{.CaptionNumber}

index.tsx
:::
::::
::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB512 .pagebreak aria-label="512" role="doc-pagebreak" title="512"}

::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC11 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
import React, { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";
:::

::: FixedLine
export function Popup() {
:::

::: FixedLine
  const \[count, setCount\] = useState(null);
:::

::: FixedLine
  **useEffect(() =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **chrome.storage.sync.get(\[\"count\"\], (result) =\> {**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **if (typeof result.count !== \"number\") {**
:::

::: FixedLine
        **result.count = 0;**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **}**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **setCount(result.count);**
:::

::: FixedLine
    **});**
:::

::: FixedLine
  **}, \[\]);**
:::

::: FixedLine
  if (count != null) {
:::

::: FixedLine
    return \<h1\>Popup count: {count}\</h1\>;
:::

::: FixedLine
  }
:::

::: FixedLine
}
:::
::::::::::::::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-2f]{.CaptionNumber}

Popup.tsx
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC12 .ProgramCode}
:::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
import \"@testing-library/jest-dom\";
:::

::: FixedLine
import { render, screen } from \"@testing-library/react\";
:::

::: FixedLine
import { vi } from \"vitest\";
:::

::: FixedLine
import React from \"react\";
:::

::: FixedLine
import { Popup } from \"./Popup\";
:::
::::::::

::::::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
describe(\"Popup component\", () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
  beforeEach(() =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    global.chrome = {
:::

::: FixedLine
      storage: {
:::

::: FixedLine
        sync: {
:::

::: FixedLine
          get: vi.fn().mockImplementation((keys, callback) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
            callback({ count: 5 });
:::

::: FixedLine
          }),
:::

::: FixedLine
        },
:::

::: FixedLine
      },
:::

::: FixedLine
    };
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
:::::::::::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  it(\"renders the popup count\", async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    render(\<Popup /\>);
:::

::: FixedLine
    expect(await screen.findByText(\"Popup count: 5\")).toBeInTheDocument();
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::
:::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
  it(\"defaults count to 0 if not set\", async () =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    global.chrome.storage.sync.get.mockImplementation((keys, callback) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
      callback({ count: undefined });
:::

::: FixedLine
    });
:::
:::::::

::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
    render(\<Popup /\>);
:::

::: FixedLine
    expect(await screen.findByText(\"Popup count: 0\")).toBeInTheDocument();
:::

::: FixedLine
  });
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::

:::: {.Caption lang="en"}
::: CaptionContent
[Example 13-2g]{.CaptionNumber}

[Popup.test.tsx]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm53}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm54}
:::
::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB513 .pagebreak aria-label="513" role="doc-pagebreak" title="513"}
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar7 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

If you are setting this example up from scratch, running [npm install]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} should get this setup working.
::::

For developers already familiar with how typical React unit testing looks, most of this should not surprise you. Note that most of this example is typical unit [test]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm55}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm56} boilerplate. The parts that are unique to browser extension unit testing are shown in bold.

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par41 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB514 .pagebreak aria-label="514" role="doc-pagebreak" title="514"}With all the packages installed, you should find that [npm run dev]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} will start up a [Vite]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm57}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm58} development build, and [npm test]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} will run your unit test suite (Figure [[13-11](#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Fig11)]{.InternalRef}).

<figure id="Fig11" class="Figure">
<div id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_MO11" class="MediaObject">
<img src="/app/temp/1773197898499_images/images/534317_2_En_13_Chapter/534317_2_En_13_Fig11_HTML.jpg" class="ImageStyle3" alt="Screenshot of a terminal window showing test results for a project using esbuild. The command `npm run test` is executed, running `vitest --watch`. The version displayed is DEV v1.6. Test results indicate that one test file and two tests have passed. Specific tests include &quot;Popup.test.tsx&quot; with checks for rendering the popup count and defaulting the count to zero if not set. The process started at 10:13:20, with a duration of 588ms. A message at the bottom reads &quot;PASS Waiting for file changes... press h to show help, press q to quit.&quot;" />
</div>
<figcaption><div class="CaptionContent">
<span class="CaptionNumber">Figure 13-11</span>
<p>A passing unit <span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm59">test</span><span id="534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm60"></span> run</p>
</div></figcaption>
</figure>
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec11Heading"}
### Integration Tests {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec11Heading .Heading}

Integration [testing]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm61}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm62} browser extensions is made possible via tools like [Playwright]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm63} ([[[[https://playwright.dev/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://playwright.dev/)]{.ExternalRef}) or [Puppeteer]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm64}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm65}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm66} ([[[[https://pptr.dev/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://pptr.dev/)]{.ExternalRef}). They allow you to programmatically control a Chromium browser, including navigating to URLs and dispatching user events. They cannot *perfectly* replicate all the different ways that a browser extension will render its views, but they are by far the best tools available to power an integration test suite.

:::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec12Heading"}
#### E2E Puppeteer Testing {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec12Heading .Heading}

:::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par43 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB515 .pagebreak aria-label="515" role="doc-pagebreak" title="515"}[Puppeteer]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm67} can be configured to load an extension via the [\--load-extension]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} flag. This flag should be pointed to a local directory on your machine that contains your [extension]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm68}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm69}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm70} code. You will also need to disable headless mode, as extensions cannot be loaded in a headless browser:

::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC13 .ProgramCode}
:::::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const path = \`path/to/your/extension\`;
:::

::: FixedLine
const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
:::

::: FixedLine
  // Disable headless mode
:::

::: FixedLine
  headless: false,
:::

::: FixedLine
  args: \[
:::

::: FixedLine
    \`\--disable-extensions-except=\${path}\`,
:::

::: FixedLine
    \`\--load-extension=\${path}\`
:::

::: FixedLine
  \]
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
::::::::::::
:::::::::::::
::::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par44 .Para}
This will boot up a [Chromium browser]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm71}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm72}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm73}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm74}, but you'll need to direct the browser to a URL for your extension. Of course, you will need to know ahead of time what the extension's ID is. A good strategy for this is to control the extension ID via the manifest's [key]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} field:

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC14 .ProgramCode}
::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
const page = await browser.newPage();
:::

::: FixedLine
await page.goto(
:::

::: FixedLine
  **\'chrome-extension://\<extensionID\>/popup.html\'**);
:::

::: FixedLine
// Now you can run test code
:::
:::::::
::::::::
:::::::::

Because all extension views, including popup pages, can be loaded via direct extension URL, all your extension pages can be tested in this way. Puppeteer can be used to run any Chromium browser, so an integration [test]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm75}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm76}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm77} suite can target browsers like Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, or Opera.

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar8 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB516 .pagebreak aria-label="516" role="doc-pagebreak" title="516"}Setting up a full working integration test with Puppeteer is outside the scope of this book. Some full examples are listed in the "Additional Reading*"* section below.
::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec13Heading"}
### Additional Reading {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec13Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par47 .Para}
Setting up automated testing is tricky and, frankly, annoying. The following are some links to blog posts and documentation about various extension testing [setups]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm78}:

::: UnorderedList
-   [[[[https://www.streaver.com/blog/posts/testing-web-extensions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://www.streaver.com/blog/posts/testing-web-extensions)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://github.com/clarkbw/jest-webextension-mock]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://github.com/clarkbw/jest-webextension-mock)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://www.npmjs.com/package/jest-chrome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://www.npmjs.com/package/jest-chrome)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://github.com/probil/vitest-chrome]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://github.com/probil/vitest-chrome)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://medium.com/information-and-technology/unit-testing-browser-extensions-bdd4e60a4f3d]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://medium.com/information-and-technology/unit-testing-browser-extensions-bdd4e60a4f3d)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://medium.com/information-and-technology/integration-testing-browser-extensions-with-jest-676b4e9940ca]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://medium.com/information-and-technology/integration-testing-browser-extensions-with-jest-676b4e9940ca)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://gokatz.me/blog/automate-chrome-extension-testing/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://gokatz.me/blog/automate-chrome-extension-testing/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://tweak-extension.com/blog/complete-guide-test-chrome-extension-puppeteer/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://tweak-extension.com/blog/complete-guide-test-chrome-extension-puppeteer/)]{.ExternalRef}

-   [[[[https://pptr.dev/#working-with-chrome-extensions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://pptr.dev/#working-with-chrome-extensions)]{.ExternalRef}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm79}

-   [[[[https://playwright.dev/docs/chrome-extensions]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://playwright.dev/docs/chrome-extensions)]{.ExternalRef}
:::
::::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec14Heading"}
## Publishing Extensions {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec14Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB517 .pagebreak aria-label="517" role="doc-pagebreak" title="517"}To publish an extension to the [Chrome Web Store]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm80}, you'll first need to pay the one-time \$5 fee for a developer account. Once this account is set up, you're ready to submit. Extensions are manually uploaded to the marketplace (e.g., [developer.chrome.com)]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional} as a zip file of your extension's files and assets, or published via API (e.g., the Chrome Web Store API).

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec15Heading"}
### Store Listing {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec15Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par59 .Para}
To publish an [extension]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm81}, you'll need to specify how it should appear to users in the Chrome Web Store. Some items are automatically extracted from the manifest:

::: UnorderedList
-   Extension title

-   Extension summary

-   Icon
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par63 .Para}
Everything else, you'll need to supply yourself:

::: UnorderedList
-   Description

-   Extension category

-   Assets (including videos and screenshots)

-   URLs (main website, support URL, privacy policy URL)
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar9 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

These fields can be changed later, but each change will make the entire extension undergo a slow manual review -- even if the code was not changed.
::::
:::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec16Heading"}
### Privacy Practices {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec16Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB518 .pagebreak aria-label="518" role="doc-pagebreak" title="518"}In the [Chrome Web Store]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm82}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm83}, you will need to provide a high-level description of what this [extension]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm84} is doing, as well as a justification for each permission category that is considered sensitive (identity, scripting, etc.). You will also need to indicate if and how you are tracking your users and in what way.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec17Heading"}
### Review Process {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec17Heading .Heading}

Your extension will undergo manual [review]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm85} the first time it is submitted to ensure it meets Chrome Web Store standards. Once it's approved, it will be available for anyone to install!
:::

::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec18Heading"}
### Beta Testing with Trusted Testers {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec18Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par71 .Para}
Releasing a beta [version]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm86} allows developers to gather feedback, identify issues, and refine their extension before a full launch. Beta releases still go through the [Chrome Web Store]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm87} review process. To release a beta, clearly indicate that the release is for testing by adding \"BETA\" to the extension name and specifying its purpose in the description. The following distribution options are supported:

::: UnorderedList
-   :::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par72 .Para}
    **By Email: Controlled, Small-Scale Testing**

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   Grant access only to specific testers by adding their emails as trusted users.

    -   Ensures early-stage feedback from a small, reliable group before wider testing.
    :::
    ::::

-   :::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par75 .Para}
    **Google Group: Scalable, Managed Access**

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   Distribute to a controlled group by linking a Google Group.

    -   []{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB519 .pagebreak aria-label="519" role="doc-pagebreak" title="519"}Makes it easier to manage and expand your tester pool while keeping access restricted.
    :::
    ::::

-   :::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par78 .Para}
    **Direct Link: Flexible, Wider Reach**

    ::: UnorderedList
    -   Set [visibility]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm88} to **Unlisted** so anyone with the link can install it.

    -   Ideal for semi-private beta tests, allowing broader feedback without public listing.
    :::
    ::::
:::
::::
:::::
:::::::::::::::

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec19Heading"}
## Updating Extensions {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec19Heading .Heading}

Once your extension is published, the process of updating it is more or less identical to the initial publishing process. You'll upload a zip file with an updated version number in the manifest. Any additional permissions will require additional justification.

:::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec20Heading"}
### Update Considerations {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec20Heading .Heading}

Once you have an existing userbase and want to push out an update, there are some things you should keep in mind.

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec21Heading"}
#### Update Delays {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec21Heading .Heading}

[Updates]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm89} are not rolled out immediately. Once you submit an update, it must be reviewed and approved before a user's browser will be able to install it. The user's browser will periodically send out update checks every few hours and download the update if there is one available. However, the browser will lazily apply that update if the extension is active, so there is sometimes a considerable delay between when an update is published and when it is installed. This delay can be between a few minutes and upwards of a week.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec22Heading"}
#### Staged Rollouts {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec22Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB520 .pagebreak aria-label="520" role="doc-pagebreak" title="520"}If your [extension]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm90}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm91} has over 10,000 active users, the **Percentage Rollout** option in the **Distribution** tab allows you to release updates gradually, limiting the update to a specified percentage of users. This staged rollout helps detect and address potential issues before the update reaches the entire userbase, minimizing disruptions and improving stability.

The **Percentage Rollout** option is only available for new versions of an already-published item and does not appear until a new draft has been uploaded. Once a draft has been submitted for review, this option becomes locked until the version is approved and published. After publishing a version with a partial rollout, developers can increase the rollout percentage without needing to resubmit for review, allowing for a controlled expansion until the update reaches 100% of users. This feature is particularly useful for monitoring performance and gathering feedback in real-world conditions before committing to a full deployment.

Note that the percentage *only applies to existing users receiving updates*. New users who install the extension after the [rollout]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm92}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm93} begins will always receive the latest version, regardless of the rollout percentage. Also, rollout assignments are not persistent. A user included in a 10% rollout for one version might not be included in the next one. This can result in users being distributed across several versions if multiple partial rollouts are performed, potentially leading to fragmentation of your userbase.
:::

::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23 .section .Section3 .RenderAsSection3 aria-labelledby="Sec23Heading"}
#### Auto-disabling {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec23Heading .Heading}

Suppose you have a [collection]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm94} of users with your extension installed. If a new version is published that requires a new permission that needs explicit user approval, your extension will be disabled until the user grants that permission. The browser will not pop up a dialog when the extension is disabled, only a small icon will appear. This means that you may suffer unintentional user [attrition]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm95} when adding addition permissions. Using optional permissions can solve this problem.

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar10 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Note
:::

Details of this are covered in detail in Chapter [[[10]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_10_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef}.
::::
:::::
::::::::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec24Heading"}
### Cancelling Updates {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec24Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB521 .pagebreak aria-label="521" role="doc-pagebreak" title="521"}You can cancel a [pending]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm96} review in the Chrome Web Store before it is approved and distributed. To do this, go to the extension listing page, click the ⋮ menu, and select **Cancel review** (available only when the status is *Pending review*). Publishers can cancel a review up to six times per day.
:::

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec25Heading"}
### Automated Rollback {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec25Heading .Heading}

To roll back a published Chrome Web Store extension, [developers]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm97}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm98} can use the rollback feature to revert to the previously published version without requiring a new review. This process involves republishing the prior version under a new version number (e.g., rolling back from 1.1 to 1.0 as version 1.2). The rollback is nearly instantaneous and applies the standard extension update cycle. If the extension was undergoing a staged rollout, the rollback cancels any partial deployments and fully reverts to the last version that was deployed to 100% of users. Any pending submissions, including those awaiting review, will be discarded. Before triggering a rollback, developers should ensure backward compatibility to avoid data loss or functional issues. The rollback can be initiated via the Developer Dashboard's **Build** ➤ **Package** page, where developers must provide a rollback reason and confirm the action. Once completed, the rollback version appears in the [dashboard]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm99}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm100}, and the extension listing updates shortly afterward. Note that while rollbacks are deployed immediately, they follow the same client-side propagation delays as standard version updates.
:::
:::::::::::

::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec26Heading"}
## Automated Publishing {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec26Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB522 .pagebreak aria-label="522" role="doc-pagebreak" title="522"}Instead of manually uploading zip files to the Chrome Web Store [website]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm101}, it is possible to automatically submit to the [Chrome Web Store]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm102} via a REST API. To do this, you will first need to acquire credentials to authenticate your API requests. Details of this process can be found here: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/webstore/using-api]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/webstore/using-api)]{.ExternalRef}.

Once you have the required credentials, you can manage a significant portion of your extension via API, documented here: [[[[https://developer.chrome.com/docs/webstore/api_index/]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/webstore/api_index/)]{.ExternalRef}.

Interacting with the API via [command]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm103} line is clunky. Instead, use an NPM package to push your updates: [[[[https://github.com/simov/chrome-webstore]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{.RefSource}](https://github.com/simov/chrome-webstore)]{.ExternalRef}.

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar11 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

The [Plasmo platform]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm104} has a terrific automatic submission tool called [Browser Platform Publisher]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm105}. Refer to Chapter [[[15]{.RefSource}](#534317_2_En_15_Chapter.xhtml)]{.ExternalRef} for details.
::::
:::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec27Heading"}
## Tracking User Activity {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec27Heading .Heading}

Once you have people installing and using your extension, you will want to know how many of them there are and what they are doing.

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec28Heading"}
### Dashboard Metrics {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec28Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par96 .Para}
The Chrome Developer [Dashboard]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm106} shows you several important metrics about your extension, including

::: UnorderedList
-   Users/day

-   Impressions/day

-   Installs/day

-   Uninstalls/day
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par101 .Para}
[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB523 .pagebreak aria-label="523" role="doc-pagebreak" title="523"}The number of weekly users you see in the Chrome Web Store is the amount of users whose Chrome browser has checked for an update of your app within the last week. It is not the [number]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm107} of people who have installed your item. This means

::: UnorderedList
-   Users who have installed your extension but have not opened Chrome recently may not be counted.

-   The count may fluctuate based on how often users open their browsers and whether their devices are online.
:::
::::
:::::::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec29Heading"}
### Analytics Libraries {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec29Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par104 .Para}
[Tracking user activity]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm108} in a browser extension is mostly the same as tracking web page activity. There are a few wrinkles to consider:

::: UnorderedList
-   **Events in a content script should be tracked in a background service worker.** Outgoing network requests to send analytics data are subject to ad blockers and cross-origin restrictions.

-   **Events in a background script will not have a page URL.**

-   **You must include your analytics library scripts in your Manifest V3 package.** Many analytics libraries such as Google Analytics give the option to dynamically load an [analytics]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm109} script -- this is no longer allowed.
:::
::::

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_FPar12 .FormalPara .FormalParaRenderingStyle1 .ParaTypeImportant}
::: Heading
Tip
:::

[Ad blockers]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm110} will be unable to block requests sent from extension pages or background scripts. Therefore, unlike web pages (where a large fraction of analytics traffic gets blocked), you can expect to have near-perfect analytics fidelity with browser extensions.
::::
:::::::

:::::::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec30Heading"}
### Install and Uninstall Events {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec30Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB524 .pagebreak aria-label="524" role="doc-pagebreak" title="524"}You are able to perform special [tasks]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm111} when the user installs and uninstalls an extension. For example, the background script can perform a task only when the user newly installs an extension:

::::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par110 .Para}
*background.js*

:::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC15 .ProgramCode}
::::::::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.onInstalled.addListener((details) =\> {
:::

::: FixedLine
    if (details.reason === chrome.runtime.OnInstalledReason.INSTALL) {
:::

::: FixedLine
      **// Anything in here will only execute on first install**
:::

::: FixedLine
      **openWelcomePage();**
:::

::: FixedLine
    }
:::

::: FixedLine
});
:::
:::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

:::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par111 .Para}
You won't be able to execute code on an uninstall, but you are able to direct the user to a URL of your choosing with [chrome.runtime.setUninstallURL]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}:

::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PC16 .ProgramCode}
:::: LineGroup
::: FixedLine
chrome.runtime.setUninstallURL(\"https://foobar.com/survey\");
:::
::::
:::::
::::::

There are no restrictions on this URL. I recommend using it to collect analytics [data]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm112}, give a survey, display contact information for troubleshooting, or offer some helpful tips that might get them to reinstall.
::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31 .section .Section1 .RenderAsSection1 aria-labelledby="Sec31Heading"}
## Enterprise Extension Management {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec31Heading .Heading}

For organizations managing extensions at scale, enterprise extension management provides administrators with control over which extensions can be installed and used within a corporate environment.

::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec32Heading"}
### Enterprise Web Store {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec32Heading .Heading}

[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_PB525 .pagebreak aria-label="525" role="doc-pagebreak" title="525"}Google offers an [Enterprise Web Store]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm113}[]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm114}, a private Chrome Web Store instance that allows organizations to curate a selection of pre-approved extensions. IT administrators can publish internal extensions for exclusive use within their company, ensuring that employees only have access to vetted and business-critical tools.
:::

::::::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33 .section .Section2 .RenderAsSection2 aria-labelledby="Sec33Heading"}
### Force-Installing or Blocking Extensions with Enterprise Policies {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Sec33Heading .Heading}

:::: {#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_Par115 .Para}
Using [Chrome Enterprise policies]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm115}, administrators can enforce strict control over extension installations by applying force-install or block policies via managed Chrome [browsers]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm116}. These policies can be set through [Google Admin Console]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm117} or [Group Policy Objects (GPO)]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm118} for Windows environments.

::: UnorderedList
-   [[ExtensionInstallSources]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm119} defines the approved sources from which extensions can be installed. This helps prevent unauthorized sideloading of extensions.

-   [[ExtensionInstallAllowlist]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm120} specifies which extensions users are permitted to install but are not mandatory.

-   [[ExtensionInstallForcelist]{.EmphasisFontCategoryNonProportional}]{#534317_2_En_13_Chapter.xhtml_ITerm121} determines which extensions are automatically installed and updated by Chrome.
